neither carries its own `ast.walk`), so a self-test can NEVER validate a DIFFERENT detector than production scans (the B1 `_build_and_insert` / #129 C2 single-source discipline; the Canary-#3 conftest-stub vacuity cousin). **D1**: `_is_silent_falsy_return_body` — body's LAST statement is a falsy `Return` (bare `return` / None / "" / [] / {} / () / 0 / False / empty `set()/dict()/list()/tuple()/frozenset()` ctor) AND no log/raise anywhere in the body (logging IS the triage). **D2**: scan widened from top-level `core/*.py` (`glob`) to recursive `core/**/*.py` (`rglob`), `core/_minifasnet` allowlist preserved — closes the nested-`core/` blind spot (`core/vision/`, `core/event_log/`, `core/*_migrations.py`) for BOTH swallow shapes. **D2b**: `_has_annotation_comment` widened to the full handler span (`except_lineno-2` … `end_lineno-1`), required by D2 (else it false-positives on `producer.py:572`'s `# CLEANUP:`, which sits between the `except:`-with-`# pragma` and the `pass`) — with an in-code short-handler caveat (safe only because every #123-detected shape is short; Completes the SessionStore lifecycle-visibility invariant that Canary #4 B1 only HALF-finished: B1 made session OPEN synchronous (`_sync_open_session`); #129 makes CLOSE synchronous (`_sync_close_session`) — open + close now symmetrically sync, every post-close check reads true state, the fire-and-forget-visibility bug class closed at BOTH lifecycle ends. **Root cause (Phase-0 wrong-premise correction: MISSED-fire, NOT double-fire)**: `pipeline._close_session` removed the session via fire-and-forget `create_task(_session_store.close_session(...))` (pop drained a tick later) + the room-end gate's `_remaining_sessions` excluded only the SINGLE current `person_id`. On simultaneous multi-expiry (`_expire_stale_sessions` closes A then B in one sync pass), each close saw the OTHER's pop still pending → `_remaining` non-empty for both → ROOM-END NEVER FIRED → `_active_room_session` orphaned (next arrival joins the dead room + `conversation_log.room_session_id` contamination); the line-2379 full-cleanup hit the same blind spot. The single-expiry double-fire was already guarded by `_sync_clear_room()` (Session 112). **Fix (Option C, symmetric close-side mirror of B1, C1-C4)**: C1 `_sync_close_session(pid)` = the 2nd blessed sync mutator (single GIL-atomic `self._sessions.pop`, no await/lock; simpler than sync-open — no build phase); C2 `close_session` delegates under the lock (no divergence); C3 `SYNC_METHOD_ALLOWLIST` += `_sync_close_session` (now exactly the 3 sync writers `{_build_and_insert, _sync_open_session, _sync_close_session}`) + MANDATORY class-docstring rewrite to the "blessed sync open/close PAIR"; C4 `pipeline._close_session:2158` swaps the fire-and-forget for the direct sync removal (the ONLY behavioral line) + Q2 stale-comment rewrite (the old `:2212-2214` "close is a create_task (async) so the session may still be in the store" became a FALSEHOOD post-C4 → rewritten to the sync-removal reality + belt-and-braces rationale for the retained `person_id` exclusion). **Golden tests RED-first** (`tests/test_followup129_room_end_multi_expiry.py`): GT0 multi-expiry fires room-end EXACTLY once + carries both participants + clears the room (Q3 all three); GT1 single-expiry regression; GT2 line-2379 full-cleanup. **§3 drain rule (auditor's sharp PI)**: only the POP is sync post-C4 — `_on_room_end` (create_task) + the full-cleanup stay async by design, so GT0/GT2's spy/state reads need `@pytest.mark.asyncio` + `await asyncio.sleep(0)`; the sync `peek_active_room_session() → None` read needs none. INV1 (no-sync-mutators AST scan, strengthened by the allowlist entry) + INV2 (`close_session` delegates, no inline pop). **§7 drain-retirement scoped close-pop-ONLY**: retired the Canary #4 inter-close drains in `test_s3b1_room_started_at_lifecycle` (sync-state reads) but KEPT the trailing synthesis drain in `test_s116_room_lifecycle_logs_participant_join_and_synthesis` (async `_on_room_end` read) — #129 RETIRES a Canary #4 test workaround, the new build removing prior scaffolding. **Auditor GREENLIGHT 2026-05-31**: C1-C4 ratified as the symmetric mirror of B1; fix adds `ENROLLMENT_RENAME_MAX_TURNS=3` turn-count guard. **Golden-test repair**: Layer-3 (the architect's INDEPENDENT full-suite run on the CUDA box + line-by-line re-read) caught that the Canary #3 golden tests GT1/GT1b were VACUOUS — they imported the conftest autouse `core.voice` stub (`embed = AsyncMock(return_value=None)`) instead of the real module, so they tested a mock hardcoded to None and could NEVER go green; Required narrowing the SessionStore "no sync mutators" invariant from conservative-sufficient to precise-necessary (with the `_sync_mint_room` precedent on the sibling PipelineStateStore, a `SYNC_METHOD_ALLOWLIST` entry + justification, and a mandated class-docstring rewrite). Without BARE `raise` (e.g., swallowing with `pass` OR `raise BrokenProcessPool(...)` re-construction without `from`), the caller-side fallback chain breaks OR the traceback is lost. process supervisor (P0.R4) catches the worst case. (2) Restart endless-loop protection — restart fires ONCE per stale-detection cycle; subsequent stale detections while degraded set are no-ops (log + skip respawn). Q7 stale-persists log keeps operator informed. (3) False-positive cost on legitimate slow iteration — 30s threshold is conservative; legitimate vision iterations complete in <50ms typical. (4) `vision_degraded` is observability-only, not self-healing — operator sees the alert + can intervene (reconnect camera, restart pipeline). If camera permanently dead, flag stays set until process restart. (3) `Retry-After` header value derives from the oldest in-window timestamp, not a smoothed average — under bursty traffic the recovery window may feel longer than the strict 60s/60req contract suggests. (4) Sliding-window logic uses a JS array.filter on every request — O(N) per request where N is the per-token timestamp count (max 60 by limit gate); negligible for the 100-entry cache cap. Migrated ONLY the line-715 call site (now line 733; inside `add_embedding`'s `with self._index_lock:` block at line 694) from `self._save_faiss()` → `self._save_faiss_unlocked()` to make the lock-held precondition explicit at the call site. Defense-in-depth: D3 anchor 3 is an AST forward-property test scanning `core/db.py` for any `with self._index_lock:` AST node whose descendants contain `Call(Attribute(self, "_save_faiss"))` — FAILS if found; prevents future refactor from re-introducing the implicit-RLock-re-entrancy pattern. **D4 (`core/state.py:1-37`)**: added `import threading` + `_persistent_lock = threading.Lock()` module-scope + wrapped `set_persistent`'s atomic-replace rebind with `with _persistent_lock:` — preemptive GIL-free Python forward-compatibility (Python 3.13+ `--disable-gil` builds break the STORE_NAME-atomicity assumption that backed P0.11's original design). Lock acquisition ~100ns; negligible overhead for single-startup-writer use case. Docstring rewritten to remove P0.11 disclaimer ("If runtime writers are added...") and replace with affirmative GIL-free forward-compatibility statement. Plus Vulnerability B: `_kuzu_degraded` was a silent in-memory flag — operators had no way to know cross-person privacy traversal was bypassed until log-grepping. 2 D-decisions ship the fix. **D1 (`core/brain_agent.py:6695-6810`)** — `_ensure_graph_sync` re-ordered per ORDERING INVARIANT comment block (10-step verbatim docstring): `_did_schema_upgrade` captured at function entry → `_mark_kuzu_dirty()` → `drop_schema()` + `_init_schema()` → rebuild path: `rebuild(knowledge_rows)` → `_clear_kuzu_dirty()` → IF `_did_schema_upgrade` AND NOT degraded: `update_graph_schema_version(NEW)`. SQL commit is the LAST mutation; gated on the in-flight intent flag so sentinel-only rebuilds (boot reconciliation, count mismatch) do NOT bump the version. Crash anywhere before the SQL commit leaves stored_version=OLD → next boot predicate retriggers idempotently. Cross-spec invariant: P0.X SQL-first framing applies to paired DATA writes; for the SCHEMA-VERSION bump itself, SQL-LAST is correct because SQL is the source-of-truth FOR which schema-state-is-canonical — committing it prematurely commits a lie. **D2 (`core/health.py` + 4 sub-contracts)** — `HealthSnapshot.kuzu_degraded: bool = False` field (lines 37-55) + gather wiring via defensive `getattr(brain_orchestrator, "_kuzu_degraded", False)` + `format_health_line` conditional `"kuzu=degraded"` emit (mirrors evlog_parts pattern) + `format_health_alerts` actionable recovery alert with VERBATIM substring lock per Plan v1 §3.3: `"Kuzu graph in degraded mode"` + `"Recovery: stop pipeline"` + `"rm -rf <resolved_path>"` + `"restart"` + `"brain.db facts will rebuild"`. Two bugs: (1) async `rebuild_faiss_async` missed the DB UPDATE batch that sync `_rebuild_faiss` already had (P0.5 heritage), so `embeddings.faiss_idx` column drifted from the in-memory `_idx_to_person` mapping after every dream-loop async prune → post-restart `_load_faiss` rebuilt with stale faiss_idx → `recognize()` routed via wrong index → returned `(None, None, score)` for affected known persons; (2) `tests/test_faiss_atomicity_invariants.py:29-32` SCOPE LIMIT comment claimed slow-tier crash-test coverage for `prune_old_strangers_async` that did NOT exist (documentation-vs-reality drift for ~weeks). 5 D-decisions shipped extending the P0.5 sync paired-write pattern (lock → SQL durable → FAISS derived → sentinel as crash beacon) to the async path. **D1 (`core/db.py:939-959`)**: `_fetch_all_embeddings_for_index()` SELECT widens from `SELECT person_id, vector` to `SELECT id, person_id, vector`; returns 3-tuple `(vecs, person_ids, row_ids)` so Phase 3 has the keys needed for the DB UPDATE write-back. **D2 (`core/db.py:961-980`)**: `_build_faiss_from_snapshot()` accepts the 3-tuple snapshot + returns 3-tuple `(new_index, new_idx_to_person, snapshot_idx_updates)` where `snapshot_idx_updates: list[tuple[int, int]]` carries (new_idx, row_id) pairs parallel to sync `_rebuild_faiss`'s `idx_updates`. **D3 (`core/db.py:693-720` + `core/db.py:1031-1091`)**: explicit-cursor pattern in `add_embedding` captures `cur.lastrowid` immediately after INSERT inside the transaction (Plan v2 §4.1 lock — avoids implicit-cursor ambiguity); pending-add list extended from 2-tuple to 3-tuple `(vec, person_id, row_id)`; Phase 3 builds combined `all_idx_updates = snapshot_idx_updates + pending_idx_updates` from both snapshot rows AND pending-replay positions; DB UPDATE batch runs AFTER `_index_lock` release (Q3 LOCK; matches sync precedent at lines 1067-1080 — holding the index lock across the DB batch would extend hold-time by seconds on a large gallery, starving concurrent recognize() callers). **D4 (`core/db.py:1031-1089`)**: sentinel discipline on async path with ORDERING INVARIANT comment block — `_mark_faiss_dirty()` SET BEFORE Phase 3 in-memory swap; `_clear_faiss_dirty()` ONLY AFTER BOTH DB UPDATE commit AND `_save_faiss()` success; any earlier exception (Phase 3 swap, DB UPDATE crash, disk save failure) leaves sentinel set → boot reconciliation in `_load_faiss` triggers sync `_rebuild_faiss` to reconcile. **D5 (3 behavioral tests + Bug 2 honest comment correction)**: `tests/test_faiss_sql_atomicity.py::test_prune_async_then_restart_recognizes_known_person` (slow-tier — Bug 1 happy-path closure: insert 5 strangers stale + 5 known → `prune_old_strangers_async` → process restart → all 5 known recognized correctly) + `tests/test_faiss_sql_atomicity.py::test_prune_async_crash_mid_db_update_recovers_via_sentinel` (slow-tier — D4 sentinel crash-recovery: counting `_ConnProxy` raises `OperationalError` on 3rd UPDATE → exception propagates → sentinel SET on disk → restart → boot reconciliation runs `_rebuild_faiss` → known persons all recognized) + `tests/test_faiss_async_rebuild.py::test_add_embedding_during_async_rebuild_tracks_row_id` (fast-tier — D3 concurrency race: `threading.Event` coordinates pause-add-resume so `add_embedding` racing with `rebuild_faiss_async` Phase 2 lands a 3-tuple in `_pending_adds_during_rebuild` and Phase 3 writes its post-swap faiss_idx back to the DB); (2) `_save_faiss()` failure leaves sentinel set + `rebuild_faiss_async` returns WITHOUT re-raising (lines 1083-1085) by design — the `except` swallows + the rebuild_lock releases cleanly via `finally`. Distinct from earlier crash points which DO re-raise. Boot reconciliation handles divergence on next process start. (3) Pending-add row_id semantics: when `add_embedding` races `rebuild_faiss_async` Phase 1-2 window, the row's DB `faiss_idx` reflects the PRE-swap position briefly; Phase 3's combined DB UPDATE batch corrects it. recognize() during the narrow race window may return stale routing for that ONE pending row if it lands between Phase 2 build and Phase 3 swap — captured + measured by Test 3. (4) D1 SELECT order is `ORDER BY id` (numeric DB row id); sync `_rebuild_faiss` SELECT matches the same order. Both paths produce identical faiss_idx assignment sequences. (4) `core/voice.py:302` HF_TOKEN read at lazy-load unchanged (S3.X migration scope). **Cumulative suite**: 2500 → 2509 (+9 — exact Plan v1 §2 forecast match). | **P0.S2 CLOSED 2026-05-20** — Dashboard authentication + bind invariant (7 D-decisions, cross-language Python + Node.js + JS-wrapper). The dashboard's `/api/factory-reset` (and every other API) was reachable by anonymous LAN attackers given any LAN-binding misconfiguration; the P0.0.1-era S2 tripwire kept the deferral honest, but the substantive fix is shipped here. **D1 — token generation + corruption recovery + Windows ACL** (`core/dashboard_token.py` NEW ~250 LOC): `_VALID_TOKEN_RE = ^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{43}$` shape lock; `_atomic_write_secret` writes `.tmp.<pid>` + fsync + chmod-on-tmp + os.replace (canonical file never world-readable); `_apply_windows_acl` invokes icacls with the locked argv shape `['icacls', path, '/inheritance:r', '/grant:r', f'{user}:F']` + `check=False` + `capture_output=True` + `timeout=5.0` (subprocess function-scope re-import per Plan v2 Q5-C string-form patch resilience); `_ensure_dashboard_token` is the pipeline-boot entry point — cleanup partial writes → existence check → shape-validate → on-corrupt backup `.dashboard_token.corrupt.<unix_ts>` + regenerate + WARNING log + new auth URL → on-valid self-heal mode drift via `_verify_mode_self_heal`. Plan v2 §3.13 corruption-recovery design: `.corrupt.<ts>` suffix preserves forensic evidence + excludes file from future regeneration check + self-heal-by-default since pipeline boot doesn't block on dashboard state (cookies tied to the previous token need re-auth — accepted UX cost over refusing-to-boot). Boot integration: first line of `pipeline.py::run()` before any other boot work. `core/db.py::wipe_all()` carries a verbatim 8-line preservation comment block naming the invariant (re-issuing auth URL on every factory reset is hostile UX + single-user scope = no cross-tenant risk). **D2 — `/api/auth` route** (`dog-ai-dashboard/app/api/auth/route.ts` NEW): GET handler with `?token=<43-char>` validation via `crypto.timingSafeEqual` (length-equality pre-check; bare `===` would short-circuit on first differing byte → timing-attack surface); cookie attributes locked at `{name: 'dogai_session', httpOnly: true, sameSite: 'strict', path: '/', maxAge: 315360000}` (10-year TTL — single-user localhost rotation is via token replacement, not TTL); on success 302 redirect to `/` + `fs.unlinkSync(AUTH_URL_PATH)` wrapped in try/catch for Plan v2 §3.12 double-click idempotency (second click of consumed URL: validation still succeeds against `.dashboard_token`, unlink no-ops on ENOENT, harmless re-issued Set-Cookie); validation reads ONLY from `.dashboard_token` source-of-truth — `.dashboard_auth_url` deletion is hygiene, NOT validation logic (P4 invariant). Distinguishable error bodies: 400 missing param, 401 mismatch ("Unauthorized"), 401 ENOENT ("Pipeline not started — token not yet generated") so operator can diagnose. **D3 — middleware coverage** (`dog-ai-dashboard/middleware.ts` NEW): matcher `'/api/:path*'` covers every `/api/*` by default; inline allowlist for `/api/auth` is the ONLY carve-out (would create chicken/egg gating the route that mints cookies); per-request `fs.readFileSync(_tokenPath(), 'utf8')` — NO module-scope token cache so manual token replacement / corruption recovery is reflected without server restart; cookie via `req.cookies.get('dogai_session')`; length-prechecked `crypto.timingSafeEqual` mirrors the auth-route helper. The motivating-failure regression guard `test_factory_reset_is_gated_by_middleware` confirms the entire P0.S2 motivation — anonymous attacker can no longer wipe via `/api/factory-reset`. **D4 — file-read invariant**: structural check on both middleware.ts (no module-scope cache) AND `_ensure_dashboard_token` (re-reads file every call); behavioral check that missing token → 401 with distinguishable body. **D5 — bind invariant + launch wrapper** (`dog-ai-dashboard/scripts/launch.js` NEW ~140 LOC): `DEFAULT_BIND='127.0.0.1'` + `_resolveBind()` reads `DASHBOARD_BIND` env var; argv-injection regex `VALID_BIND_RE = /^[a-zA-Z0-9.:_-]+$/` fires BEFORE the 0.0.0.0 double-opt-in check (defense against `DASHBOARD_BIND="0.0.0.0 --foo bar"`-style injection that would otherwise pass simple equality); 0.0.0.0 (bind-all-interfaces) requires `DASHBOARD_BIND_ALLOW_ANY=1` second-opt-in env var with hard-error stderr naming the variable + threat ("ALL interfaces, exposing the dashboard to every network the host is reachable on"); 6-line WARNING block emits on every non-localhost bind; `--dry-run` flag prints resolved argv to stdout + exits 0 (testable surface); `child_process.spawn` with `stdio: 'inherit'` + `shell: process.platform === 'win32'` for the .cmd shim. `package.json` scripts now invoke `node scripts/launch.js dev|start` instead of inlining `--hostname` flags. **D6 — factory-reset preservation**: P0.S2 preservation comment block at BOTH `core/db.py::wipe_all` AND `dog-ai-dashboard/app/api/factory-reset/route.ts` (inline `// NOTE:` reinforcement at end of array body — defense-in-depth against future array-additions). Naming the invariant in both Python and Node mirrors the discipline that any `.dashboard_token` or `.dashboard_auth_url` deletion in either path is a regression. **D7 — mode + ACL enforcement**: POSIX path uses os.chmod(0o600); Windows path adds icacls via `_apply_windows_acl`; both run on every boot via `_verify_mode_self_heal` (idempotent re-icacls on Windows; POSIX detects drift + WARNs + restores). icacls failures (FileNotFoundError / TimeoutExpired / returncode≠0) all WARN-and-continue per Plan v1 §1.P3 "best-effort + warn user" — pipeline never crashes on Windows ACL quirks. **S2 tripwire REFACTORED per Plan v1 §2 test 21**: old `test_dashboard_package_json_scripts_explicitly_bind_localhost` (asserting `--hostname 127.0.0.1` literal in package.json) replaced by `test_dashboard_package_json_scripts_invoke_launch_wrapper` (scripts MUST start with `node ` + contain `scripts/launch.js`) + NEW `test_dashboard_launch_script_enforces_default_localhost` (wrapper source has DEFAULT_BIND in locked localhost set + DASHBOARD_BIND env-var branch + DASHBOARD_BIND_ALLOW_ANY double-opt-in branch + 0.0.0.0 mention). Same invariant ("default bind localhost; LAN opt-out requires explicit env-var"); new surface (logic moved from package.json to launch.js wrapper). **36 collected tests** (`tests/test_dashboard_token.py` 12 + `tests/test_dashboard_token_windows.py` 2 + `tests/test_dashboard_middleware.py` 7 + `tests/test_dashboard_auth_route.py` 8 + `tests/test_dashboard_launch_script.py` 6 + `tests/test_dashboard_bind_tripwire.py` +1 new): 2 POSIX-only D7 tests skip on Windows dev machine (34 passed; 6 total skipped including 2 P0.S7.5.2 carryover). **Defense-in-depth classification per Plan v2 §11 operational definition LOCKED at closure**: 10 DiD tests (atomic-replace, corruption-recovery + complement, `_verify_token_shape` unit pair, icacls failure recovery, middleware ENOENT→401, auth missing-param→400, constant-time-compare structural, launch double-opt-in success, launch argv-injection rejection) + 26 behavioral-correctness tests. **Plan v2 §11 verbatim operational definition** banked in `feedback_auditor_q5_estimates_trail_grep.md`: "Defense-in-depth test = verifies behavior under input that has NOT been observed in production AND would NOT be caught by the motivating-failure regression guard. Tests verifying real-world UX scenarios are behavioral-correctness, not defense-in-depth, regardless of how 'edge-case' they feel." Plan v2 §3.12 multi-tab double-click reclassified at closure from DiD → behavioral-correctness (real-world UX); §3.13 corruption recovery stays DiD. **Q5-B trigger math result**: non-DiD actual 26 vs auditor upper bound 22 = **HIGH-BY-18% — UNDER the 30% Q5-B trigger threshold**. The trigger stays armed for the NEXT non-DiD cycle ≥30% over upper bound; rename of `Auditor-Q5-estimates-trail-grep` → `Auditor-Q5-systematic-under-estimate` does NOT activate on P0.S2 closure. The "slight overage" framing holds; trajectory across 4 consecutive ON-TARGET-with-slight-overage instances: 0% → 20% → 7% → 18% (P0.S2 closure landed BETTER than the 27% Plan v2 projection because actual classification was DiD-heavier than projected). **Strict-industry-standard mode 9th consecutive application + 3rd successful closure** across 2 distinct multi-cycle specs (P0.S7.5.1 + P0.S2; P0.S7.5.2 closed within the same window adding 4 more applications). **2nd cross-spec generalization** — discipline transferred from P0.S7.5.2 (Python-only, 5 D-decisions) to P0.S2 (cross-language Python + Node.js + JS-wrapper, 7 D-decisions) without architect prompting. Watch for elevation if 5+ instances recur. **Known Limitations (P0.S2)** — banked verbatim per Plan v1 §1.P5 + Plan v2 §13 in to_be_checked.md §"P0.S2": (1) GET /api/auth uses URL-parameter token — single-user localhost acceptable; S2.X follow-up to POST-with-form-body if TLS/proxy migration creates logging surface; (2) `.dashboard_token` leaked if user shares `faces/` directory — fundamental file-based-provisioning limit; S2.X candidate for OS-keyring storage; (3) Token rotation is manual (delete + regenerate); (4) icacls failure on Windows is best-effort with chmod 0600 fallback. **Cumulative suite**: 2466 → 2500 passed (+34 passed; +36 collected with 2 POSIX-only D7 skipped on Windows). | **P0.S7.5.2 CLOSED 2026-05-20** — Canary-3 multi-subsystem fixes (5 D-decisions). The 2026-05-20 multi-person live canary surfaced 5 independent root causes across the reconciler / pipeline / voice gallery / audio / brain prompt-blocks: (1) Jagan's offscreen-floor false-fire on legitimate-but-short utterances; (2) Lexi's voice-routing engagement-gate never firing because the new_stranger path didn't mirror ambient-gate semantics; (3) voice gallery centroid contamination from short-utterance noise; (4) Whisper 1-word artifacts ("You", "Yeah", "Thank") triggering phantom turns; (5) brain mis-tooling canary-3 question-shape ("Do you know me?") to `report_identity_mismatch` on stranger turn 1. **D1 (P0 CORRECTNESS)** — `_rs_pif_view` producer at `pipeline.py:7234` now mirrors the full `PresenceSnapshot` schema including `last_recognized_at`; reconciler's offscreen-floor predicate stops false-firing because the dict no longer falls back to `.get(..., 0)`. Producer fix per auditor lean (D1.b) — NOT consumer change — preserves the semantic distinction between "face recognized within X" vs "any-source presence touched within X" (voice-source entries still excluded by `last_recognized_at=0.0` AND `source != "voice"` defense-in-depth). **D2 (P0 CORRECTNESS)** — voice-routing new_stranger branch at `pipeline.py:7496-7515` mirrors the ambient-gate engagement semantics via NFKC-lowercased substring match (`_nfkc_lower(_system_name) in _nfkc_lower(text)`); when stranger says system name in turn 1's STT, the session opens with `engagement_gate_passed=True` + `set_waiting_for_name(False)` + `set_voice_only_origin(True)` matching the ambient path. Without this, voice-routing-opened sessions stay stuck at `bootstrap_credits=0` forever. **D3 (MEDIUM)** — voice gallery accumulation gates added: minimum-utterance-duration (`MIN_VOICE_ACCUM_DURATION_SECS=1.5`s — matches ECAPA-TDNN min reliable length per `core/voice.py:147`) gate at `pipeline.py::_accumulate_voice` call site + centroid-distance (`VOICE_SELF_UPDATE_CENTROID_MIN=0.55` — mirrors face-gallery's Session 51 design) gate inside `core/db.py::add_voice_embedding`. Bootstrap-safe (`VOICE_CENTROID_GATE_MIN_SAMPLES=5`): centroid gate inactive until gallery has ≥5 samples. Plan v2 §3.1 LOCKED recompute (NOT cache) — cache-invalidation would require enumerating 6+ gallery-write sites + AST-asserting per P0.5 inverse-check discipline; not worth ~5ms saved on a rare event. Verbose-by-design skip logs for canary 4 diagnostic value. **D4 (MEDIUM)** — 1-word artifact filter in `core/audio.py::transcribe`: bare 1-word transcripts accepted only when EITHER (a) terminated with `.!?` OR (b) lowercased word in `STT_KNOWN_IMPERATIVES` allowlist `{yes, no, stop, help, okay, ok, sure, yeah, yep, nope}`. Plus Smart-Turn debounce broadened from `silent_streak == smart_turn_count` to `silent_streak >= smart_turn_count` so race-conditioned boundary chunks don't drop Smart-Turn checks (`not smart_turn_fired` flag is the debounce — fires at most once per silence streak). **D5 (MEDIUM)** — `STRANGER_IDENTITY_BLOCK_MIN_TURNS` dropped from 2 to 0 so canary-3 question-shapes hit guidance immediately on turn 0; block restructured per Plan v2 §5.2 verbatim as NUMBERED CONTRAST: `RULE 1 — STATEMENTS about their identity (CALL update_person_name)` + `RULE 2 — QUESTIONS about your knowledge of them (DO NOT call report_identity_mismatch)`. Both rules carry concrete examples (6 self-intro triggers for Rule 1, 5 question shapes for Rule 2 including Lexi's exact canary-3 phrasing "I know you very well but I'm not sure if you know me"). Forward reference at the end of Rule 2 (`THAT triggers RULE 1`) makes the causal chain explicit. **39 collected tests** (`tests/test_p0_s7_5_2.py`): 16 logical anchors with parametrize fan-out on `test_d4_stt_filter_logic` (23 cases covering all 10 imperatives + case variants + punctuation suffixes + 4 Whisper artifacts + multi-word baseline). Plan v2 logical-count forecast 14-15; actual 16 — 1 above upper bound for parametrize-light interpretation (auditor-Q5 6th instance — slight HIGH-BY-7%, the 23 parametrize cases were Plan v2 §4.1's locked table verbatim). **5/5 deliberate-regression confirmations passed** (a/b/c/d/e ↔ D1/D2/D3/D4/D5): (a) dropped `last_recognized_at` from `_rs_pif_view` producer → AST test 1 fired correctly + reverted; (b) replaced D2 engagement-gate detection with hardcoded `False` → AST tests 4+6 fired + reverted; (c) dropped D3 centroid gate from `add_voice_embedding` → behavioral test 10 fired + reverted; (d) reverted Smart-Turn guard `>=` → `==` → source-inspection test 16 fired + reverted; (e) dropped Rule 2 anti-pattern block from STRANGER IDENTITY → contrastive-shape test 8 fired + reverted. All P0.S7.5.2 tests green after all reverts; full suite green at 2466 passed, 4 skipped, 9 xfailed, 0 failed. **3 in-flight ripple-effect test updates banked**: (i) `test_stranger_identity_block_absent_below_turn_threshold` REVERSED INTENT — pre-D5 it asserted block does NOT fire at turn 1 (threshold=2); D5 drops threshold to 0 so block now MUST fire at turn 0. Test renamed `test_stranger_identity_block_fires_on_first_turn` with updated assertion + canary-3 rationale in docstring. (ii) `test_accumulate_voice_replenishes_bootstrap_for_engaged_stranger` used 1.0s audio (16000 samples); D3 duration gate rejects sub-1.5s — test updated to 24000 samples (1.5s) so the bootstrap-credits assertion can still fire. (iii) `test_transcribe_prints_stt_with_timestamp_and_latency` used bare "hello" (1 word, no punctuation, not in allowlist) → D4 filter rejected → test updated to multi-word "hello there" preserving the observability assertion. **Strict-industry-standard mode 5th consecutive application**: Phase 0 audit + Plan v1 + Plan v2 + Phase 1-5 closure all under strict mode. Pre-mortem per D-decision + multi-direction trace per D-decision + 11-gate quality checklist per D-decision + cross-spec impact analysis + closure-audit scheduled — all 5 operational-test gates passed at every artifact. Discipline routine confirmed. **Discipline counts**: spec-first review cycle 16-for-16 → **17-for-17**. The 5 instances (P0.S7.2 cross-session retrieval / P0.S7.3 KAIROS timing / P0.S7.5 bundled-queue / P0.S7.5.1 marker/metadata asymmetry / P0.S7.5.2 multi-subsystem cluster) all share the shape "live canary surfaces composed-real-world bug that spec-time grep + Phase 0 audit could not reach." Discipline-stability evidence: the architect held the strict-read line across non-canary cycles that could have inflated the count (P0.S7.D-B was deferral-rationale-expires; P0.S7.D-D was wrong-premise; P0.S7.D-E was partial-falsification — all explicitly NOT bumped despite being adjacent). **Auditor-Q5-estimates-trail-grep 6th instance** banked at architect-memory: auditor estimate 12-16 tests; Plan v1 + Plan v2 locked at 14-15; actual 16 logical anchors (with 23-case parametrize fan-out on test_d4_stt_filter_logic per Plan v2 §4.1 locked table). Plan v2 ON-TARGET → slight HIGH-BY-7%; this is the **2nd consecutive on-target multi-D-decision spec** (P0.S7.5: 5 D-decisions ON-TARGET; P0.S7.5.2: 5 D-decisions ON-TARGET). Phase 0 granularity sub-observation CONFIRMED across 3 ON-TARGET instances now (P0.S7.5 + P0.S7.5.1 + P0.S7.5.2), all with decomposed Phase 0 + named edit sites per D-decision. **Strict-industry-standard mode track record bump**: 5th consecutive application + 2nd successful closure (P0.S7.5.1 + P0.S7.5.2). **Deferred-canary strategy 1st application**: post-P0.S7.5.2, no live canary fires; entry added to `c:\Users\jagan\dog-ai\to_be_checked.md` per the strategy locked 2026-05-20. Canary-week trigger remains end-of-P0.R11. **Known Limitations (P0.S7.5.2)**: (i) D3 existing voice galleries contaminated pre-D3 stay contaminated until natural prune; canary week starts with clean state via factory reset. (ii) D2 NFKC-lowercased substring match (NOT word-boundary); false-positives on visitor names containing system-name substring possible but bounded by typical short proper-noun names. (iii) D4 `STT_KNOWN_IMPERATIVES` covers top-10 confirmation/denial words; uncommon 1-word responses ("indeed", "absolutely") filtered as acceptable trade-off. Expansion criteria per Plan v2 §4.2 documented in core/config.py docstring (3+ canary instances + no semantic ambiguity + CLAUDE.md narrative documentation). **Cumulative suite**: 2427 → 2466 (+39 collected — Phase 1 +8, Phase 2 +31 with parametrize fan-out; logical-anchor delta is +16). **Bundled-queue P0.S7 family arc status**: D-C Stage 2 + D-D Stage 2 hard-deletes + 130 test-site migrations REMAIN deferred under the new strategy (no immediate canary trigger). Combined Stage 2 PR cleared to fire when end-of-P0.R11 canary week validates the bundled set. | **P0.S7.5.1 CLOSED 2026-05-20** — Visitor alert marker/metadata asymmetry fix (D1). Canary 2 (2026-05-19) RE-CANARY surfaced a downstream failure of P0.S7.5 D1's persistent injection: VISITOR_ALERT now re-injects every turn (correct per P0.S7.5 D1), but the marker `[visitor_name:unknown]` (stranger-branch shape from `_run_visitor_alert`) NEVER got swapped to `[visitor_name:Lexi]` at promotion time because `update_visitor_alert_for_promoted_person` (Session 114 Part 5) used a literal-substring check `if f"[visitor_name:{old_name}]" in content` — but the metadata `old_name='visitor'` and the content marker `'[visitor_name:unknown]'` were ASYMMETRIC by `_run_visitor_alert`'s own design (stranger branch writes `unknown` as the placeholder, metadata stores `visitor` as the name). The literal-substring check silently no-op'd on every stranger promotion. Brain kept seeing the unknown-branch VISITOR CONTEXT template ("Someone stopped by but didn't tell me their name…") and followed it faithfully into "No one was here" fabrication on Jagan's return-asks. **D1 fix at `core/brain_agent.py:3038-3063`**: replaced literal-substring check with `re.sub(r"\[visitor_name:[^\]]+\]", lambda _m: new_marker, content)`. Wildcard pattern matches ANY current placeholder (`unknown` / real name / future drift); **lambda replacement** (callable, not f-string) means `new_marker` is used VERBATIM — no regex backreference interpretation of `new_name` (`\1`, `\g<name>`, `\\`). Defense-in-depth Gate 2 upgraded from N/A → ✓ per Plan v2 §6 (closes the regex-backreference-injection-via-name vector — relevant if future LLM-hallucinated names contain escape sequences). **6 tests added across 2 phases** in new `tests/test_p0_s7_5_1.py`: Phase 1 (5) — `test_regex_swaps_existing_marker` (unit primary happy path), `test_regex_noop_when_marker_absent` (unit defensive), `test_regex_idempotent_when_already_renamed` (unit), `test_update_alert_uses_regex_not_literal_substring` (AST forward-property — locates `update_visitor_alert_for_promoted_person` via AST walk + asserts `re.sub` call exists AND no `.replace(` with `[visitor_name:` f-string arg; structural over source-inspection because docstrings can contain the literal-substring form even when actual call is gone — 4th instance of `feedback_adjacent_string_literal_normalizer.md` pattern), `test_regex_replacement_handles_backslash_in_new_name` (Plan v2 §2.5 defense-in-depth — `new_name="Test\\name"` round-trips byte-identically; if implementation reverted to f-string, `\n` would be interpreted). Phase 2 (1) — `test_visitor_alert_marker_swap_e2e` (full lifecycle: seed stranger + best_friend + turn → `_run_visitor_alert` queues with `[visitor_name:unknown]` → rename stranger to Lexi in faces.db → `on_identity_confirmed` fires → assert marker swapped to `[visitor_name:Lexi]` + metadata reconciled). **1/1 deliberate-regression confirmation passed** (a): reverted lambda → f-string replacement; test 4b (`test_regex_replacement_handles_backslash_in_new_name`) failed correctly — `re.sub` interpreted `\n` in `Test\name` as a literal newline character chr(10), so content rendered as `[visitor_name:Test<NL>ame]` (newline) NOT `[visitor_name:Test\name]` (literal backslash). Confirms lambda-replacement is load-bearing for the verbatim-replacement contract. Reverted to lambda; all 6 P0.S7.5.1 tests green; full suite green at 2427. **Plan v2 forecast EXACT match**: +6 collected (5 Phase 1 + 1 Phase 2). **Strict-industry-standard mode 2nd consecutive application**: Plan v2 §2.2 honestly clarified that auditor's MEDIUM 1 framing (re.escape on `old_name` in the pattern) didn't apply to Plan v1 §8.2's fixed wildcard pattern — but adopted the defense-in-depth spirit at the correct surface (replacement-string special chars via lambda). Discipline test: did NOT silently fold to the auditor's literal framing OR silently ignore it; named the misalignment + fixed the real risk. **Discipline counts**: spec-first review cycle 15-for-15 → **16-for-16**. Pattern across 5 instances: D-B HIGH ~40% (21→15), D-D LOW ~4× (40→130), D-E LOW ~60% (3-5→8), P0.S7.5 ON-TARGET (12-19→16-17), P0.S7.5.1 ON-TARGET → slight HIGH-BY-20% (4-5 → 5→6 with Plan v2 +1). **Phase 0 granularity sub-observation CONFIRMED**: mini-spec with 1 D-decision (D1 only) + 1 named edit site (`core/brain_agent.py:3043-3047`) landed within mid-range estimate even after Plan v2's defense-in-depth refinement. Sub-observation has predictive power across 2 ON-TARGET instances now. **Partial-falsification-tentative stays at 2** (architect-memory only). **Two-stage-canary-gated-cleanup stays at 2** (P0.S7.5.1 is single-stage targeted fix). **Strict-industry-standard mode 2nd application banked** (auto-memory, not yet CLAUDE.md doctrine — too few instances to elevate). **Known Limitations (P0.S7.5.1)**: Canary-2-era broken nudges stay broken until natural expiry. P0.S7.5.1 D1 fixes the marker-update path going forward; it does NOT migrate existing broken nudges in production databases. Specifically: nudges queued BEFORE D1 deployment with `[visitor_name:unknown]` content where the visitor was later promoted to a real name (Lexi etc.) will retain the broken `[visitor_name:unknown]` marker until their natural `expires_at` (24h after queue) OR manual dismissal. Brain will continue surfacing the unknown-branch VISITOR CONTEXT template ("Someone stopped by but didn't tell me their name...") for these specific nudges. New nudges queued AFTER D1 deployment work correctly: regex swap fires at promotion time, marker reconciles with metadata, brain surfaces the visitor by name. Rationale for no migration: factory-reset between canaries is the project's canary discipline; canary 3 starts with clean state. In production, broken nudges expire naturally within 24h. Migration script would add complexity for a transient liability with a known TTL. When to revisit: if production runs without factory-reset between canaries become normal practice, OR if the 24h expiry proves too long for stale-nudge user experience, file a follow-up spec to add a one-shot migration. Until then, accepted-with-rationale. **Post-closure RE-CANARY 3 trigger**: fires IMMEDIATELY per Plan v2 §10 (no observation window). Same Lexi-visitor scenario as canary 2. **Expected canary 3 markers** (vs canary 2 markers banked as production-canary diff): (1) Brain calls `search_memory(person_name='Lexi', ...)` — entity is the VISITOR'S real name, NOT the asker's name (canary 2 broke this); (2) VISITOR CONTEXT block renders name-known branch ("The visitor's name is 'Lexi'…") — NOT unknown-branch; (3) Brain answers with Lexi-specific recall content — NOT "No one was here" fabrication. **On RE-CANARY 3 PASS** → fires combined Stage 2 PR (D-C Stage 2 + D-D Stage 2 hard-deletes + 130 test-site migrations). P0.S7 family arc closes definitively. **On RE-CANARY 3 FAIL with new root cause** → diagnose; file follow-up spec. **Cumulative suite**: 2421 → 2427 (+6 collected — exact Plan v2 forecast match). | **P0.S7.5 CLOSED 2026-05-19** — Bundled-queue canary follow-up. 5 canary-surfaced failure modes from the 2026-05-19 multi-person canary closed in a single bundle. **D1 (VISITOR_ALERT nudge persistence)**: new `ONE_SHOT_NUDGE_TYPES: frozenset[str]` in `core/config.py` enumerates the 3 proactive nudge types (`CROSS_PERSON_HYPOTHESIS`, `INTENTION_FOLLOWUP`, `MEMORY_PROMPT`) that legitimately get one-shot `mark_nudge_injected`. VISITOR_ALERT is INTENTIONALLY EXCLUDED — owner needs persistent context whenever they ask, not just the first re-engagement turn. `BrainOrchestrator.get_prompt_addendum` gates `mark_nudge_injected` on type membership; persistent types stay pending until expires_at or dismissed. Plan v2 LOW 2 — per-turn re-injection observability log (`[PromptPrefAgent] persistent nudge re-injected (type=X, id=Y)`) for re-canary cost validation. **D2 (SHARED CONTEXT gate widening)**: new `FaceDB.get_recent_audience_rooms(requester_pid, best_friend_id, hours_back, limit)` helper queries `conversation_log` for distinct `room_session_id`s within the past 24h where the requester appears in `audience_ids` OR is best_friend (owner override per 3A.4.6). `RoomOrchestrator.build_shared_context_block` gains a fallback branch: when scene is single-person OR has no `room_session_id`, query `get_recent_audience_rooms` + merge histories chronologically. Owner-returns-alone scenario now surfaces persisted history. New config `SHARED_CONTEXT_RECENT_AUDIENCE_HOURS = 24.0` (matches visitor-alert expiry — temporally aligned defenses). **D3 (canonical ack race fix)**: `_handle_update_person_name` now uses `await _session_store.rename(...)` (synchronous) instead of `_loop.create_task(_session_store.rename(...))` (race-prone). All 3 in-handler rename sites converted uniformly. Downstream `peek_snapshot` at the canonical-ack template observes the post-rename name. **D4 (`update_person_name` re-confirm prevention)**: tool description extended with "CRITICAL — DO NOT RE-CONFIRM (Bug Q parallel, P0.S7.5 2026-05-19)" anchor explicitly naming the feedback-loop failure mode + canary detail (5 repeated calls). New `format_known_speaker_identity_block(person_name, person_type)` helper renders `<<<KNOWN SPEAKER IDENTITY>>>` block (mirror of `format_system_identity_block`); invoked from `render_session_stable_prefix` AFTER `<<<SYSTEM IDENTITY>>>` gated on `session_person_type in ("known", "best_friend")` AND `KNOWN_SPEAKER_IDENTITY_BLOCK_ENABLED` config flag. Mutual exclusion with STRANGER IDENTITY block — strangers receive promotion prompting; known/best_friend receive don't-re-confirm prompting. **D5 (HONESTY POLICY FABRICATED ABSENCE bullet)**: explicit anti-pattern bullet added to existing HONESTY POLICY block naming the canary's exact failure phrasings ("no one was here", "I was just waiting for you"), the core honesty principle ("Absence of memory is NOT evidence of absence"), and the two-axis fabrication explicit ("(a) asserting nobody visited; (b) describing the brain's own activity"). Inherits the existing `HONESTY_POLICY_BLOCK_ENABLED` flag — no new flag added. **5/5 deliberate-regression confirmations passed** (a/b/c/d/e ↔ D1/D2/D3/D4/D5): (a) reverted D1 gate → test 2 + test 14 fired correctly; (b) reverted D2 fallback → test 15 fired; (c) reverted D3 await → create_task → test 6 fired; (d) dropped D4 block invocation → test 10 + test 11 fired (D4 confirmation also caught test 11's mutual-exclusion property); (e) dropped D5 bullet → test 12 fired. All reverted; suite green. **3 in-flight Phase 1+2 calibration items banked**: (i) Plan v2 §3.3 references `PromptPrefAgent.get_prompt_addendum` but the actual surface lives on `BrainOrchestrator.get_prompt_addendum` (line ~8342). Same spec-detail-vs-code mismatch shape as P0.S7.4's `_build_system_prompt` vs `render_session_stable_prefix`. Test target corrected at implementation. (ii) Plan v2 references "the rename" in D3 (singular) but `_handle_update_person_name` has 3 in-handler rename sites (lines 3515/3657/3768) — one per branch (stranger promotion / dispute-flip / mature rename). All 3 converted uniformly to `await` per Plan v2 D3 intent; the singular reference was spec shorthand. (iii) Plan v2 D5 source-inspection test asserted `"Absence of memory is NOT evidence of absence"` literal; Python adjacent-string-literal split at parse time meant the substring wasn't contiguous in `inspect.getsource()` output. Same shape as P0.S7.4 γ test — applied the `re.sub(r'"\s+"', "", raw_src)` normalizer. Banked as 4th instance of this pattern (P0.S7.2 Phase 1, P0.S7.4, P0.S7.D-D test 10 tightening, P0.S7.5 D5) — strong evidence the source-inspection workhorse needs an adjacent-literal normalizer by default. **3 in-flight Phase 1 ripple-effect test updates banked**: 2 P0.S7.1 observability tests had hardcoded expected log substrings (`gate=single_person`, `gate=no_room_session_id`) that no longer match after D2 widening routes those scenarios through the new `gate=recent_audience_empty` path. Updated to match the new compound gate logs; banked as expected ripple of intentional log-shape change. **1 bonus regression guard test added** (test 14b — one-shot CROSS_PERSON_HYPOTHESIS still consumed on first call). Pattern across 4 instances: D-B HIGH ~40% (21→15), D-D LOW ~4× (40→130), D-E LOW ~60% (3-5→8), P0.S7.5 ON-TARGET (12-19→16-17). **Phase 0 granularity sub-observation banked**: estimate accuracy correlates with audit-phase D-decision granularity. Architect resisted the temptation to promote the pattern to CLAUDE.md doctrine at 2 instances — per auditor's prior verdict, keep tentative until 3+ instances. Framing crispness improving but not yet locked. **OBS A — D3 rename-failure stale-name ack** documented as known limitation: D3 closes the race condition (frequent); the rare rename-failure case (DB lock, IO error) still has the bug shape — swallow-and-log path means downstream `peek_snapshot` reads pre-rename name. Acceptable trade-off; don't propagate failure into a turn crash. If becomes more common, file follow-up. **OBS B — D4 same-name (person == system) edge case** deferred to actual occurrence: when `system_name == person_name` (user named AI after a household member), both KNOWN SPEAKER IDENTITY and SYSTEM IDENTITY blocks reference the same name. Two-block-header separation (`<<<SYSTEM IDENTITY>>>` vs `<<<KNOWN SPEAKER IDENTITY>>>`) provides sufficient semantic discrimination for now. **Post-closure milestone: bundled-queue RE-CANARY trigger banked in Pending Work** — runs IMMEDIATELY per auditor Q6. Same Lexi-visitor scenario; expected behavior chain: VISITOR_ALERT injects persistently → `[visitor_id:lexi_xxx]` marker stays in addendum every turn → VISITOR CONTEXT block renders with explicit `search_memory(person_name='Lexi')` directive → brain calls `search_memory` and answers correctly with Lexi-specific content → SHARED CONTEXT block (D2 widened) ALSO surfaces persisted room turns → canonical ack speaks correct name on rename → no `update_person_name` repeat after rename complete. **On RE-CANARY PASS** → fires combined Stage 2 PR (D-C Stage 2 + D-D Stage 2 hard-deletes + 130 test-site migrations). **On RE-CANARY FAIL with new root cause** → diagnose; file follow-up spec. **Cumulative suite**: 2404 → 2421 (+17 collected — Phase 1 +5, Phase 2 +8, Phase 3 +4 including bonus one-shot consumed regression guard). | **P0.S7.D-E CLOSED 2026-05-19** — γ targeted fix for multi-speaker per-speaker history. New `_append_per_speaker_history` helper colocated with `_format_multispeaker_transcript` at `pipeline.py:~1077` appends each SECONDARY speaker's segment text to their own `_conversation_store._history` during the multi-speaker emit path. Primary speaker's history is appended by `conversation_turn` with the combined transcript (existing behavior unchanged). Without γ, a secondary speaker's overlap utterance was lost from their per-person history — so when they took the next primary turn, their conversation history showed nothing of what they just said during the multi-speaker burst. Single-stage fix (no Stage 2 follow-up); additive — if a future canary surfaces a deeper structural issue, the full α refactor ships as a separate post-canary spec. **Helper contract (Plan v2 §1.4 final + MEDIUM 1 dedup MANDATORY)**: signature `_append_per_speaker_history(spans_with_pids, primary_pid, now_ts)`. Invariants helper-enforced: skip None pids (voice ID failed); skip empty/whitespace-only content; skip rows where `pid == primary_pid` (conversation_turn covers primary; double-write would silently duplicate); DEDUP same-pid spans within the call via `seen_pids: set[str]` — each speaker gets at most ONE history append per multi-speaker turn (inverse-check on the upstream span-merger invariant per Plan v2 §1.3). Fire-and-forget via `create_task`; catches `RuntimeError` (no running loop in sync test contexts) silently. **`_spans_with_pids` collection inline at pipeline.py:~7045-7059** populates `(pid, name, text)` tuples alongside `_named_pairs` in the per-span transcription loop. **Call site at pipeline.py:~7790** wrapped in try/except per Plan v2 §2.3 MEDIUM 2 (helper failures are best-effort observability — never interrupt the user's dispatch path); call gated on `if _multi_speaker_detected:`. Phase 2 test 6 walks `run()` AST, asserts exactly one `_spans_with_pids = ...` assignment (the inline init) precedes the helper call site with no intervening reset/reassignment. Sub-pattern A 5th instance surfaced + locked at Phase 0; THIS CYCLE'S CLOSURE GRADUATES sub-pattern A from memory-note to numbered doctrine `### Phase-0-catches-wrong-premise` per Plan v2 §5.1 verbatim wording. **3-layer defensive None-handling** (Plan v2 §4 refined): Layer 1 = `_init_room_orchestrator()` in pipeline.py asserts all 6 deps non-None at production boot; Layer 2 = shim functions raise RuntimeError if `_room_orchestrator is None`; Layer 3 = per-method None checks only on the 3 methods that need face_db/brain_orchestrator (`build_shared_context_block` takes `db` arg directly; `fetch_recent_room_context` + `on_room_end` graceful-fallback to None). `RoomOrchestrator.__init__` STORES deps without asserting — tolerates None at construction time so the conftest autouse fixture can re-init with whatever subset of deps the test context provides. **Function-shim shape (NOT attribute binding)** per Plan v2 §3.3: each shim is a function that defers `_room_orchestrator.<method>(...)` lookup to CALL TIME (with None-check). Attribute binding at module-level would evaluate at IMPORT TIME → resolves to `None.build_room_block` → AttributeError at module load. Function shims avoid this trap. **Autouse-fixture extension (Plan v2 §3.3 MED 1)** landed in BOTH `conftest.py` (root) AND `tests/conftest.py` (subdir) — the subdir conftest defines a separate autouse fixture that overrides the root's for tests under `tests/`, so both needed extension. **Phase 1 Step 5 dry-run gate** per Plan v2 §3.4 fired against `tests/test_p0_s7_phase2.py` (heaviest user of `_build_shared_context_block`); initially red because the subdir conftest wasn't extended — extending it cleared the gate. Banked as Phase 1 in-flight calibration: the auditor predicted ONLY the root conftest extension would be needed; reality required both. Confirmation (e) initially didn't fire on the loose check; tightening the check made it fire. **In-flight observation banked**: AST-precise structural checks are strictly more reliable than source-substring checks when the legacy text persists in docstrings/comments — same shape as P0.S7.D-B confirmation (b) + P0.S7.D-C confirmation (e) banked dispositions. Without this, flag-gating the legacy block would drop the "Lexi (disputed identity)" signal in multi-person scenes where the disputed participant isn't the current speaker (the `<<<IDENTITY DISPUTED>>>` block in brain.py only fires for the current speaker). `_build_room_block` signature gained `best_friend_id: "str | None" = None` kwarg; both call sites (pipeline.py:3304 KAIROS + pipeline.py:7985 conversation_turn) updated to pass `best_friend_id=best_friend_id`/`_bf_id`. **D2 [Brain] Context: summary repoint**: `room=yes/no` field at pipeline.py:5423 now sources from `_multi_person = len(_all_snaps_ct) >= 2` instead of `room_context` truthiness — semantic preserved ("multi-person context in scope this turn") while implementation source flips from "legacy block rendered" → "multi-person session active." Forward-property AST structural tests beat source-text ordering checks at catching structural inversions. Useful in-flight observation for future spec drafts: AST forward-property tests are the workhorse, source-inspection ordering checks are loose belt-and-braces. Reverted; suite green. **P0.S7.4 γ strengthening (parallel micro-PR):** the γ MEMORY HONESTY DISCIPLINE bullet at `core/brain.py:2283-2293` strengthened per `tests/p0_s7_4_spec.md` §2.2: `MUST call search_memory BEFORE denying` → `MUST call search_memory IMMEDIATELY — on the FIRST mention, BEFORE responding`. Adds explicit forbidden-first-response list (5 patterns: "I'm not sure what you're referring to...", "I don't recall having a conversation...", "I don't think I said...", "I didn't actually...", "Can you remind me what we discussed?") + required-first-response pattern naming the tool call as the first action + hedge fallback gated on `AFTER retrieval` + `acknowledge the retrieval attempt` + extended failure-mode definition from `False denial` to `False denial OR pre-retrieval hedging`. Test `tests/test_p0_s7_4_gamma_strengthening.py::test_honesty_policy_block_contains_strengthened_memory_discipline` is source-inspection on the rendered prompt; uses a regex normalizer (`re.sub(r'"\s+"', "", raw_src)`) to collapse Python's adjacent-string-literal splits so multi-line phrases like `"BEFORE responding"` match contiguously despite the `"BEFORE " "responding"` source layout. **Two in-flight disposition observations banked**: (i) the spec at `tests/p0_s7_4_spec.md` named `_build_system_prompt` as the bullet's enclosing function, but the actual function is `render_session_stable_prefix` (Wave 4 cached-prefix optimization moved the HONESTY POLICY block out of `_build_system_prompt` for byte-identical Section 1+2 prefix caching). Test target corrected to `render_session_stable_prefix`; spec line range `core/brain.py:2283-2293` was correct. Same shape as the kind of spec-detail-vs-actual-code mismatch the "architect-reads-production-code-before-sign-off" discipline catches at closure. (ii) Pre-existing `test_p0_s7_2_phase1.py::test_honesty_policy_block_present_for_known_speakers` asserted the literal `"search_memory BEFORE denying"` rendered-prompt substring; the strengthening replaces that exact phrasing. Test predicate widened to accept EITHER the original `"search_memory BEFORE denying"` OR the strengthened `"search_memory IMMEDIATELY"` — the contract is the autonomous-retrieval imperative, not a specific phrasing. **Stage 2 trigger documented as banked follow-up dependency** in Pending Work — hard-delete `_build_cross_person_excerpts` + `CROSS_PERSON_EXCERPTS_ENABLED` flag + flag-related tests AFTER the user's bundled-queue canary validates D-A + D-C + D-B + D-D + D-E + γ AS A SET. On canary FAIL → flip flag to True for one-flag-flip rollback; Stage 2 stays unfiled until canary passes. **Real-LLM validation of P0.S7.4 strengthened bullet** also deferred to the same bundled-queue canary per P0.S7.2 §11.10 re-canary discipline (target: brain calls search_memory IMMEDIATELY on first mention of unrecognized reference; 0 forbidden-first-response hedges in canary log). Two-part fix surfaced by the 2026-05-18 Session A → Session B canary where Jagan returned to a fresh session, asked about cheese cookies recipe shared in a prior multi-person room, and brain confidently denied ever sharing it. Part γ (prompt-side honesty) tells brain to call search_memory BEFORE denying; Part κ (extraction) creates the facts that make search_memory return useful results. Both load-bearing — neither alone closes the gap. **Phase 1 — γ HONESTY POLICY extension**: appended **MEMORY HONESTY DISCIPLINE** bullet to `<<<HONESTY POLICY>>>` block (core/brain.py); inherits `HONESTY_POLICY_BLOCK_ENABLED` flag (S68 Bug N); names forbidden self-denial phrasings (`"I didn't actually..."` / `"I don't think I..."`) + severity anchor ("hard correctness failure — it makes the system untrustworthy") + hedge fallback (`"I don't have clear notes on that — can you remind me?"`). +2 Phase 1 tests (Plan v2 §6 exact match). **Phase 2 — κ multi-person assistant-turn extraction**: `TriageAgent.should_process` gains `room_participant_count: int = 1` kwarg + multi-person branch returns `(True, "multi_person_assistant_turn")` on assistant + count≥2 + content≥`ASSISTANT_TURN_EXTRACT_MIN_CHARS=80`. New `_ASSISTANT_ROOM_EXTRACT_SYSTEM` prompt with **5-action-type enum** (P1: shared_information / answered_question / **asked_question** / made_suggestion / engaged_general_discussion) + **verbatim P2 addressee counter-example** ("Jagan, Lexi mentioned earlier she likes cooking" → primary=Jagan, NOT Lexi). New `_fan_out_to_participants(extracted, names, pids, disputed_pids)` helper — ONE LLM call + mechanical fan-out (L1.c locked) producing per-participant Extractions with **P3 disputed-skip gate** (`if pid in disputed_pids: continue`) + **auditor Q1 enum-validation belt-and-braces** (`_VALID_ACTION_TYPES` frozenset; LLM-hallucinated value collapses to `engaged_general_discussion`) + privacy_level=`personal` (D6) for both subject-of-fact AND witness-of-fact + confidence=0.85. `Extraction` dataclass gained additive `person_id: str | None = None` field (Plan v2 §4.2 specifies `person_id=pid` on fan-out extractions; field was missing pre-fix). New `ExtractionAgent.extract_assistant_room_turn(content, names, pids, disputed_pids)` method. No deliberate stability tripwire shipped — the 36 Hypothesis tests are themselves the regression coverage; re-emergence would surface naturally on the next full-suite run. If the flake re-emerges, file a fresh follow-up rather than re-opening this entry. No discipline-count bumps — re-running surfaced no gap (verification-before-completion is a named doctrine without a tracked numeric count; routine application of the rule, no track-record entry needed). | **P0.S1 COMPLETE — Anti-Spoof on Every Face Match**. Closed the auditor's THE GAP at the progressive_enroll site (pipeline.py site 5 — voice-only stranger engagement never gated by anti-spoof; gallery poisoning vector). Full surface shipped across 4 phases (Phase 0 audit → Plan v1 → Plan v2 with HIGH 1/2/3 + MED 4/5/6 + LOW 7/8/9 refinements → 4 implementation phases). **Phase 1 — Foundation**: `VALID_EMBEDDING_SOURCES` dropped to 3 entries (Path 1b — strict deletion + required `source` kwarg, no default; architect's call after developer surfaced the test-fan-out calibration miss). New `ALLOWED_EMBEDDING_SOURCES_REQUIRING_ANTI_SPOOF` frozenset (= VALID after D4) + `add_embedding(..., anti_spoof_verdict: bool | None = None)` catch-all gate (fail-closed default — refuses protected sources unless verdict=True). 4 production call sites migrated (sites 1-3 capture verdict via `_fb_verdict` / `_en_verdict` locals; site 4 recognition_update threads `_anti_spoof_ok`). `TrackEntry` + `TrackSnapshot` extended with 5 anti-spoof fields; new `upsert_embedding_with_verdict` + `peek_anti_spoof_verdict` (atomic C0 contract — embedding + verdict observable together via peek). New `core/anti_spoof_rejection_store.py` Store subclass (MED 5 — Plan v1's module-level dict promoted to Store-pattern per P0.6 ratchet cap=0). Config: `ANTI_SPOOF_BURST_THRESHOLD=3` + `ANTI_SPOOF_BURST_WINDOW_SECS=60.0` + 4 reason codes (PASSED / REJECTED / UNAVAILABLE / NO_VERDICT — C1 distinguishability). **+18 Phase 1 tests** (17 anticipated + 1 bonus sanity); **5 pre-existing tests updated** but actual fan-out was ~18 calls in `test_faiss_delete.py` + `test_pipeline.py` + `test_faiss_sql_atomicity.py` + `test_faiss_async_rebuild.py` — calibration miss banked. **Phase 2 — Producer**: `_classify_anti_spoof_verdict(frame, bbox, checker)` module-level helper (single source of truth for 4-reason-code mapping — both scan paths consume it; reduces drift). Both `_background_vision_loop` scan paths (§8.1 ambient-listen + §8.2 secondary-scan) wired: classify → atomic `upsert_embedding_with_verdict` → append to `_h2_iter_verdicts` per-iteration accumulator. Greeting branch reuses captured verdict (`if _as_live is not True:` instead of calling `verify_live` twice on same frame — efficiency + structural correctness). H2 vision_frame emit upgraded to use real per-iteration aggregate (`_h2_iter_live`) — replaces pre-P0.S1 placeholder `True`. **VisionFramePayload schema widened** from `bool` to `Optional[bool]` to surface the ANTI_SPOOF_REASON_UNAVAILABLE case at replay time (P0.0.7 prerequisite locked 2-state; P0.S1 Phase 2 needs 3-state — the original 2-state coerced unavailable to False, conflating hardware-down with active attack). C0 same-frame discipline enforced via `# P0S1-C0:` marker-comments at both scan paths (developer chose §14b.2 marker fallback over §3.2 AST graph walk — production uses `run_in_executor(None, embedder.embed, _crop)` making `embedder.embed` a Name expression, not a Call; AST graph walk would be brittle, marker route is unambiguous). **+8 Phase 2 tests** (5 per §5 + 3 reason-code matrix split + 1 bonus H2-emit guard). **Phase 3 — Consumer + watchdog**: progressive_enroll site 5 closure — reads verdict via `_track_store.peek_anti_spoof_verdict(_gate_track)` (sync consumer side of C0 atomic upsert), threads through `add_embedding(..., anti_spoof_verdict=_gate_live)`. On block: logs `BLOCKED progressive_enroll`, calls `_anti_spoof_rejection_store.record_rejection`, dispatches `report_anti_spoof_rejection` to watchdog, fires `report_anti_spoof_burst` at **EXACT EQUALITY** (`if _rej_count == ANTI_SPOOF_BURST_THRESHOLD:` per §14b.1 — NOT `>=` which would re-fire). D9 voice-only fallthrough preserved (Bug C Session 64 code path unchanged) — face write blocked but else-branch grants bootstrap credits, session opens, voice profile seeds. D10.c silent-to-speaker — TTS path untouched; dashboard sees `[Anti-Spoof] BLOCKED` + watchdog feed. WatchdogAgent gained `report_anti_spoof_rejection` (severity=info, per-instance, metadata carries reason+score+track+pid for C1 distinguishability) + `report_anti_spoof_burst` (severity=warning, fires once at threshold). BrainOrchestrator passthroughs added matching `report_dispute_rename_burst` shape. Cleanup hooks: `_close_session` captures both identity-bound + stranger-mapped track_ids BEFORE prune-for-session-close, pops each from rejection store. Stale-prune in `_background_vision_loop` computes `_pre_prune_tracks - _active_tids` set-difference and pops rejection store symmetrically — returning user re-using a track gets a fresh window. **+10 Phase 3 tests** (9 per §10 + 1 split catch-all matrix). **Phase 4 — Invariants + tripwires**: **5 AST invariants** (`tests/test_p0_s1_phase4.py`): ALLOWED set locked, no-legacy-unknown in production callers, every protected-source add_embedding passes verdict, every protected-source call site has upstream verify_live OR _classify_anti_spoof_verdict OR peek_anti_spoof_verdict, non-literal source args flagged (registry empty). **4 D9 tripwires**: recognition_update catch-all blocks verdict=False (MED 4 belt-and-braces vs gate-strip refactors), recognition_update catch-all blocks verdict=None, voice-only fallthrough branch intact (rejection elif must NOT set `_face_captured=True`), burst-alert dispatch uses EXACT equality (regex scan forbids `>=` / `>`). **5 replay smoke tests** (verdict T/F/None matrix round-tripping through `_event_log_default` JSON encoder + `from_json_dict` consumer — proves Optional[bool] schema widening works; reason-code distinguishability through WatchdogAgent metadata; burst-fixture sequence shows exactly one dispatch). **3/3 deliberate-regression confirmations** (induction-surfaces-invariant-gaps): (a) injected `add_embedding(source="progressive_enroll")` without verdict kwarg → verdict-passed AST test failed correctly; (b) injected progressive_enroll call with verdict but no upstream verify_live → upstream-verify_live test failed correctly; (c) injected `verify_live(other_frame, ...)` with different frame variable under a `# P0S1-C0:` marker → same-frame marker test failed correctly. All 3 reverted; suite green. **Architectural notes banked**: (a) **Path 1b execution** — architect's call after developer surfaced the §11 5-vs-18 test-fan-out calibration miss; locked D4 executed strictly (no deviation) with `source` made required (no default — eliminates silent backdoor). (b) **§14b.2 marker-comment route choice** for same-frame discipline — chosen over AST graph walk because `run_in_executor` indirection makes `embedder.embed` a Name not a Call; marker route is unambiguous, AST route would be brittle. Architect flagged as candidate **developer-improves-on-spec** instance at Phase 2; standing up at closure. (c) **VisionFramePayload schema widening** (Phase 2) — P0.0.7 prerequisite was locked 2-state `bool`; P0.S1 Phase 2 widened to `Optional[bool]` because the 4-reason-code semantic requires distinguishing UNAVAILABLE (None) from REJECTED (False). The pre-existing P0.0.7 invariant test asserting "must NOT be Optional" was updated with explicit rationale documenting the deliberate widening — not a regression, a semantic upgrade. (d) **C1 reason-code distinguishability end-to-end** — TrackStore carries reason at producer (`_classify_anti_spoof_verdict`), threads to consumer via `peek_anti_spoof_verdict`, surfaces in `report_anti_spoof_rejection` metadata at dashboard layer. Operators distinguish hardware-down from active attack at every level, not just structural. (e) **Site 5 was THE GAP** — Phase 0 audit `tests/p0_s1_audit.md` documented progressive_enroll as the only protected source NEVER going through verify_live; Phase 3 closed via verdict-from-track-store pattern (producer writes verdict atomically at scan time, consumer reads it at gate-pass time — same-frame discipline structurally enforced). **Cumulative test delta**: +18 Phase 1 + +8 Phase 2 + +10 Phase 3 + +14 Phase 4 = +50 tests. (4) Read-only replay CLI at `tools/replay_session.py` (~410 lines) with filters (`--session`, `--room`, `--type`, `--since`, `--limit`), parent-chain tree rendering (`└─` indent for natural-pair children), `_PAYLOAD_CLASSES.from_json_dict` dispatch for per-event-type one-line summaries, URI-mode read-only DB open (zero side effects against live production DB), UTF-8 stdout reconfigure for Windows cp1252 compatibility. (5) Scenario fixture builders at `tests/fixtures/event_log_fixtures.py` (~520 lines) covering 4 canonical chains (greeting flow / stranger first-encounter / multi-person room / dispute path) — **D7.4 reusable**: P0.S1 imports `build_greeting_flow`/etc. to replay anti-spoof scenarios without live camera. (6) **+61 new event_log tests across 4 files**: 15 contract tests (`tests/test_event_log_contract.py`), 13 logical / 35 parametrized invariant cases (`tests/test_event_log_invariants.py`; includes D7 N=1 producer-per-event_type AST scan with exactly-one upper+lower bound, R2 JSON round-trip per payload class, C1 `_recent_parent` writer-task-scope-only AST scan, C2 `from_json_dict` round-trip per payload, C3 `_PAYLOAD_CLASSES` dispatch coverage, R1 cache-clear on session close, R3 migration_0012 end-to-end), 11 hook coverage tests (`tests/test_event_log_producer_coverage.py`), 5 replay smoke tests (`tests/test_event_log_replay.py`; round-trip integrity / parent-chain integrity with orphan tolerance / filter composition AND semantics / schema-version dispatch / **anti-spoof field preservation as P0.S1 load-bearing prerequisite**). (7) Health-log integration: `HealthSnapshot.event_log_drops` + `event_log_emit_failures` fields with int=0 defaults; `format_health_line` conditionally surfaces non-zero counters; `format_health_alerts` emits **two distinct alerts** (drops → "writer task falling behind, bounded queue shedding" / emit_failures → "Grep `[EventLog] WARN`") so operators investigate the right surface — collapsing to a single "event_log degraded" alert would force them to check both surfaces every time. (8) **D5 circular-dependency guard preserved end-to-end** — no `event_log_dropped` self-emitting event; counters ARE the observability channel. **Architectural notes banked**: (a) `safe_emit_sync` consolidation is the 5th instance of **developer-improves-on-spec** discipline (P0.8.2 F2, P0.9.1 init_ledger, P0.9.2 verify split, P0.10 Block C band-divergence trigger, P0.0.7 Step 5 polish) — track record now 5-for-5, discipline already named in CLAUDE.md Architectural Disciplines section.
