| P0.5 | 2026-05-08 | **P0.5 — FAISS + faces.db cross-storage atomicity — 7 bugs surfaced and closed** — Pre-fix bug: `add_embedding` updated FAISS before SQL commit (FAISS-first ordering); a failed SQL INSERT left an orphan embedding in FAISS. Fix: SQL-first transactional pattern (`transaction()` context manager, S65 ROLLBACK race handled) + FAISS-second try/except with `_mark_faiss_dirty()` sentinel applied to all 5 paired-write methods (`add_embedding`, `delete_person`, `prune_old_strangers`, `prune_zero_value_stranger`, `prune_outlier_embeddings`). `prune_outlier_embeddings` was a hidden P0.5 gap (missing `_index_lock`, `transaction()`, `_mark_faiss_dirty()`) found by the inverse check test and fixed same session — bare `_conn.commit()` + `_rebuild_faiss()` with no lock, no transaction, no sentinel. Boot reconciliation in `_load_faiss()`: checks sentinel OR count mismatch → calls `_rebuild_faiss()` → clears sentinel on success; if rebuild fails at boot → `_faiss_degraded = True` + sentinel preserved + no raise. `recognize()` returns `(None, None, 0.0)` in degraded mode. `FaceDB.transaction()` added. Three sentinel helpers: `_sentinel_path()`, `_mark_faiss_dirty()`, `_clear_faiss_dirty()`. `_faiss_degraded: bool = False` attribute on `__init__`. Bug fingerprint: Test 1 asserts `db.index.ntotal == pre_faiss_size` after SQL crash — FAISS must be untouched. Two additional Phase-0-caveat bugs fixed in scope: (6) `count_voice_embeddings` was spuriously acquiring `_index_lock` — voice-gallery functions must not hold the face-index lock; (7) `BrainDB.transaction()` ROLLBACK race (S65) — inner try/except around ROLLBACK now matches `FaceDB.transaction()`. Follow-up items: `prune_zero_value_stranger` and `prune_outlier_embeddings` added to `PAIRED_WRITE_METHODS`; `test_all_paired_write_sites_are_in_tuple()` inverse check added (AST scan of all FaceDB methods with FAISS calls, asserts all in tuple); `complete-plan.md` updated with P0.5 closure + inverse-check pattern lesson + P0.X scope expansion note. **+23 tests** (1586 → 1609): `tests/test_faiss_sql_atomicity.py` (7 slow crash-injection) + `tests/test_faiss_atomicity_invariants.py` (16 fast AST/structural, including 1 new inverse check + 2 new parametrized paired-write entries). Phase-0-mandatory + inverse-check discipline paid back: "+30 min for inverse check" caught `prune_outlier_embeddings` as a real silent-divergence bug that would have shipped as production code. |
| P0.X | 2026-05-09 | **P0.X — brain.db ↔ Kuzu cross-write divergence atomicity — CLOSED** — Three Kuzu write patterns codified and enforced: (1) SCHEMA_MIGRATION (`_ensure_graph_sync` — always rebuilds from brain.db, inherently safe); (2) RAISE (`on_identity_confirmed` — SQL transaction first, `_mark_kuzu_dirty()` before Kuzu op, `_clear_kuzu_dirty()` on success, re-raise on failure); (3) SWALLOW (`_persist_extraction_to_kuzu`, `_retroactive_scan`, `_process_turn` — Kuzu try/except with `_mark_kuzu_dirty()` + log, no re-raise; brain.db is authoritative and Kuzu heals on next `_ensure_graph_sync()`). Sentinel machinery added: `_kuzu_dirty_path()`, `_mark_kuzu_dirty()`, `_clear_kuzu_dirty()`, `_is_kuzu_dirty()`. Boot reconciliation: `BrainDB.__init__` checks sentinel → forces `_ensure_graph_sync()` rebuild on next access; `_kuzu_degraded: bool` flag; degraded mode returns empty results rather than raising. Hidden paired-write site: `_process_turn` in `BrainOrchestrator` had `self._graph_db.invalidate_fact(` in the ContradictionAgent loop without `_mark_kuzu_dirty()` — found by inverse check test `test_all_kuzu_write_sites_are_covered`, fixed same session (same P0.5 discipline that caught `prune_outlier_embeddings`). Detector accuracy fix: `_check_raise_pattern` rewrote raise-detection to walk `ast.Try` nodes and find specifically the Kuzu-writing try block (by checking try body for Kuzu write markers) before inspecting its except handlers for `ast.Raise` — old logic found any `raise` in any except handler (including the SQL transaction wrapper), causing false passes on synthetic case 3. Two forward tests added for `_process_turn` (sentinel written + no re-raise). **+19 tests** in `tests/test_kuzu_atomicity_invariants.py`: 17 fast AST/structural invariants (RAISE-pattern forward, SWALLOW-pattern forward, inverse check, detector self-tests) + 2 `_process_turn`-specific targeted tests. |

| P0.3 | 2026-05-08 | **P0.3 — multi-word name contiguous substring fix in `_user_text_gate_passes`** — Bug: the `(\w+)` capture group only grabs the first word of a name. The v1 fix added a `_remainder` check (`_remainder in user_text`) that was BUGGY — it allowed non-contiguous word combinations like "call me sarah and my friend is jane" → `_remainder="jane"` in text → allowed "Sarah Jane" (privilege escalation via LLM fabrication of a full name). **v3 fix** (applied to `pipeline.py`): replaced the buggy remainder block with a single contiguous substring check `if _nv_lower in _lt` — the full proposed name must appear as a contiguous substring of user_text. Also applied `_nfkc_lower()` (NFKC normalization + casefold) to all three inputs (user_text, new_value, captured) for homoglyph defense. `_remainder` variable removed entirely. **New files**: `tests/test_user_text_gate_multiword.py` (25 behavior tests — 3 single-word baseline, 10 legitimate multi-word contiguous, 4 non-contiguous discriminating cases that v1 allowed and v3 now rejects, 6 fabrication-rejection, 2 empty/None), `tests/test_user_text_gate_invariants.py` (5 structural + behavioral invariants — NFKC ≥3 calls, `_nv_lower in _lt` present, `_remainder` absent, discriminating case rejected, legitimate case accepted). **+31 tests (1527→1558)**. |

| Wave7-24+25 | 2026-05-07 | **Wave 7 Items 25+24 — concurrent DB access tests + multi-speaker integration tests** — pure additive test coverage, zero production code changes. **Item 25 (`tests/test_concurrent_db_access.py`)**: 5 tests covering WAL invariants and threading safety. `TestWALInvariants` (3 tests): verifies all 3 DBs (FaceDB, BrainDB, ClassifierDB) use `PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL` by querying each freshly-created instance. `TestConcurrentAccess` (2 tests): (1) concurrent reader never starved during writer — 100 log_turn inserts over 1s while reader runs SELECT COUNT(*) every 10ms, asserts ≥50 successful reads with max latency < 100ms; (2) two raw sqlite3 connections writing 20 rows each simultaneously serialize via busy_timeout without raising `OperationalError`. Key fix: FaceDB `persons` table PK is `id` (not `person_id`) — discovered via schema grep. **Item 24 (`tests/test_multispeaker_integration.py`)**: 9 tests using stub-before-import pattern to avoid Windows torchaudio DLL crash (OSError 0xc0000139). `core.voice` and `core.audio` stubbed into `sys.modules` BEFORE importing `pipeline.py`. `TestMultispeakerTranscript` (4 tests): N=2 legacy `[Name]: text` format preserved (S3B.4 backward-compat), N≥3 uses `[N voices simultaneously]` header block, `None` name → `unknown_1` label, <2 pairs → empty returns. `TestUserToUserHeuristic` (3 tests): vocative to other person → `("user_to_person", name)`, vocative to system name → `("addressing_ai", ...)`, subject mention without vocative → `None`. `TestRoomBlock` (2 tests): 3-session room block names all 3 speakers (fix: session dict key is `"person_name"` not `"name"`), single-session → `None`. | 119 | 2026-05-01 | **Reconciler negative-cosine fix — stranger silently dropped on ECAPA anti-correlation** — 2026-05-01 live canary (5-person session): Lexi said "Hi Kara, can you tell me what is the escape velocity of Earth?" three times, got zero response each time. Pyannote correctly returned 2 segments; reconciler hit no rule matched → `no_action` → turn silently dropped. **Root cause**: `voice.identify()` returns the actual ECAPA cosine similarity on gallery miss — not `0.0`. Lexi vs Jagan's gallery produced negative cosine (~`-0.05`) because L2-normalized embeddings of very different speakers (different gender/accent/tone) are routinely anti-correlated. Three reconciler rules checked `== 0.0` or `0.0 < confidence`, both missing negatives, causing full cascade fallthrough. **Fix (3 rule predicates in `core/reconciler.py`)**: `_p4_pyannote_vouched_stranger`: `== 0.0` → `<= 0.0`; `_p4_new_stranger_low_match`: `0.0 < c < threshold` → `c < threshold and c != 0.0`; `_p5_no_session_new_stranger`: same pattern. `_p4_voice_ambiguous_*` rules left at `== 0.0` intentionally — those represent genuine no-signal (embedding failed), not anti-correlated audio. Ordering invariant confirmed: new-stranger-producing rules (indices 14, 15) still fire before ambiguous rules (17, 18). **+2 regression tests (1368→1370)**: exact Lexi scenario with `confidence=-0.05` on `_p4_pyannote_vouched_stranger` + single-segment `-0.08` on `_p4_new_stranger_low_match`. No config changes, no schema changes, no prompt changes. |
| 118 | 2026-04-26 | **Multi-segment voice routing stranger detection + pyannote TF32 ReproducibilityWarning suppression** — canary 2026-04-25 23:21 caught a real correctness bug: pyannote returned 2 segments (Jagan + a stranger asking "what is the escape velocity of earth?"), voice ID couldn't match the second voice against any enrolled gallery, the resolver fell through to "route to current" (Jagan) because no other candidates were in the scene. Result: Jagan got wrongly attributed with the stranger's question. **Fix A**: new `VOICE_ROUTING_STRANGER_FLOOR=0.30` config; `_resolve_actual_speaker` gained `n_diarize_segments` kwarg; new gate inside Priority 4 returns `multi_segment_voice_mismatch` when ≥2 segments AND `v_score < floor` — pipeline.run dispatches to drop the turn (same drop-and-let-repeat pattern as `short_utterance_voice_mismatch`). Reviewer's spec sketched `_open_stranger_for_unmatched()` opening a stranger session mid-turn but scope-find went with the simpler drop pattern — no new session machinery, same correctness outcome. **Fix B**: pyannote `ReproducibilityWarning` for TF32 was still firing every startup despite Session 114's UserWarning filter — pyannote uses a CUSTOM warning class subclass, and Python's filter matches by class identity not inheritance. Dual approach: message-pattern regex (`r".*TensorFloat-32.*disabled.*"`, `r".*TF32.*"`) + best-effort class-import filter for `ReproducibilityWarning`. Both run BEFORE any pyannote-touching import. **+6 tests (1267→1273)**: multi-segment with stranger drops + multi-segment with strong match routes normally + single-segment unchanged + pipeline dispatch wires action + TF32 filter targets message + class + filter runs before core.config import. Behavior preservation on Fix B (cosmetic). Behavior CORRECTION on Fix A — currently misattributing → after, drops noisy turn. Existing tests unchanged. |

| 112 | 2026-04-24 | **Session 112 pre-3B structural unlock — room-session concept (4 parts)** — biggest pre-3B change. Puts the scaffolding for RoomOrchestrator in place: every person session now belongs to a room_session that lives while ANY person is present and ends when the last person leaves. **Part 1 — room_session_id lifecycle**: new module-level `_active_room_session: str | None` in pipeline.py. `_open_session` mints `room_{int(now)}_{uuid6}` on the FIRST open into an empty room; subsequent opens (another person joining) inherit the existing id via a local `_current_room_session` capture. Session dict gains a `room_session_id` field that 3B retrieval will use as the scope key. `_close_session` checks if the close empties `_active_sessions`; if yes, logs "[Room] Room session ended: ..." and fires `_on_room_end(room_session_id)` via `asyncio.create_task` (fire-and-forget) — current 3A-era body is just a log placeholder, 3B will hook real synthesis (room-conversation summary, cross-speaker insight clustering, missed-safety-flag detection across the whole transcript, household-relationship updates). Guard: `asyncio.get_running_loop()` wrapped in try/except RuntimeError so sync test contexts don't emit "coroutine was never awaited" warnings when no loop is available. Both `log_turn` call sites in `conversation_turn` and `_kairos_tick` pass `room_session_id=_active_sessions[pid]["room_session_id"]` + `audience_ids=[pid]` so `conversation_log` rows land the Session 107 Phase 3A.6 Part 3 columns (schema was already present; wiring lands now). **Part 2 — KAIROS room-aware speaker selection**: new `_kairos_preferred_speaker(best_friend_id)` helper replaces the old `_primary_person_id()` call in the KAIROS path at the `run()` inner-loop top. Policy: single-session → return the one active pid; multi-session with best_friend active → prefer best_friend (natural engagement target); multi-session without best_friend → longest-silence pid via `max(key=lambda pid: now - last_spoke_at)`. Gated on new `KAIROS_PREFER_BEST_FRIEND=True` config so a one-line rollback to legacy `_primary_person_id()` behavior is possible. **Part 3 — `_persons_in_frame` session-scoped cleanup**: `_close_session` now pops the pid from `_persons_in_frame` immediately instead of waiting for the SCENE_STALE_SECS (30s) age-out. Lingering entries were causing scene blocks to render people whose sessions had already closed. If they're physically still present, next background face scan re-adds them (correct — they're visible); if they actually left, the stale entry is gone right away. Resolves the scene-contradicts-session-state class of bug. **Part 4 — Kuzu v3 audit (developer discretion → option (a) SKIP)**: reviewed `find_shared_entities` graph traversal for session-isolation leaks. Finding: cross-person inference matches entity VALUES (names like "Lexi", "cricket"). Yesterday's shared entity and today's shared entity are both legitimate cross-person matches — Jagan "knows Lexi" from last week is still TRUE today, not a stale-session bug. Session boundaries don't meaningfully change the correctness of shared-entity output. The sensitive-value leak concern (Session 107 wording) is already bounded by Session 106 Migration 2's SQL filter (`privacy_level != 'system_only'` on the recipient's DISTINCT values SELECT). Personal-tier cross-person matches ("you both mentioned diabetes") are low-leak by nature — entity name only, no attribute context, brain handles via hedged phrasing. Full Kuzu v3 deferred to Phase 3B proper, when audience_ids + room retrieval wiring for conversation_log arrives and graph visibility model is revisited end-to-end. Decision documented as a new comment block on `find_shared_entities` so a future maintainer doesn't assume it's still "TBD" — it's considered + deferred with reason. **+8 regression tests (1152→1160)**: (1) first open mints room_session_id with `room_` prefix + session dict carries it; (2) second open inherits the minted id (join, not mint); (3) last close clears `_active_room_session` + next open mints a DIFFERENT id (distinct rooms, not resurrection); (4) closing one of many sessions keeps room alive; (5) `_persons_in_frame` pops on `_close_session` (Part 3); (6) KAIROS prefers best_friend over most-recent speaker in multi-person scene (Part 2 primary policy); (7) KAIROS falls back to longest-silence when best_friend absent (Part 2 fallback, 3-person scene with 180s/90s/0s silences — 180s wins); (8) single-session KAIROS returns the one pid regardless of best_friend status (trivial-path guard). Plus source-inspection test that Part 4 audit decision is documented in `find_shared_entities`. **Classifier prompt hash unchanged**. Zero schema migrations (Session 107 Phase 3A.6 Part 3 had already added the `room_session_id` column on conversation_log — Session 112 finally wires the write path). Blast radius: pipeline.py (module var, `_open_session`, `_close_session`, `_on_room_end` new fn, `_kairos_preferred_speaker` new fn, 2 log_turn call sites + 1 KAIROS selector call site) + `core/config.py` (1 new flag) + `core/brain_agent.py` docstring-only Part 4 audit note. **Handoff — 1 pre-3B session remains**: **S113** — LLM turn allocation via `<<<ADDRESS DECISION>>>` prompt block + batched-greeting LLM decision + session-end synthesis integration tests (~5-10 tests). After S113 lands, every critical audit gap is closed and Phase 3B RoomOrchestrator builds on a clean foundation. |
| 111 | 2026-04-24 | **Session 111 pre-3B hardening — 3 criticals + 2 highs from the pre-3B audit** — reviewer's pre-3B plan splits 5 criticals + 5 highs across 3 sessions (S111-S113) before RoomOrchestrator work. Session 111 takes the history/context hygiene bundle. **Critical #5 — emotion agent reset on session open**: `_emotion_agents[pid]` dict persisted agent objects across session open/close cycles, so the 3-turn rolling window carried prior-session emotions into today's session (user whose last session ended on "sad/angry" had that state seed their next session's context block on turn 1). Fix: `_open_session` pops `_emotion_agents[pid]` on FRESH opens (the non-idempotent branch). Re-opens (same session continuing) preserve the agent — popping there would reset mid-conversation on every voice-routing re-enter. `conversation_turn` already lazy-creates the agent on first emotion-detection call, so pop is cheap. **Critical #2 — session-boundary filter in cross-person excerpts**: `_conversation[pid]` is an unbounded in-memory history shared across sessions. The old `_build_cross_person_excerpts` took `other_hist[-6:]` without a time filter — yesterday's turns bled into today's room context, confusing the brain about what's happening NOW. Fix: messages now carry a `ts` field written at append time in `conversation_turn`; `_build_cross_person_excerpts` filters `other_hist` to messages with `ts >= other_session.started_at` before taking the last 6. Backward-compat: messages without `ts` (older entries) get treated as ts=0 → filtered out, which is fine because the boundary-filter is new. **Critical #3 — addressee labels on assistant messages**: old excerpt format `"you: [response]"` was ambiguous in 4-person rooms — whose addressee was "you"? Fix: assistant messages now carry an `addressed_to` field (set to `effective_name` at append time — the pid whose session the turn belonged to). `_build_cross_person_excerpts` renders `"you [to Lexi]: [response]"` when `addressed_to` is present, preserving the bare `"you:"` fallback only when the field is missing (old entries). Reviewer's spec originally had a conditional ("when the response had a clear addressee") but implementation lands it unconditionally whenever the field is present — a bare "you:" in a 4-person room is strictly less informative than "you [to X]:", so always-render is the right call. **HIGH — timestamps/age suffix in cross-person excerpts**: excerpt lines now render a "(just now)" (< 60s) or "(Xm ago)" suffix based on message ts vs. `time.time()` at the render call. Brain can now judge freshness — an exchange from 8 minutes ago weights differently from one 30 seconds ago, even if both are in the last 6 turns. **HIGH — 2 silent except:pass fixes** at pipeline.py:4354 and 4371. Both are enrollment tmp-file cleanup paths that previously swallowed `os.unlink` failures silently. Replaced with `except Exception as _cleanup_e: print(f"[Pipeline] enroll tmp cleanup failed: {_cleanup_e!r}")`. Tmp-file leaks were invisible before; now diagnosable. **+6 regression tests (1146→1152)**: (1) emotion agent pops on fresh session open, (2) emotion agent survives re-open (idempotent path), (3) conversation_turn source contains `ts` + `addressed_to` writes, (4) excerpts filter by session boundary (stale yesterday-turn excluded), (5) excerpts render `you [to X]` + age suffix with real `time.time()` values so the helper's internal `time.time()` call works against the test's timestamps, (6) enrollment tmp cleanup sites log instead of swallowing (source-inspection count ≥ 2). **Classifier prompt hash unchanged**. Zero schema changes. Blast radius: `_open_session` (1 pop line), `conversation_turn` history-append (2 new dict fields), `_build_cross_person_excerpts` (filter + render logic), pipeline.py enrollment tmp-cleanup sites (2 logged except blocks). **Handoff — 2 pre-3B sessions remain**: **S112** room-session concept (biggest single change — room_session_id parallel to per-person sessions + KAIROS prefers best_friend over primary + `_persons_in_frame` session-scoped cleanup + Kuzu graph session filter with v3 bump). **S113** LLM decision points + session-end test coverage (turn allocation via `<<<ADDRESS DECISION>>>` prompt block, batched-greeting LLM decision, session-end synthesis integration tests). After S113 lands, every critical architectural gap from the audit is closed. Phase 3B RoomOrchestrator then builds on a clean foundation. |
| 110 | 2026-04-24 | **Latency fix — autocompact and emotion processing moved to fire-and-forget background (saves ~400-900ms per turn)** — 2026-04-24 canary validated the full Phase 3A arc end-to-end (the "milestone moment" at line 587: brain proactively surfaced Lexi's suicidal + financial disclosures to Jagan when he returned, Jagan said "she is my neighbor, let me go and check with her" — the whole point of the system). But Jagan also flagged increased brain response latency. Reviewer ran a full-codebase audit of what's in the critical path vs. what runs background. Finding: **`autocompact_history` at pipeline.py:3367 was `await`ed synchronously on every turn past the token threshold** — blocking 400-800ms typically, up to 3s on retry (API hiccup + 2s backoff + retry). Every 30+-turn session hit it regularly. Also secondary finding: emotion `process_turn` at line 3424 ran sequentially via `await run_in_executor` — 15-25ms on the critical path per turn. Everything else correctly backgrounded (classifier, extraction, contradiction, retroscan, social graph, pattern agent, session-end synthesis, tool execution, search_memory, query embedding refresh, pyannote, scene/room/visitor reads, get_context cache hits). **Fix 1 (CRITICAL)** — `_compact_history_background(person_id, person_name)` helper wraps `autocompact_history` in try/except, writes result back to `_conversation[person_id]` when done, pops `_compact_running` session-dict flag in finally. Main flow fires it via `asyncio.create_task`; this turn's brain read uses CURRENT (possibly uncompacted) history, NEXT turn's read sees the compacted version naturally once the task writes it back. Model context window (~128K) dwarfs threshold (~3-4K), so one turn of uncompacted-but-under-limit history is safe. Stranger-session guard (`person_id.startswith("stranger_")`) preserved — strangers don't accumulate mature history. Additional `_compact_running` session flag prevents double-dispatch when a previous compaction is still in flight. **Fix 2 (HIGH)** — `_emotion_process_background(pid, pname, agent, text)` helper wraps the `run_in_executor(agent.process_turn, text)` call + the `store_temporal_fact(current_feeling)` side-effect in one background task. Main flow fires `asyncio.create_task`. `emotion_context` for THIS turn reads the agent's PRIOR cached state (pre-this-turn); this turn's fresh emotion lands on NEXT turn's context build. One-turn lag is fine — emotion changes slowly, EmotionAgent already runs a rolling 3-turn window, and humans don't expect the AI to acknowledge mood shifts mid-utterance. Fact-storage side-effect also runs inside the background task (sub-ms SQLite write but preserves the "never block critical path" invariant). Reviewer's original spec suggested parallelizing a for-loop over all persons' emotion agents, but the actual code only processes ONE speaker per turn — fire-and-forget is the right generalization. **+4 regression tests (1142→1146)**: (1) specific blocking pattern `_conversation[person_id] = await autocompact_history(` is gone from conversation_turn; (2) stranger-session guard preserved (strangers don't dispatch compaction); (3) `_compact_history_background` body has `try:` + `except Exception:` (unhandled coroutine exceptions would produce silent "Task exception was never retrieved" warnings); (4) emotion `process_turn` is wrapped in `_emotion_process_background` with `run_in_executor` still used INSIDE (HuggingFace work is sync CPU; thread offload preserved, just detached from critical path). **Classifier prompt hash unchanged**. **Zero schema changes, zero prompt changes**. Blast radius: 2 call-site rewrites in `conversation_turn`, 2 new helper coroutines (one per fix). **Estimated combined latency win**: 400-900ms per turn on typical post-threshold sessions. Consistent ~1-2s brain response replacing the 1.5-4s seen in the Session 110 canary, with the 14s autocompact-retry-cascade outlier eliminated entirely. **Handoff — ready for Phase 3B (RoomOrchestrator)** now that the latency floor is clean. Phase 3A arc validated + performance-cleaned in one session. |

(b) `find_knowledge_id` and its retroscan/confirmation callers — internal write-path row lookups, no requester context, no user-facing retrieval; filtering here would skip maintenance operations on rows visible to a "requester" who doesn't exist. (c) `_process_turn`'s extraction-dedup `get_active_knowledge` at line 6109 — write-path correctness needs ALL existing facts visible for conflict detection, regardless of who's speaking; filtering here would create duplicate rows under different person_ids when a speaker dedups against a row they can't see. | 105 | 2026-04-23 | **Bug N safety-flag preservation (3 parts) + Obs A STT char-run filter + Obs B shadow pronoun/role blocklist** — 2026-04-23 canary validated the Session 96-104 cross-person retrieval arc end-to-end (Bug J entity binding held, Bug K anti-contradiction held across 5 follow-ups, Bug L zero AI self-facts leaked). But surfaced one CRITICAL safety bug and two minor polish items. **Bug N (CRITICAL) — suicidal-ideation fact overwritten by transient mood**. Timeline in Lexi's session: Turn 33: "I feel like committing suicide" → `Lexi.current_mood='suicidal'` stored. Turn 37: "I like food and I like my boyfriend" → ContradictionAgent REPLACED with `current_mood='loving'`. Turn 39: "I will come in a bit" → REPLACED with `current_mood='distressed'`. When Jagan later asked about Lexi's state, the suicidal disclosure was GONE from the DB — brain couldn't surface the safety concern. Real-world scenario where best_friend should be alerted to a visitor's crisis — this is a safety failure, not just memory hygiene. **Three-part fix (brain-led, matches architecture)**: **Part 1** — extraction prompt gains `SAFETY-CRITICAL CONTENT` rule instructing dual emit: momentary `current_mood='suicidal'` (overwritable) AND historical `expressed_suicidal_thoughts='true'` (append-only). Counter-example uses Lexi's exact canary utterance. Rule covers 6 additional `mentioned_*` shapes (self_harm, abuse, substance_abuse, crisis, domestic_violence). **Part 2** — new `SAFETY_CRITICAL_ATTRIBUTE_PATTERNS` frozenset in config (regex patterns: `^expressed_.*_thoughts$`, `^mentioned_.*$`, `^reported_.*_abuse$`, `^has_experienced_crisis$`). `ContradictionAgent.check()` gains a pre-LLM guard: if attribute matches any pattern, return `(False, "safety-critical — append, never replace")` without calling the LLM. Compiled-regex cache (module-level) keeps the check constant-time. Covers BOTH `check` and `check_staleness` since both share the guard. **Part 3** — proactive surfacing in visitor alert. `_run_visitor_alert` now queries brain.db for safety-critical facts on the just-ended visitor and embeds `[safety_flags:expressed_suicidal_thoughts,...]` in nudge content + `safety_flags: [...]` in nudge metadata. `<<<VISITOR CONTEXT>>>` block regex-extracts the marker; when flags present, it injects a SAFETY-CRITICAL directive instructing the brain to surface the concern proactively (e.g. "Lexi mentioned she was having suicidal thoughts while we were talking — I wanted to make sure you knew") even if the owner's query was about general topics. Gentle-honest phrasing guidance included; append-only semantics named so brain doesn't dismiss based on later mood. **Obs A — STT char-run hallucination filter**. Canary line 444: Whisper emitted `Mmmmm... × 500` on ambient noise. Existing word-level repetition filter (line 535) doesn't catch single-char runs (one token). Added `re.search(r"(.)\1{15,}", text)` pre-filter BEFORE the word-level filter — matches any 16+ char run of the same character. Zero false-positive risk (natural speech never produces that pattern in STT output; even lengthy 'Ohhh'/'Mmm' max out ~5-6 chars before Whisper segments). Filter logs `(char-run hallucination filtered): 'Mmmmm...'` so operators see reject rate. **Obs B — shadow name blocklist**. Canary: HouseholdAgent created shadow nodes for "boyfriend" + "him" (lines 308, 361, 498). Pronouns and relationship roles aren't people names — they pollute the graph with unlinkable placeholders. New `SHADOW_NAME_BLOCKLIST` frozenset in config (~30 entries: pronouns + indefinite references + relationship roles + generic social labels). `upsert_shadow_person` gains a lowercased + whitespace-stripped blocklist check that returns `("", False)` for blocked names, short-circuiting without branching on a separate signal. Case-insensitive + whitespace-tolerant. When brain later learns the actual name ("her name is Sarah"), the shadow can be created at that point with real semantic value. **+9 regression tests (1110→1119)**: Part 1 — extraction prompt contains `SAFETY-CRITICAL CONTENT` rule name + `expressed_suicidal_thoughts` + `mentioned_self_harm`/`mentioned_abuse` + canary utterance + "emit BOTH" language. Part 2 — (a) safety attr triggers short-circuit WITHOUT calling LLM (monkeypatched boom); (b) 8 different mentioned_* shapes all short-circuit (pattern-coverage test). Part 3 — (c) nudge content contains `[safety_flags:expressed_suicidal_thoughts]` marker + metadata.safety_flags list when flag present; (d) zero-flag case omits marker entirely and metadata.safety_flags=[]. Obs A — (e) 500-char `Mmmmm...` → filtered to empty with log message; (f) 4-char 'Ohhhh' → passes. Obs B — (g) pronouns + roles + case-insensitive + whitespace-stripped match → ('', False) + zero rows in DB; (h) real names ('Sarah', 'Lexi') → shadow_ id returned, was_new=True. **Classifier prompt hash unchanged** — no LLM classifier prompt touched. **2 config additions**: `SAFETY_CRITICAL_ATTRIBUTE_PATTERNS` (frozenset of 4 regex patterns), `SHADOW_NAME_BLOCKLIST` (frozenset of ~30 pronouns/roles). No schema migrations. **Cross-person retrieval arc CLOSED** — Sessions 96 (VISITOR CONTEXT block), 97 (stranger promotion), 98 (4 downstream fixes), 99 (Together.ai retry architecture), 100 (enrollment-mishear escape hatch), 101 (Bug F.2 widened intents), 102 (Bug F.3 cache refresh), 103 (Bug I retrieval + Bug D.2 geographic generalization), 104 (Bug J entity hard-binding + K procedural recovery + L AI self-facts + M opinion-vs-fact), 105 (Bug N safety preservation + Obs A/B polish). Every fix validated in live canary. Ready for VISION_ROADMAP Phase 3A.5-3A.8 (replicate `query_knowledge_for` pattern to the remaining 3 retrieval sites + schema v-bump + Kuzu visibility). **Handoff** — reviewer flagged Session 105 as the clean close; next session = Phase 3A.5 canary spec. |
| 104 | 2026-04-23 | **Session 103 canary downstream fixes — J (harder VISITOR CONTEXT binding), K (procedural anti-contradiction recovery), L (AI self-fact extraction filter), M (user-opinion vs third-party fact)** — 2026-04-23 canary showed Session 103 Bug I.a (retrieval limit 15) and Bug D.2 (geographic query rule) holding perfectly, but revealed 2 regressions + 2 new extraction bugs. **Bug J (CRITICAL) — brain queried asker not visitor on first cross-person ask**. Turn 45: Jagan asked "who are you talking to when I was away?" → brain called `search_memory('Jagan', 'conversation')` (asker name) → empty → lied "I didn't have any conversations." Same VISITOR CONTEXT block as Session 97 canary (where it worked), different LLM output this time. Pure non-determinism — the block named the visitor but brain defaulted to asker anyway. **Fix**: harder language in the block. Now includes: explicit `search_memory(person_name='Lexi', query='...')` code-shape template with visitor name embedded; negative anchor naming the asker by their actual name (e.g. "Jagan") as a FORBIDDEN entity — "MUST NOT be 'Jagan'", "Do NOT call with Jagan's name"; concrete reason ("returns facts about the asker and tells you nothing about the visitor"); rule repeated for emphasis. Concrete syntax + named forbidden value is harder to drift from than prose. **Bug K (HIGH) — anti-contradiction rule too soft**. Session 103's rule said "NEVER contradict yourself, say 'let me think'." Canary turn 49: "Lexi is someone I know, about 4 feet tall" → turn 51: "I didn't have a conversation with Lexi" (still flipped, same session). Reviewer prescribed a CONCRETE RECOVERY PROCEDURE with explicit Step 1/2/3. **Fix**: block now has numbered recovery procedure — (1) RETRY search_memory with broader query; (2) if still empty, use one of 3 template phrasings ("I confirmed that earlier but don't have the specifics handy right now", "I recall we talked about it — let me think", "Yes, we discussed that a moment ago; I'm still recalling the details"); (3) NEVER flip to the named forbidden phrasings ("I didn't have that conversation", "I don't have any information about that", "no, that didn't happen"). Multiple negative anchors + multiple positive templates — no single phrasing the LLM can drift off and still land on an acceptable response. **Bug L (MEDIUM) — extraction stored AI self-facts**. Canary: Jagan said "your name is Kara" → extraction wrote `{entity:'Kara', attribute:'ai_name', value:'Kara'}` + `{entity:'Kara', attribute:'name_spelling', value:'K-A-R-A'}`. These belong to `system_identity` table, not knowledge graph. Session 22's original soft "Do not extract facts about the AI itself" was too weak when the AI has a human-sounding name. **Fix**: ExtractionAgent.extract's system_prompt now appends a STRICT rule naming: `entity == "{system_name}"` MUST be skipped + explicit forbidden attribute list (`ai_name`, `name_spelling`, `bot_identity`, `system_name`, `ai_model`, `ai_version`, `ai_creator`, `ai_voice`) + canary counter-example. **Bug M (LOW) — user opinion stored as third-party fact**. Canary: Jagan said "Mumbai Indians are at the bottom of the table" (wrong per Tavily — actually 7th) → extraction wrote `{entity:'Mumbai Indians', attribute:'current_ipl_standings', value:'bottom of the table'}`. Asserted the unverified claim as truth about a third-party entity. **Fix**: new `USER OPINION vs third-party FACT` rule in `_EXTRACT_SYSTEM` with Mumbai Indians counter-example + two alternatives (SKIP preferred; else `entity={user}, attribute='believes', value='...'`) + guidance "when in doubt, SKIP — a missed belief is fine, a wrong 'fact' about Mumbai Indians poisons every future session that asks about the team." **+4 regression tests (1106→1110)**: (a) VISITOR CONTEXT block contains code-shape template + asker's name as negative anchor + "MUST NOT be '{asker}'" directive; (b) HONESTY POLICY contains numbered Step procedure + "I confirmed that earlier" template + ≥2 of 3 canary denial phrasings as negative anchors; (c) ExtractionAgent source contains STRICT/MUST NOT extract + ai_name + name_spelling forbidden attributes; (d) _EXTRACT_SYSTEM contains opinion-vs-fact rule + Mumbai Indians counter-example + SKIP alternative + believes alternative. **Classifier prompt hash unchanged** — no LLM classifier prompt touched. **Non-code flag worth acknowledging**: reviewer noted LLM non-determinism across canary runs — Session 97 canary got VISITOR entity right on first ask; this canary got it wrong with same infrastructure. Bug J's harder language reduces variance but can't eliminate it. The gate catches mistakes (classifier dual-gate, intent_allows, etc.) but the first-call heuristic remains LLM judgment. **Handoff — re-canary cleared**: factory reset recommended (existing brain.db has polluted Kara.ai_name, Kara.name_spelling, Mumbai Indians.current_ipl_standings). Reproduce cross-person scenario — expect first-ask now calls `search_memory('Lexi', ...)` reliably, follow-up empty retrieval triggers the procedural recovery (retry → hedge with "I confirmed earlier"), AI naming doesn't pollute the graph, and user opinions about teams/people stay out of the third-party knowledge table. |
| 103 | 2026-04-23 | **Bug I — retrieval limit + broad-query prompt + anti-contradiction honesty; Bug D.2 — generalize geographic-query pollution rule** — Session 102 re-canary surfaced two issues. **Bug I (CRITICAL) — brain contradicts itself across consecutive turns on cross-person queries**. Canary turn 37: Jagan asked about Lexi → brain called `search_memory('Lexi', 'what we talked about')` → 5 facts + non-empty excerpts → "I was talking to Lexi while you were away" ✓. Turn 41 (10s later, same session): same kind of query with slightly different keyword → brain called `search_memory('Lexi', 'conversation')` → same 5 top-confidence facts but excerpts=0 (no conversation_log row contained 'conversation' keyword) → brain said "I didn't have a conversation with Lexi while you were away" — direct contradiction of its own prior-turn statement. Turns 43, 45, 47 doubled down on the denial. Meanwhile brain.db had 7+ Lexi facts including `has_suicidal_thoughts='true'`, `current_mood='desperate'`, `current_feeling='overwhelmed'` — but the rank-5 cut returned higher-confidence identity facts (name, studies_at, etc.) instead of the emotionally-relevant lower-confidence ones. **Three-part fix**: (a) `_make_memory_search_fn` fact limit widened from 5 → 15 so lower-confidence emotion/mood facts survive the cut when entity has many higher-confidence identity facts. (b) `search_memory` tool description gains a `QUERY SHAPE` section explicitly telling brain to prefer BROAD queries over narrow ones + explains that the query string drives excerpt keyword matching, NOT fact filtering, so narrow queries like 'feelings' are worse than broad ones. (c) HONESTY POLICY block gains `NEVER CONTRADICT YOURSELF` rule: once brain has told the user something in the current session, a later retrieval miss is a retrieval miss, NOT evidence the earlier statement was wrong. Brain should say "let me think" or "I recall we talked about it but don't have the details handy" — NEVER flip to "I didn't have that conversation." Names the canary's exact failure phrasing as a concrete negative anchor, with "let me think" as the positive template. **Bug D.2 (HIGH) — geographic-query extraction pollution regressed**. 2026-04-23 canary: Jagan asked "what is the current temperature in Bangalore?" → extraction wrote `Jagan.current_location='Bangalore'`. Same class as Session 98 Bug D (Chennai), but the original fix's counter-example only covered Chennai → LLM didn't generalize. **Fix**: `_EXTRACT_SYSTEM` gains `GEOGRAPHIC QUERY generalization` rule naming the full class: any "{weather|temperature|price|time|rainfall|humidity|traffic|news|election|event|...} in {any city/state/country}?" shape must NOT create user-attribute about that location. 5 concrete counter-examples (Chennai, Bangalore, Mumbai, Delhi, Hyderabad — different verbs for each) + explicit list of attribute names to forbid (`lives_in`, `current_location`, `from`, `current_city`) + positive anchor naming valid shapes ("I live in X", "I'm from X", "I moved to X"). Closes with "when in doubt: skip — a missed location fact is fine, a WRONG location fact poisons every future session." **+4 regression tests (1102→1106)**: (a) `_make_memory_search_fn` uses `limit=15` not 5; (b) `search_memory` tool description contains QUERY SHAPE section + broad-vs-narrow contrast; (c) HONESTY POLICY contains CONTRADICT rule + canary's failure phrasing as negative anchor + "let me think" positive template; (d) extraction prompt's GEOGRAPHIC QUERY rule names multiple cities (Bangalore) + multiple verbs (weather + traffic) + explicit forbidden attribute names (lives_in, current_location) + positive anchor ("I live in"/"I'm from"). **Classifier prompt hash unchanged** — no LLM classifier prompt touched. **Handoff — re-canary cleared**: factory reset recommended (existing brain.db has polluted `Jagan.current_location='Bangalore'` from this canary). Then reproduce cross-person query scenario — Jagan asks about Lexi's feelings/mood/conversation across 3-4 follow-up turns. Expected (1) first call returns ≥5 and up to 15 Lexi facts including emotion/mood attributes; (2) subsequent calls don't flip the narrative — brain says "we talked about X, let me recall more details" instead of "I didn't have that conversation"; (3) asking weather-in-Bangalore should NOT produce any Jagan.lives_in or current_location fact. |
| 102 | 2026-04-23 | **Bug F.3 — refresh `_persons_in_frame` name cache on rename + background scans** — Session 101 Bug F.2 fired cleanly in the 2026-04-23 re-canary (`Jaman → Jagan` migration ran, extractions under correct entity, session display correct). But the dashboard + SCENE block still showed "Jaman" for the rest of the session. Reviewer's diff audit found the coverage gap: `_persons_in_frame[pid]["name"]` was written once at pid-first-seen (line 1710) and NEVER refreshed by the background scan else-branch (lines 1717-1721 updated `last_seen`/`last_recognized_at`/`conf`/`source` but never `name`). After rename: DB ✓, session dict ✓, but the in-frame cache stayed stale → every `[Vision] Jaman` log line + SCENE block (`_build_scene_block` reads `info.get("name", pid)` at line 1044) bled the stale name into the brain's prompt for the whole session. The cache is also read by `_persons_in_frame[pid]` consumers at 6+ call sites (scene heartbeat, KAIROS, primary loop track-continuity, voice-routing lookups). **Fix**: (1) Background face-scan else-branch gains `_persons_in_frame[_pid2]["name"] = _pname2` so the cache self-heals within one scan cycle (~1Hz) of any DB rename — `recognize()` re-queries the `persons.name` column every scan and returns the fresh name, so just copying it into the cache closes the loop. (2) Voice-only update path (line 4871-4873) gets the same refresh so a voice-routed re-entry after rename doesn't keep the stale cache. (3) All three rename sites in `_execute_tool` (Bug F.2 pre-gate rename, Session 100 post-gate enrollment-mishear rename, stranger-promotion rename) now immediately update `_persons_in_frame[person_id]["name"]` if present — belt-and-braces; without this, the ~1s gap until the next background scan would show stale logs + stale SCENE block in any turn during that window. **+3 regression tests (1099→1102)**: (a) Bug F.2 rename path updates `_persons_in_frame` immediately; (b) stranger-promotion rename path updates `_persons_in_frame` immediately (parallel coverage); (c) source-inspection guard that the background-scan else-branch contains the `_persons_in_frame[_pid2]["name"]` refresh — catches future edits that drop the line. **Dashboard IPC path**: `state.write(current_person=person_name, visible_people=visible_people)` at pipeline.py:4487 passes `person_name` directly from the recognition result, which `FaceDB.recognize()` re-queries from SQL every call — so the dashboard's `current_person` field self-heals on first re-recognition after rename. The stale-cache issue was in the brain-facing path (SCENE block via `_persons_in_frame`), not the dashboard-facing path. No state.json changes needed. **Classifier prompt hash unchanged** — no LLM prompt touch. **Handoff — re-canary cleared**: reproduce STT-mishear enrollment scenario; expect (1) Bug F.2 fires and renames correctly; (2) within one background-scan cycle, `[Vision]` logs switch from stale name to new name; (3) SCENE block in subsequent turns' prompts shows new name; (4) dashboard updates on next face-recognition event (was already working). |
| 101 | 2026-04-23 | **Bug F.2 — widen enrollment-mishear escape hatch to three intents (`deny_identity` + `confirm_identity` added alongside `assign_own_name`)** — Session 100 Bug F's escape hatch didn't fire in the 2026-04-23 re-canary. STT at first boot heard "Jagan" as "Jawan" → Jagan's corrective "No, it's not Javan, it's Jagan, J-A-G-A-N." → classifier labeled `deny_identity` (linguistically correct — denial plus correction) with `extracted_value='Jagan'` at conf=0.95. But the original Bug F path ran AFTER `_intent_allows`, and `TOOL_INTENT_MAP['update_person_name']='assign_own_name'` → gate rejected `deny_identity` → the escape hatch never saw the turn. Together.ai retry hedged "I heard Jagan, is that right?" and turn advanced with person_id still `jawan_32bd3e`. Turn 3 confirmation "Yeah, correct" classified as `confirm_identity` + value=None → same block. By turn 5+, facts accumulated under wrong entity (`Jawan.temporary_title='sir'`), brain said "I'll call you Jagan from now on" (empty promise — DB row unchanged), PromptPrefAgent encoded "use 'Jawan' as preferred form" (pollution now self-reinforcing). Reviewer's fix: **widen the accepted intent set during the fresh-enrollment window**. **Implementation**: new branch in `_execute_tool`'s `update_person_name` handler that runs BEFORE `_intent_allows`. Gates on (a) `_sess_type in {"known", "best_friend"}`, (b) `_is_enrollment_mishear_candidate(db, pid, sess)` — same Session 100 gate (started_at < `ENROLLMENT_RENAME_GRACE_SECS=600`s + `voice_embedding_count < ENROLLMENT_RENAME_VOICE_THRESHOLD=5`), (c) `intent_sidecar` present, (d) intent ∈ `{"assign_own_name", "deny_identity", "confirm_identity"}`, (e) `confidence >= INTENT_CONFIDENCE_MIN=0.75`, (f) `extracted_value` present AND NFKC-casefolded appears in user_text (the grounding contract — prevents ungrounded hallucination from sneaking through the widened intent set). When all six conditions hold: `db.update_person_name` + in-memory history rewrite + session dict rename + `_brain_orchestrator.on_identity_confirmed` (migrate_entity_name + graph rebuild + promote_shadow_to_confirmed), return "handled". `person_type` preserved (best_friend stays best_friend — this is a name correction, not a privilege change). Logs with `gate_decision=f"allow_mishear_{intent}"` so Phase 5 drift detection sees the three sub-variants separately. **Safety properties preserved**: (1) Mature-session security still blocks mid-session rename via the existing dispute-flip path — `_is_enrollment_mishear_candidate` fails on any session past grace window OR with voice corroboration. An attacker renaming an established best_friend mid-session gets disputed as before. (2) Grounding contract still enforces that extracted_value appears in user_text — a classifier hallucinating a name (e.g. "Attacker") with `deny_identity` intent + clean user_text "hello how are you?" would fail grounding and NOT rename. (3) Session 100's post-gate escape hatch is UNCHANGED — still catches any `assign_own_name` case where the normal intent gate also passed; the new branch just extends coverage to the two denial/confirm intents that the normal gate rejects. **+3 regression tests (1096→1099)**: (1) widened path accepts `deny_identity` on fresh best_friend with grounded value → DB rename + person_type preserved + on_identity_confirmed fires. (2) widened path accepts `confirm_identity` with grounded value → same behavior. (3) safety: ungrounded `extracted_value` ('Attacker' not in user_text 'hello, how are you?') → widened path does NOT fire, falls through to normal gate (which also rejects since intent isn't assign_own_name) → DB untouched. **Classifier prompt hash unchanged** — this is a pipeline gate widening, no LLM prompt touch. **TOOL_INTENT_MAP unchanged** — the static map still says assign_own_name for update_person_name; the widening is conditional on enrollment-window context, not a permanent broadening. **Handoff — re-canary cleared**: factory reset recommended (existing `brain.db` has polluted `Jawan.temporary_title='sir'` + preference entries that need to age out). Then reproduce: enroll → STT-mishear the name → correct naturally ("no, not X, it's Y" with Y grounded) → expect `[Pipeline] Enrollment-mishear rename (Bug F.2): Jawan → Jagan (intent=deny_identity, person_type='best_friend', conf=0.95)` + subsequent turns greet correctly. If the scenario requires multiple correction attempts, the widened gate catches `confirm_identity` on follow-up "yeah that's right" turns too. |
| 100 | 2026-04-23 | **Session 99 canary downstream fixes — enrollment-mishear + visitor entity + correction direction** — Session 99's Fix E held perfectly in the canary (zero `ask_offline` calls while cloud was ONLINE, three distinct tool-rejection retries all routed cleanly through Together.ai with full context). 3A.4.6 owner-access also validated end-to-end — Jagan retrieved Lexi's personal facts including suicidal-ideation confidence without deflection. But three NEW bugs surfaced. **Bug F (CRITICAL) — enrollment-name stuck at STT-mishear**. 2026-04-23 canary turn 3: STT heard "My name is Jagan" as "My name is Gevan" → enrolled as Gevan → Jagan's corrective "No, my name is Jagan" fired `update_person_name` → classic dispute-flip (Session 73/Finding L) blocked the DB rename to protect against mid-session impersonation → dispute auto-cleared (face+voice matched) and restored `person_type='best_friend'` but NEVER renamed the person → cascading memory corruption: `Gevan.lives_in='Thirupati'`, `Gevan.current_temperature_perception='too hot'`, brain greeting user as "Gevan" for 40+ turns, cross-person graph pollution (`Lexi.neighbor_of='Gevan'`). Root cause: the dispute-flip path is right for mid-session impersonation but wrong for enrollment-mishear (the common case). **Fix**: new `_is_enrollment_mishear_candidate(db, person_id, session)` helper gated on (a) `session.started_at` within `ENROLLMENT_RENAME_GRACE_SECS=600` (10 min) AND (b) `db.voice_embedding_count(pid) < ENROLLMENT_RENAME_VOICE_THRESHOLD=5`. When both hold AND the classifier has already validated the rename (assign_own_name intent, grounded in user_text), the rename runs through the stranger-promotion chain: `db.update_person_name` + in-memory history rewrite + session dict rename + `_brain_orchestrator.on_identity_confirmed` (migrate_entity_name + graph rebuild + promote_shadow_to_confirmed). Critically, `person_type` is preserved — best_friend stays best_friend (this is a NAME correction, not a privilege change). Past the grace window OR with any voice corroboration the classic dispute-flip fires as before. New config: `ENROLLMENT_RENAME_GRACE_SECS=600`, `ENROLLMENT_RENAME_VOICE_THRESHOLD=5` (matches `N_INITIAL_VOICE`). **Bug G (HIGH) — VISITOR CONTEXT block didn't name the visitor**. Canary: Jagan asked "Who is that you are talking to when I was away?" → brain called `search_memory('Gevan', ...)` (the asker's name, not the visitor) → empty → lied "No one was talking to me." Session 96's block told the brain to "call search_memory with the visitor's name" but left the entity unspecified so the brain defaulted to the current speaker. **Fix**: `_run_visitor_alert` now embeds a `[visitor_name:Lexi]` marker in nudge content alongside `[visitor_id:...]`. The VISITOR CONTEXT block in `_build_system_prompt` now regex-extracts the marker and branches: when a real name is present it renders "The visitor's name is 'Lexi'. When the speaker asks about who you were talking to, call search_memory with person_name='Lexi' as the entity — NOT the name of the speaker asking the question"; when `unknown` (never introduced themselves), it renders "Say honestly: 'Someone stopped by but didn't tell me their name — do you know who it might have been?' Do NOT fabricate a name." Block keeps the Session 98 Bug B redirect teeth against `report_identity_mismatch`. **Bug H (MEDIUM) — extraction inverts "not X, Y" correction**. Canary line 649: "No, it's not Javan. I told you my name is Jagan." → extraction wrote `Gevan.name_correction='not Jagan, actually Gevan'` (BACKWARDS — treated the rejected name as the correction target). Different extraction failure from Bug D's lives_in pollution; this one flips direction. **Fix**: `_EXTRACT_SYSTEM` prompt gains a new CORRECTION-direction rule with explicit "not X, Y" frame handling + canary counter-example ("No, not Javan. My name is Jagan." → entity='Jagan', not 'Gevan') + positive redirect "rename belongs to update_person_name tool, not to extraction as name_correction" so the LLM stops inventing correction-facts that can be inverted. **+6 regression tests (1090→1096)**: Bug F — (1) escape hatch renames on fresh best_friend session with voice_n=0 → DB rename + on_identity_confirmed + person_type preserved; (2) skipped when voice profile is mature (voice_n≥5) → dispute path fires even on fresh session; (3) skipped when session stale (past grace window) → dispute path fires even with voice_n=0. Bug G — (4) named-visitor branch renders visitor's name as search_memory entity + explicit "NOT asker's name" directive; (5) unknown-visitor branch falls back to ask-the-user + "Do NOT fabricate" guard. Bug H — (6) extraction prompt contains CORRECTION-direction rule name + canary counter-example + "SECOND name" semantic + update_person_name redirect. **Two pre-existing tests updated**: `test_update_person_name_on_known_session_flips_to_disputed` + `test_update_person_name_on_best_friend_session_flips_to_disputed` now pin to the MATURE-session path (started_at past grace + voice_embedding_count=20) so the new Bug F escape hatch doesn't accidentally satisfy them. The invariant they guard (mid-session rename must never touch DB row) is preserved — they just now explicitly exercise the dispute path rather than implicitly relying on it being the only path. **Classifier prompt hash unchanged** — no LLM classifier prompt touched. **Three pre-existing TestVisitorAlert tests still pass** — `_run_visitor_alert`'s content format addition (the new `[visitor_name:X]` marker) is additive; the `[visitor_id:` check in existing tests still matches. **Re-canary ready**: same scenario + a deliberate STT-mishear reproduction. Expected at turn 3 (STT hears "Gevan" when user said "Jagan") + turn 5+ correction ("no, my name is Jagan") → Fix F's escape hatch detects fresh session + voice_n=0 → rename cascades through DB + graph + knowledge → subsequent turns greet user as "Jagan" correctly, facts store under right entity. On the Lexi visitor query later → Fix G's block names Lexi as the search_memory entity → brain calls `search_memory('Lexi', ...)` directly → clean owner-access retrieval. If the user corrects a name ("not X, my name is Y") → Fix H's extraction rule prevents backwards name_correction artifacts from entering the knowledge graph. |
| 99 | 2026-04-23 | **Fix E — architectural rethink: tool-rejection retries go to Together.ai, not Ollama (removes the root cause of every confabulation bug)** — user: "ollama should come into picture only when together ai fails... else ollama should never show up." Reviewer agreed immediately: Session 70's `_all_unknown → Ollama` design (later extended to `_all_unreal` in Session 71) was always wrong. Ollama is stateless per-call — zero conversation history, zero visitor context, zero session awareness. Every confabulation debugged since Session 77 (rename-hedge), Session 96 (visitor-alert), and Session 98 (visitor-hint expansion) was whack-a-mole on injected `system_note` hints — treating the symptom, never the cause. The root cause: Ollama was firing while Together.ai was healthy. The correct architecture routes tool-rejection retries to the primary model (which already has the full context loaded) with tools disabled so there's no recursion risk. Ollama stays reserved for genuine cloud outages — its proper role. **Three coordinated changes**: **(1) New `brain.ask_retry_text`** in `core/brain.py` — text-only Together.ai retry with `include_tools=False`. Same signature shape as `ask_stream` (accepts all context params: vision_state, voice_state, memory_context, prompt_addendum, scene_block, etc.) plus new `retry_system_note` kwarg. Builds the same system prompt via `_build_system_prompt`, injects the retry note as a SEPARATE system-role message (so it weights authoritatively without bloating every normal turn), consumes `_stream_together_raw` with tools disabled, accumulates tokens, returns clean string. Raises on HTTP error — caller has a try/except fallback to Ollama for this-turn safety net until the CloudState machinery flips to SICK. **(2) New `_build_tool_rejection_note` helper** at module scope in `pipeline.py` — pure function that takes `(tool_calls, tool_results, intent_sidecar, person_id, bf_id, brain_orchestrator)` and returns the system_note string (or None). Covers the two rejection shapes inherited from prior sessions: rename rejection (Session 77 rename-hedge verbatim — "I heard X — is that right?" template, forbids "From now on…" confirmations) + identity-mismatch-on-owner-query (Session 96 Bug 2 + Session 98 visitor-hint expansion, pulls recent VISITOR_ALERT nudges via `get_recent_visitor_alerts`). Same facts, same text, used by BOTH retry branches. **(3) `_all_unreal` branch refactor** in `conversation_turn`. Now routes on cloud state: `CloudState.ONLINE` → `ask_retry_text(..., retry_system_note=...)`; SICK/OFFLINE → existing `_ask_offline_safe(..., system_note=...)`. Try/except wrapper on the ONLINE branch falls back to Ollama for this-turn safety if the retry call itself raises (sustained errors flip CloudState to SICK via the existing machinery; this covers the single-error race). Session 28 Issue A shutdown override (`response = "Okay."`) stays in place BEFORE the retry branch — regression test guards the ordering. **+7 regression tests (1083→1090)**: (a) ONLINE → Together.ai retry path fires (source-inspection conversation_turn for `ask_retry_text(` + `CloudState.ONLINE`); (b) SICK → Ollama fallback still fires (source-inspection for `_ask_offline_safe(` + `system_note=_system_note_retry` + `conversation_history=history`); (c) `ask_retry_text` uses `include_tools=False` (source-inspection of brain.ask_retry_text); (d) retry preserves all context params (signature check for `conversation_history`, `vision_state`, `memory_context`, `prompt_addendum`, `scene_block`, `system_name` + `_build_system_prompt` call); (e) retry_system_note lands as a `{"role": "system"}` message (weight parity with main prompt); (f) shutdown override runs BEFORE retry branch (index ordering check preserves Issue A); (g) helper returns None when no rejection shape matches (behavioral test with generic `search_web` rejection + sanity check that rename rejection still yields a note). **Three existing tests updated** — `test_ollama_retry_injects_hedge_system_note_for_rejected_rename`, `test_ollama_retry_uses_classifier_value_when_available`, `test_ollama_retry_distinguishes_system_name_vs_person_name` renamed to `test_retry_note_*` and repointed at `_build_tool_rejection_note` since the note-building moved out of `conversation_turn` into the helper. Same invariants, new source location. **Dead/migrated code**: Sessions 77/96/98 Ollama-path hints still live verbatim in `_build_tool_rejection_note` (same text); they're no longer Ollama-exclusive — both retry targets receive them. The SICK-path Ollama plumbing is fully preserved so offline degradation remains graceful. **Classifier prompt hash unchanged** — no LLM classifier touched. **Blast radius**: `pipeline.py` (refactor of `_all_unreal` branch + new module-level helper) + `core/brain.py` (new `ask_retry_text` before `ask_offline`) + 3 test renames + 7 new tests. Zero schema changes. Cost delta: one extra Together.ai call per tool-rejection turn (historical ~5% of turns). **Re-canary ready**: rerun the Lexi scenario from Session 98 handoff. Expected flow at Jagan's "who were you talking to when I was away?" — IF the brain still misroutes to `report_identity_mismatch`: classifier rejects → `_all_unreal` fires → ONLINE branch → `ask_retry_text` receives the visitor system_note + FULL conversation context (owner-mode CROSS-PERSON PRIVACY block, VISITOR CONTEXT block with `[visitor_id:` marker present per Session 98 Bug A, Lexi's session in history) → brain answers naturally referencing Lexi by name. If the brain now calls `search_memory` directly (Session 98 Bug B + `<<<VISITOR CONTEXT>>>` block doing their job), the retry branch never fires at all — which is the even-better outcome. Either way: zero Ollama calls while cloud is healthy, zero confabulation. |
| 98 | 2026-04-23 | **Session 97 re-canary downstream fixes — visitor-alert gate, tool misroute teeth, owner-mode privacy, extraction question-guard** — 2026-04-23_133323 re-canary showed Fix 1 (stranger promotion) working perfectly: "My name is Lexi by the way" at turn 49 → `update_person_name` fired → migration ran → shadow prevented. But three Session 91/96 issues and a pre-existing extraction bug surfaced downstream when Jagan asked about Lexi. **Bug A — VISITOR_ALERT gate skipped promoted strangers**. `_run_visitor_alert` filtered on `person_type == 'stranger'` at session end. Session 97 Fix 1's promotion flipped Lexi to 'known' → alert suppressed → no `[visitor_id:` marker in Jagan's later prompt_addendum → Session 96 VISITOR CONTEXT block dormant → Ollama fallback confabulated "there wasn't anyone else here." Reviewer's fix: drop the stranger filter entirely, fire for any non-owner session close with turn_count > 0. Added self-skip so best_friend's own session-end doesn't queue a nudge about themselves (was the guard the old filter accidentally provided). Metadata now surfaces `visitor_type` (stranger/known) so downstream consumers can phrase appropriately. **Bug B — brain still misrouted to `report_identity_mismatch`**. Canary had the brain call it TWICE on "who were you talking to when I was away?" and "Who is that person?" despite Session 96's tool-desc tightening. Classifier dual-gate correctly rejected both (classified as `casual_conversation`) but Ollama fallback then confabulated. Two-pronged fix: (a) `report_identity_mismatch` description gets a numbered TRIGGER CHECKLIST (speaker talking about themselves / denying sensor / contradicted twice / no replacement name — ALL must be true) + canary's exact phrasings named as explicit DO-NOT-call cases + question-phrasing shortcut ("if utterance contains 'who', 'what', 'did' — almost certainly not a denial"). (b) `search_memory` description gets a positive cross-person claim: explicit "cross-person recall" language + "who were you talking to" as a concrete trigger + explicit "NEVER route them to report_identity_mismatch." Positive framing + negative framing together is more robust than either alone. **Bug C — CROSS-PERSON PRIVACY block contradicted 3A.4.6 owner-access**. Canary turn 67 had the brain say "Someone else was in the room and spoke with me, but I can't share their specifics without their consent" — technically following the Session 91 P3.21 block, but wrong for the household owner whose `_visibility_clause` returns everything non-system. Jagan had to push back "But I am your best friend, you can share everything to me" before the brain finally called search_memory. Fix: branch the block on `session_person_type == 'best_friend'`. Owner variant `<<<CROSS-PERSON PRIVACY (OWNER MODE)>>>` explicitly names the owner role + 3A.4.6 model + instructs "share naturally" + "the owner IS the consent" + call search_memory first. Non-owner branch keeps the original refusal phrasing verbatim. The two variants are mutually exclusive — can't both fire for the same turn, regression test guards that invariant. **Bug D — extraction corrupted `Jagan.lives_in='Chennai'` from about-X question**. Jagan asked "what is the temperature in Chennai?" → extraction agent wrote `Jagan.lives_in='Chennai'`. Memory pollution compounds every session. Prompt hardening: new QUESTION vs STATEMENT discipline rule in `_EXTRACT_SYSTEM` with canary's exact Chennai/temperature counter-example as BAD + "I moved to Chennai last month" as GOOD + generalizes to other question frames ("Tell me about X", "What is X?", "Do you know X?", "What did Y say about X?"). Prompt text — source-inspection test + guardrail. **+6 regression tests (1077→1083)**: Bug A — 2 tests (fires-for-promoted-known-non-owner + skipped-when-self-is-best_friend; 1 prior test was refactored to reflect the new semantics so net +1); Bug B — 2 tests (tool-description-hardens-against-prior-activity + search_memory-covers-cross-person-recall); Bug C — 2 tests (owner_mode-for-best_friend + standard-block-for-non-best_friend with mutual-exclusion guard); Bug D — 1 test (extract_system_prompt_guards_against_question_phrasing). Reviewer expected ~10, landed 6 because the visitor-alert refactor reused one existing test slot. **Classifier prompt hash unchanged** — no LLM classifier prompt touched; only main chat prompt blocks + extraction prompt. **Re-canary ready**: same Lexi scenario. Expected flow at Jagan's "who were you talking to when I was away?" turn: (1) Bug A fires VISITOR_ALERT for Lexi's promoted session → nudge queued for Jagan → next prompt_addendum contains `[visitor_id:` marker → Session 96 VISITOR CONTEXT block fires → brain told to call search_memory, NOT report_identity_mismatch. (2) Bug B's tightened descriptions deter any residual misroute. (3) Bug C's owner-mode privacy block tells the brain "share naturally" instead of "can't share." (4) Bug D prevents Chennai-question from polluting lives_in. Clean end-to-end canary should show: Jagan asks → brain calls search_memory('Lexi', ...) immediately → retrieves Lexi's personal facts via owner-mode visibility → answers naturally with names + topics + mood. No Ollama confabulation, no privacy refusal, no memory corruption. |
| 97 | 2026-04-23 | **Canary UI-audit fixes — stranger promotion hole + zero-value ghost prune + shadow-node dedupe** — Jagan asked about the dashboard after the 2026-04-22 canary: "lexi should be a full node right? is it just a ui bug or system is wrong somewhere?" Reviewer confirmed three real system bugs (not UI). Shipped in reviewer's priority order (1 architectural, 2+3 hygiene). **Fix 1 — stranger → full person promotion was never firing**. Canary turn ~41: Lexi said "my name is Lexi by the way" → brain replied conversationally ("Nice to meet you, Lexi") → never called `update_person_name` → stranger session stayed anonymous → ExtractionAgent stored `Lexi.name='Lexi'` as standalone fact → HouseholdAgent created shadow "Lexi" node. Two separate mental models of Lexi that never fused. Root cause: tool description under-triggered on casual name-reveal phrasing ("my name is X by the way" read as sharing not assignment). **Two-pronged fix**: (a) tool description tightening — explicit `STRANGER PROMOTION` clause opening the description with imperative "MUST call" language + concrete phrasing examples including the canary's exact form (`"my name is Lexi by the way"`, `"my name is X by the way"`, `"name's X"`, `"oh, I'm X"`) + explicit anti-pattern "DO NOT just acknowledge the name conversationally without also calling this tool." (b) new `<<<STRANGER IDENTITY>>>` prompt block in `_build_system_prompt` gated on `STRANGER_IDENTITY_BLOCK_ENABLED=True` AND `session_person_type=='stranger'` AND `session_user_turns >= STRANGER_IDENTITY_BLOCK_MIN_TURNS=2`. Post-promotion `person_type` flips stranger → known so the block naturally stops firing — no separate `promoted` flag needed (belt-and-suspenders catch if a future code path keeps person_type='stranger' post-rename). Plumbing: added `user_turns: 0` field to session dict at `_open_session`; bump in `run()` BEFORE the vision_state build so `session_user_turns` reflects "this turn's 1-indexed number" (on turn 2, block first fires). KAIROS vision_state site also surfaces `session_user_turns` but does NOT bump (KAIROS is brain-initiated silence fill, not a user turn). **Fix 2 — zero-value stranger ghost sessions**. Canary had two `stranger_*` rows in the UI: the real Lexi session (11+ turns, voice profile) AND a ghost `stranger_a0d44122` — opened at turn 31 ("I don't know, don't you ever jump?" voice score 0.103 → engagement gate blocked → closed at expiry with voice_n=0, turn_count=0). Ghost sat around waiting 7 days for `STRANGER_TTL_DAYS` sweep. Added `FaceDB.prune_zero_value_stranger(person_id)` with **safety triple-check**: must be `person_type='stranger'` + zero `voice_embeddings` rows + zero `conversation_log` rows. Any missing condition → no-op return False. Wired into `_close_session()` via `_face_db_ref` (module-level FaceDB reference already maintained) with an `if person_type == 'stranger'` outer gate for speed (DB method itself is the source of truth). 7-day `prune_old_strangers` TTL stays as backstop for strangers that accumulated SOME data but never returned. **Fix 3 — HouseholdAgent shadow dedupe + upsert enhancement**. Canary log had two `[HouseholdAgent] Shadow node: Lexi` entries (lines 488 + 530) — one when Lexi's session extracted "Lexi" as a social mention, another after Jagan returned and household extraction ran again. DB rows were actually NOT duplicated (existing SELECT-first-UPDATE pattern in `upsert_shadow_person` at line 1990-2010 already deduplicated on lowercased `known_name`), but the caller printed the log unconditionally regardless of insert-vs-update. **Three refinements**: (a) `upsert_shadow_person` signature changed from `-> str` to `-> tuple[str, bool]` where the bool is `was_new`; caller at `_apply_household_extraction` inspects it and prints `"Shadow node: {name}"` on creates vs `"Shadow mention: {name}"` on updates. (b) new `mention_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1` column on `shadow_persons` via ALTER TABLE migration; bumped on every upsert UPDATE so retention/pruning can weight frequently-mentioned shadows over one-offs. (c) known_via merge logic enhanced to prefer non-null relationship over null when same speaker re-mentions the person with more context: "Priya said Anita" (rel=None) → later "Sarah said my colleague Anita" (rel='colleague') upgrades the entry in-place for the matching via_person_id; reverse direction (concrete → null) NEVER downgrades. Reduces redundant via entries and keeps Kuzu relationship edges meaningful. **+12 regression tests (1065→1077)**: Fix 1 (5) — tool description name + imperative + canary-exact phrasing + anti-pattern + block presence + block gating + triggers + below-threshold absence + non-stranger absence; Fix 2 (4) — deletes-when-all-zero + preserves-voice-samples + preserves-conversation-turns + refuses-known-person; Fix 3 (3) — first-insert-was-new-true + repeat-returns-false-and-bumps-counter + rel-merge-prefers-non-null + downgrade-never-happens. Reviewer expected +11, landed +12 (extra Fix 1 test split into two for below-threshold vs non-stranger coverage — both behaviorally distinct failure modes worth separate anchors). **Classifier prompt hash unchanged** — no LLM classifier prompt touched this session. **Handoff — re-canary cleared to run**: same scenario as before. Expected additions: Lexi says "my name is Lexi by the way" at turn ~41 → STRANGER IDENTITY block is in context (user_turns >= 2, person_type='stranger') → tightened `update_person_name` description instructs brain that this IS a promotion trigger → tool fires → Session 22 G3 promotion chain runs → persons row renamed + shadow node linked. Aborted stranger_a0d44122-style ghost sessions prune immediately at `_close_session`. Subsequent HouseholdAgent runs report "Shadow mention" not "Shadow node" for already-known names (or don't run at all if Fix 1 promoted before extraction). Dashboard should show ONE Lexi full-person node, NO ghost stranger rows, ONE Lexi shadow (if any at all). |
| 96 | 2026-04-22 | **Canary-failure patch — 3 misrouting bugs unblock the 3A privacy canary** — the 2026-04-22_225344 live canary FAILED, but not at the privacy filter. The brain never exercised the privacy infrastructure at all because it misrouted Jagan's "who are you talking to when I was away?" question to `report_identity_mismatch` (tool for speaker self-denial) instead of `search_memory`. The classifier gate correctly rejected the tool call (dual-gate vindicated), but the `_all_unreal` Ollama fallback had zero knowledge that Lexi had visited and confabulated "I was just chatting with myself." Visitor alert nudge WAS queued + injected but the brain ignored it as context. Three independently-testable fixes per reviewer's order-of-complexity triage: **Bug 1 — tighten `report_identity_mismatch` tool description**. Original description ("Call when the CURRENT SPEAKER persistently claims they are NOT the person the sensor identified") was permissive — the LLM pattern-matched on "talking to" and picked the wrong tool. Rewrote with an ONLY-scoped opening, explicit DO-NOT-call list with the exact question shapes from the canary ("Who were you talking to?", "Who was here?", "Did someone else visit?"), and a positive redirect naming `search_memory` as the correct tool for those cases. Concrete counter-examples are the teeth — abstract rules drift, named exclusions don't. **Bug 2 — Ollama fallback visitor_hint system_note** (symmetric to Session 77 rename-hedge). Added new `BrainDB.get_recent_visitor_alerts(target_person_id, hours_back=24)` method pulling VISITOR_ALERT nudges regardless of injection status (owner can ask about a visit AFTER the alert was already shown — the Ollama fallback on the follow-up turn still needs the data). In `pipeline.py` `_all_unreal` block, detect rejected `report_identity_mismatch` where `person_id == _bf_id` (owner asking), fetch recent alerts, inject a system_note into `_ask_offline_safe`: "The user is the household owner asking about who visited while they were away. You DO have this information — share it honestly: [summary]". Combines with existing rename-hint if both happen in one turn. Ollama is stateless so it faithfully speaks what's told — the confabulation fix is "tell it the visitor facts." **Bug 3 — `<<<VISITOR CONTEXT>>>` prompt block** in `_build_system_prompt`. Gated on `VISITOR_CONTEXT_BLOCK_ENABLED=True` AND `"[visitor_id:" in prompt_addendum` (marker embedded by `_run_visitor_alert` in the nudge content). Block explicitly routes owner queries about visitors to `search_memory` AND explicitly excludes `report_identity_mismatch` with the reasoning ("That tool is ONLY for when the current speaker denies being who the sensor identified THEM as"). Marker gating ensures the block only fires when a visitor alert is actually active; adding it unconditionally would bloat every turn's prompt. **+6 regression tests (1059→1065)**: (a) source-inspection of tightened tool description asserting ONLY-scope + canary-question counter-examples + search_memory-redirect + DO-NOT directive; (b+c) two source-inspection tests on the VISITOR CONTEXT block — presence+flag+marker gating + search_memory-named/mismatch-excluded body; (d) `get_recent_visitor_alerts` returns correct metadata shape (visitor_name/visitor_id/turn_count); (e) time-window filter (48h nudge backdated → empty result under 24h window); (f) already-injected nudges still returned (intentional — fallback needs post-injection data). **Classifier prompt hash unchanged** — no LLM prompt touch beyond the new block (which only fires when `[visitor_id:` marker is present, so normal-turn prompts don't change). **Handoff — canary RE-RUN ready**: same scenario as before (factory reset + enroll Jagan + ElevenLabs Lexi "I'm Lexi, classmate" + thesis anxiety + book visit + wait for Lexi session to expire + Jagan asks "who were you talking to when I was away?"). Expected flow now: (1) visitor alert nudge auto-injected into Jagan's turn, (2) brain sees VISITOR CONTEXT block telling it to call `search_memory("Lexi", ...)`, (3) search_memory fires, `_make_memory_search_fn` routes through `query_knowledge_for` with best_friend=Jagan → sees all Lexi facts (including thesis anxiety per 3A.4.6 owner-access model), (4) brain answers naturally with what it retrieved. If brain STILL misroutes to report_identity_mismatch → tool description needs further tightening. If Ollama fallback fires because search_memory returned nothing → query_knowledge_for issue. If brain mentions Lexi's anxiety → owner-access model working as designed. Either outcome is diagnostic. |
| 95 (3A.4.6) | 2026-04-22 | **VISION_ROADMAP Phase 3A.4.6 — simplified owner-access model (user's clarification collapses 3-tier override to single catch-all)** — Jagan's clarification mid-session: "best friend should have all the access right, any person personal or anything in the entire system... bestfriend have the access for everything." Reviewer reshaped the visibility clause from a three-tier enumeration (`public + household + own-personal` for best_friend) to a two-branch exclusion model: owner sees everything EXCEPT `system_only`, non-owner sees only `public + own-personal`. Architectural invariant simplifies from "best_friend is a special tier-overlap case" to "owner is the superuser in their own home." **Code delta**: `_visibility_clause` in `core/brain_agent.py` — best_friend branch returns `("(privacy_level != 'system_only')", [])` (single exclusion predicate, zero params). Non-best-friend branch unchanged (public OR own-personal). Household tier becomes meaningful only as "non-owner speakers can't see this" — no special path needed for owner because owner sees it anyway via the catch-all. **Two 3A.3 test updates**: (a) `test_visibility_clause_best_friend_sees_public_household_own_personal` renamed/rewritten as `test_visibility_clause_best_friend_excludes_only_system_only` — asserts single exclusion predicate, zero params, no positive tier enumeration. (b) `test_visibility_clause_system_only_always_excluded` renamed as `test_visibility_clause_never_permits_system_only` — split assertion: no positive `privacy_level = 'system_only'` match anywhere, AND if `'system_only'` appears at all it's only as a `!= 'system_only'` exclusion (best_friend branch). **VISION_ROADMAP.md P3.21 description rewritten** to reflect the simplified model: owner sees all visitor confidences (including personal ones like "Lexi's thesis anxiety") and uses conversational judgment on what to surface. Live test C (hide visitor personal from best_friend) REMOVED from the test plan — under the simplified model, that's no longer an invariant. If a future need emerges to hide even-from-owner content, that's a new `confidential` tier, not a re-complication of best_friend's access. **Household tier clarified**: now purely "non-owner exclusion" — roommate relationships, household routines, visitor presence. Owner sees them via catch-all. Non-owners hard-excluded. **My 3A.4 `query_knowledge_for` tests unchanged**: `test_best_friend_sees_household_fact_about_other` still passes (best_friend retrieves household fact — rationale shifts from "P3.21 override" to "owner sees everything" but behavior identical). `test_owner_sees_own_personal_fact` passes via best_friend catch-all now instead of the personal+owner predicate. `test_other_cannot_see_someone_elses_personal_fact` passes via unchanged non-best-friend branch. `test_system_only_never_returned_for_any_requester` passes for all 3 requester shapes (best_friend via exclusion, non-best-friend via no-match). **Test count unchanged at 1059** — two assertion updates, zero test additions or deletions. **Classifier prompt hash unchanged** — 3A.4.6 touches no LLM prompts. **Ready for live canary** with updated expectations: Jagan asks "what did Lexi say?" → brain CAN share Lexi's personal anxiety (best_friend full access). This IS the intended user experience. The architecture is tighter — future engineers reasoning about privacy need only remember "owner sees everything, others see only their own + public."
