Less nuance, fewer edge cases, matches the user's mental model of "this is my home." |
| 95 (3A.4.5) | 2026-04-22 | **VISION_ROADMAP Phase 3A.4.5 — write-path migration to 4-tier (Option B, unblocks 3A.4 canary)** — flagged at end of 3A.4: legacy `_privacy_level(attribute)` still wrote `'public'`/`'private'` on every new row via `store_knowledge`, which would have made Jagan unable to see his OWN health_condition/confided_concern facts (the visibility_clause has no `'private'` predicate). Reviewer: "canary would fail in a misleading way — Jagan can't see his own just-stored facts, which looks like the visibility clause is broken rather than the write path." Reviewer prescribed Option B: each caller classifies before calling store_knowledge; DB stays a thin persistence layer. **Six coordinated changes**: (1) `Extraction` dataclass gained `privacy_level: str = PRIVACY_LEVEL_DEFAULT` field (default 'personal' = fail-closed). Dataclass positional-arg compat preserved since privacy_level is the last (defaulted) field. (2) `store_knowledge` INSERT now uses `e.privacy_level` directly — no auto-classification in the DB layer. (3) `ExtractionAgent.extract()` async path: call `await _classify_privacy_level(entity, attr, value, http=self._http)` before constructing each Extraction. Static-map hits stay free; novel attrs hit the LLM classifier exactly once then cache. Wrapped in try/except so classifier exceptions don't wedge extraction — fall back to `PRIVACY_LEVEL_DEFAULT`. (4) Sync agents (RoutineAgent for `typical_arrival_hour`/`typical_visit_duration_min` + `store_temporal_fact` for `current_feeling`) hard-code `privacy_level="personal"` since they emit a small closed set of attributes that are all personal by definition. Avoids threading async through sync code paths. (5) `promote_shadow_to_confirmed` raw INSERT (line 2115) now includes `privacy_level` column with `PRIVACY_LEVEL_DEFAULT` value — previously fell back to column DEFAULT `'public'` which would have leaked shadow-promoted facts under the new visibility_clause. (6) `_privacy_level(attribute)` function DELETED entirely. Grep confirms zero remaining production callers (only docstring references remain). `PRIVATE_ATTRIBUTES` import removed from brain_agent.py (config.py retains the constant, unused, since removing it is a separate concern). **`filter_facts_for_requester` updated for 4-tier names**: `!= "private"` flipped to `== "public"` (under 4-tier, everything sensitive = non-public); added explicit `if not requester_person_id: return facts` guard to preserve pre-3A.4.5 pass-through behavior when internal callers don't know who's asking (dream loop synthesis, schema normalization, housekeeping paths that previously returned all facts). The guard fix was caught by `test_get_context_uses_semantic_ranking_when_embedding_provided` which regressed to empty results without it — canonical example of why "one-line semantics change" is never really one line. **TestPrivacyFilter class overhauled (7 tests, 2 added)**: the old 3 "tagged on store" tests tested `_privacy_level`'s auto-classification by attribute name — dead under the new contract. Replaced with 3 pass-through tests proving `Extraction.privacy_level` round-trips correctly for each tier ('personal' default, explicit 'public', 'household' + 'system_only'). The 4 filter tests updated: tier names `'private'` → `'personal'` + test renamed to reflect 4-tier semantics. `test_semantic_search_returns_privacy_level` updated to explicit `privacy_level="public"` on the Extraction (no more implicit 'public' default under 4-tier). **+2 new ExtractionAgent tests** per reviewer's spec: (a) `test_static_map_attribute_skips_llm_classifier` — monkeypatches `_call_llm_chat` to raise AssertionError, constructs a fake Together.ai response extracting `health_condition='diabetes'`, asserts `results[0].privacy_level == "personal"` AND LLM was never called (static-map hit) AND cache remains empty. (b) `test_novel_attribute_invokes_classifier_and_caches` — uses a genuinely novel attribute (`favorite_coding_language`, grep-verified not in static map), monkeypatches `_call_llm_chat` to return valid JSON, asserts first extraction fires LLM once + caches result + second extraction short-circuits to cache (call_count still 1). Guards the "cache warms, subsequent writes skip LLM" property which is the cost-amortization invariant. **Test count: 1057 → 1059.** Reviewer expected ~1060-1062; hit the low end because the TestPrivacyFilter rewrite replaced 3 old tests with 3 new tests (net zero), and I added 2 new ExtractionAgent tests (net +2). Net scope match. **Classifier prompt hash unchanged** — 3A.4.5 touches NO LLM prompts, only the write-path plumbing. Phase 5 drift detection baseline holds. **VISION_ROADMAP.md updated** per reviewer's "preserve the architecture reasoning" ask: P3.1-P3.3 marked `[x]`, P3.4 marked `[~]` (canary in flight), new 3A.4.5 entry added with rationale "landed in Session 95 to unblock 3A.4 canary — old 2-tier write path was producing `'private'` rows invisible under the new visibility clause". **Handoff — live canary cleared to run**: with write-path migration landed, the Lexi/thesis-anxiety/book-visit scenario is now unambiguous. Jagan should see (i) his own personal facts (lives_in='Tirupati', etc.), (ii) Lexi's household-tier facts (visited, general topic), (iii) NOT Lexi's personal facts (thesis anxiety confided in private). Three-way distinction validates the full model end-to-end. If canary passes → 3A.5 replicates `query_knowledge_for` pattern across the remaining 3 retrieval sites. |
| 95 (3A.4) | 2026-04-22 | **VISION_ROADMAP Phase 3A.4 — canary wire-in: `query_knowledge_for` + `_make_memory_search_fn` + legacy row backfill (first real behavior change)** — one retrieval site only per reviewer's canary discipline. The other 3 retrieval sites (`find_knowledge_id`, `_cull_stale_knowledge` query side, `ProactiveNudgeAgent.run_cross_person_inference`) stay on the legacy `get_active_knowledge + filter_facts_for_requester` pipeline until 3A.5 replicates the pattern. **Three touches, one goal**: (1) `BrainDB.query_knowledge_for(requester_pid, best_friend_id, *, entity=, attribute=, limit=20)` — new method on BrainDB composing `_visibility_clause` into the SELECT. Returns the reviewer-spec'd 6-column shape (entity/attribute/value/confidence/person_id/privacy_level), sorted by confidence DESC then created_at DESC, limited by `limit`. Includes the same invalidation/temporal filters as `get_active_knowledge` (`invalidated_at IS NULL` + `(valid_until IS NULL OR valid_until > now)`) so expired/invalidated rows don't leak through. **Deviation from reviewer's sketch**: reviewer wrote `invalidated = 0` but the schema column is `invalidated_at` (nullable, NULL = valid) per line 662 of brain_agent.py — corrected in implementation to match reality. (2) One-shot legacy backfill in `BrainDB.__init__` after the existing ALTER TABLE block: two UPDATEs convergent on `PRIVACY_LEVEL_DEFAULT='personal'`. First targets NULL rows (reviewer's original spec — belt-and-braces safety net; the column has DEFAULT 'public' so no rows should actually be NULL in practice, but a hand-edit or older schema path could produce them). Second migrates legacy 2-tier `'private'` rows → `'personal'` — this is the migration reviewer's spec didn't explicitly call out but NEEDS: the pre-existing `_privacy_level(attribute)` classifier at line 212 writes `'public'` or `'private'` into stored rows; the new `_visibility_clause` has NO predicate matching `'private'`, so without this migration every legacy owner-only fact vanishes at the canary site (including from its own owner). 'private' → 'personal' maps 1:1 semantically (both = owner-only). Both UPDATEs print rowcount when non-zero; both are no-ops on every subsequent process start once the rows have migrated. (3) `_make_memory_search_fn` in pipeline.py (~line 2870) — replaced the 2-step `get_active_knowledge + filter_facts_for_requester` chain with a single `query_knowledge_for` call. Everything else preserved: the S73 Bug D3 dispute skip (check still runs BEFORE the privacy query), the conversation_excerpts path (independent, FaceDB not BrainDB), the Bug N sparse-memory hint system. `_subject_pid = db.get_person_id_by_name(...)` lookup removed since `_visibility_clause`'s `privacy_level='personal' AND person_id=?` handles owner-detection via the fact's stored `person_id` directly. **+6 tests (1051→1057)** in `test_brain_agent.py::TestBrainDBQueryKnowledgeFor`, each bypasses `store_knowledge`'s 2-tier classifier via direct `INSERT INTO knowledge (..., privacy_level=?)` so tiers can be tested independently: (a) best_friend_sees_household_fact_about_other — Jagan retrieves `visited_household='true'` about Lexi → returned ✓ (P3.21 override). (b) non_best_friend_cannot_see_household_fact — Kara (another visitor) retrieves same row → `[]` (the critical leak-prevention invariant). (c) owner_sees_own_personal_fact — Jagan retrieves his own `lives_in='Tirupati'` (person_id='jagan_abc', personal) → returned. (d) other_cannot_see_someone_elses_personal_fact — Lexi (household member) retrieves Jagan's `lives_in` → `[]` (S94 refinement: best_friend override doesn't extend personal visibility across owners). (e) system_only_never_returned_for_any_requester — 3 requester combinations (owner, best_friend, no-bf context) all get `[]` against a `voice_embedding_hash` row (the "never disclose" invariant anchored as a regression guard). (f) backfill_migrates_null_and_legacy_private_to_personal — simulates a pre-backfill DB via raw SQLite with NULL and 'private' rows, opens via BrainDB (which triggers __init__ backfill), asserts both rows now have `privacy_level='personal'` AND that `query_knowledge_for` returns them to Jagan (owner) but hides them from Lexi (other). Single test covers both backfill variants + end-to-end retrieval. **Known caveat (flag for reviewer's attention)**: `_privacy_level(attribute)` legacy classifier at brain_agent.py:212 still writes `'private'` for sensitive attributes on new rows via `store_knowledge`. The backfill catches them ONLY on next BrainDB __init__. A long-running process would accumulate new `'private'` rows that are invisible at the canary site until restart. Per reviewer's scope rule ("3A.4 is pure one-site canary + backfill + 6 tests"), I did NOT migrate `_privacy_level` to return `'personal'` — that's a broader write-path change that would break the TestPrivacyFilter suite's "health_condition → private" assertions (lines 3743-3763). Flagging for reviewer: the next phase (3A.5 or 3A.6) probably needs to either (α) update `_privacy_level` to emit 4-tier values and fix TestPrivacyFilter's assertions, or (β) call `_classify_privacy_level` (our P3.2 LLM classifier) at `store_knowledge` write time per the 3A.2 handoff plan ("Option A: classify at agent layer, pass `privacy_level` into `store_knowledge` as required arg"). **Classifier prompt hash unchanged** — 3A.4 touches no prompt. Phase 5 drift-detection baseline holds. **Handoff — reviewer diff review before live canary**: reviewer wants to eyeball the diff before the canary session fires. Deterministic scenario (per reviewer's spec): factory reset → enroll Jagan as best_friend → ElevenLabs "Hi Kara, I'm Lexi, Jagan's classmate" (Lexi session opens) → "I've been feeling anxious about my thesis deadline" (should classify `current_anxiety` as personal via 3A.2 LLM classifier) → "I came by to borrow a book" (should classify as household-ish presence fact) → wait 30s for Lexi session to expire → Jagan asks "Who were you talking to when I was away?" → expected brain response mentions Lexi's visit + book topic (household-visible to owner) but does NOT mention the anxiety (personal-to-Lexi, invisible even to best_friend). Live canary validates LLM classifier + _visibility_clause + _make_memory_search_fn compose correctly end-to-end. If canary passes → 3A.5 replicates the pattern to the other 3 retrieval sites. If canary shows Jagan seeing the anxiety fact → investigate whether `_classify_privacy_level` is even being invoked at store_knowledge time (likely NOT — see "known caveat" above — and the expected-reveal would confirm the missing write-path wiring). |
| 95 (3A.3) | 2026-04-22 | **VISION_ROADMAP Phase 3A.3 — `_visibility_clause` SQL composer (pure helper, no wire-in)** — reviewer's live smoke-test sign-off arrived with 3/3 classifier cases matching predicted tiers against the real Together.ai endpoint (`(Jagan, current_anxiety, "deadline") → personal`, `(Lexi, roommate_of, "Kara") → household`, `(stranger_abc, session_token, "f4a8...") → system_only`). `tests/smoke_privacy_classifier.py` kept as a repeatable manual tool for future prompt revisions. Reviewer then spec'd 3A.3 with explicit "Proceed." — pure code addition, zero risk, one file touched. **Shape**: `_visibility_clause(requester_pid, best_friend_id) -> (where_clause, params)` in `core/brain_agent.py` right after `_classify_privacy_level` (same privacy-layer block, single source of truth for visibility rules). Returns a SQL WHERE fragment callers compose under an outer AND: `full_where = f"{base_where} AND ({vis_where})"`. **Policy (load-bearing, matches PRIVACY_LEVELS semantics):** (1) `public` → always included (unconditional first clause), (2) `personal` → owner-only via `privacy_level = 'personal' AND person_id = ?` with `requester_pid` as the param (drives the "Jagan sees only Jagan's own personal facts" invariant), (3) `household` → ONLY when `best_friend_id AND requester_pid == best_friend_id` (owner privilege), (4) `system_only` → HARD-EXCLUDED by construction (no clause, no matter who's asking — embeddings/credits/diagnostics never leak to ANY user, not even the owner; this is the whole reason 3A.1 carved system_only as a distinct tier instead of collapsing it with personal+best_friend). **Shape details**: each predicate individually parenthesized, OR-joined, whole expression wrapped in outer parens so caller's `f"... AND ({vis_where})"` composition gets `WHERE entity = ? AND ((predicate_1) OR (predicate_2))` — double parens by design, proves proper nesting. Params list is ordered to align with `?` placeholders (one param for the personal clause; zero for public/household since those are string literals). **+6 deterministic tests (1045→1051), zero LLM calls**: (a) best_friend_sees_public_household_own_personal — asserts all 3 tier substrings present, system_only absent, params = [requester_pid]. (b) non_best_friend_sees_public_own_personal_not_household — the CRITICAL exclusion; without household suppressed, Lexi could see Jagan's social graph. (c) no_best_friend_id_acts_as_non_privileged — pre-first-boot / unset best_friend scenario (None). (d) system_only_always_excluded — regression guard across 3 (requester, best_friend) combinations; the invariant that made us carve system_only as its own tier. (e) composes_cleanly_with_and — verifies double-paren shape in composed SQL; catches any future refactor that drops the outer wrapping. (f) params_align_with_placeholders — `clause.count("?") == len(params)` across all 3 shapes; guards against future edits that add a param but forget a placeholder. **Classifier prompt hash unchanged** — 3A.3 touches no LLM prompt; Phase 5 drift-detection baseline holds. **What 3A.3 deliberately does NOT do**: (1) no wire-in at any retrieval site yet — that's 3A.4 (canary on `_make_memory_search_fn` in pipeline.py ~2263-2283). (2) no Kuzu touch — that's 3A.5+ with schema bump. (3) no schema migration on `knowledge` table — the `privacy_level` column arrives with 3A.3's consumer (3A.4). The helper is dormant until called. **Handoff**: reviewer will spec 3A.4 — canary-wire `_visibility_clause` into `_make_memory_search_fn` + bring the `privacy_level` column migration online (schema v-bump + ALTER TABLE path + `store_knowledge` signature change to take `privacy_level: str` as required arg per reviewer's Option A design decision from 3A.2 handoff). Validate end-to-end with one live session (Jagan + visitor scenario) before replicating to the other 3 retrieval sites in 3A.5. |
| 95 (3A.2) | 2026-04-22 | **VISION_ROADMAP Phase 3A.2 — `_classify_privacy_level` tiered helper (review gate before 3A.3 wire-in)** — same session continued after 3A.1 refinements landed; reviewer's info.md spec'd the helper end-to-end with explicit "Proceed." greenlight. **Shape**: pure helper module code, zero behavior change in production paths until 3A.3 wires it into `BrainDB.store_knowledge`. Location: `core/brain_agent.py` module-scope, right after the legacy `_privacy_level(attribute)` PRIVATE_ATTRIBUTES helper (topical grouping — both deal with privacy, but the new helper implements the tiered P3.2 contract while the legacy one is kept for binary public/private sites that haven't migrated yet). **Layered lookup (fastest first):** (1) `PRIVACY_LEVEL_STATIC_MAP[attribute]` — O(1) dict check, zero I/O, most facts hit this path (3A.1's ~22 pre-classified attributes). (2) `_privacy_classifier_cache[attribute]` — process-lifetime dict; cache is keyed by attribute alone, not (entity, attribute), because the tier is a property of WHAT the fact is, not WHOSE fact it is ("health_condition" is personal for everyone — reviewer's choice). (3) `_ask_privacy_llm(entity, attribute, value, http=...)` — LLM fallback for novel attributes via the shared `_call_llm_chat` helper (Session 69) with `response_format={"type": "json_object"}`, `max_tokens=150` (tight — classifier returns `{level, reasoning}` only), `timeout=5s` (mirrors `_classify_intent`), standard exponential-backoff retry inherited from `_call_llm_chat`. **Fail-closed policy** (load-bearing): LLM failure (None from `_call_llm_chat` on timeout/network/5xx/4xx), malformed JSON (brace-salvage in `_parse_json` fails), OR valid-JSON-but-invalid-tier (e.g. `{"level": "secret"}`) — all three paths return `PRIVACY_LEVEL_DEFAULT='personal'` WITHOUT writing to cache. Caching a failure would permanently pin the attribute at the wrong tier on a single transient provider blip. Only successful classifications with a tier ∈ PRIVACY_LEVELS get cached. Also handles `http=None` (caller has no LLM path) → default without caching, same safety contract. **Prompt** (`_PRIVACY_CLASSIFIER_SYSTEM`): 4-tier definition block, 5 RULES (when in doubt→personal, social-graph→household, embarrass/harm→personal, mechanical→system_only, name-level→public with location/role caveat), 5 EXAMPLES verbatim from reviewer's info.md (Lexi.name→public, Lexi.relationship_to_jagan→household, Jagan.confided_worry→personal, Lexi.visited_household→household, stranger_abc.voice_embedding_hash→system_only). User message is a 3-line `entity:/attribute:/value:` envelope. Classifier hash (`_PRIVACY_CLASSIFIER_SYSTEM` sha256 12-char): unchanged from this session's initial ship — will baseline on first 3A.3 live run. **Config additions (2)** in `core/config.py`: `PRIVACY_CLASSIFIER_TIMEOUT_SECS=5.0`, `PRIVACY_CLASSIFIER_MAX_TOKENS=150`. **+6 tests (1039→1045)**: (a) static_map_hit_never_calls_llm — monkeypatches `_call_llm_chat` to raise AssertionError, calls with `attribute="name"` (in static map), asserts "public" returned and LLM never invoked. (b) cache_hit_never_calls_llm — seeds cache with `{"novel_attr": "household"}`, asserts classifier returns cached tier without invoking LLM (same boom-on-call monkeypatch). (c) llm_success_caches_result — monkeypatches `_call_llm_chat` to return `'{"level": "personal", "reasoning": "sensitive"}'`; asserts return value + cache write, then calls a SECOND time with a different entity/value but same attribute and asserts the LLM call count is still 1 (cache short-circuit on second call). (d) llm_timeout_fails_closed_no_cache — monkeypatch returns None (the actual signature `_call_llm_chat` exposes for all transient failures), asserts "personal" returned and cache unwritten. (e) llm_invalid_level_fails_closed_no_cache — monkeypatch returns `'{"level": "secret", ...}'`, asserts "personal" returned and cache unwritten (the "classifier went rogue" protection). (f) llm_malformed_json_fails_closed_no_cache — monkeypatch returns raw prose ("I think this is probably personal but I'm not sure"), asserts "personal" returned and cache unwritten. All 6 tests use a `_clear_privacy_cache` fixture that resets `brain_agent._privacy_classifier_cache` around each test — classifier state is process-lifetime so tests must isolate. **What 3A.2 deliberately does NOT do** (per reviewer): (1) no wiring into `BrainDB.store_knowledge` — that's 3A.3 with the `privacy_level` column migration + call-site updates. (2) no retroactive classification of existing facts — that's a separate one-shot backfill job. (3) no touching of retrieval — that's `_visibility_clause` helper in P3.3. **Review gate standing before 3A.3**: reviewer will review the actual prompt (`_PRIVACY_CLASSIFIER_SYSTEM` content) + a few hand-run classifications on edge cases (a voice_embedding-adjacent novel attribute, a mood-adjacent novel attribute, a household-social-graph-adjacent novel attribute) before greenlighting the schema migration. Same discipline as Phase 1's classifier prompt review. **Handoff**: ping reviewer with the prompt text + classifier-hash for sign-off. Recommended edge-case manual runs: `(Jagan, current_anxiety, "deadline")` — expect `personal`; `(Lexi, roommate_of, "Kara")` — expect `household`; `(stranger_abc, session_token, "f4a8...")` — expect `system_only`. On reviewer green light → 3A.3 (schema migration: add `privacy_level TEXT DEFAULT 'personal'` to `knowledge` table + wire `_classify_privacy_level` into the `store_knowledge` write path + update 2-3 retrieval sites to filter on it). |
| 95 (3A.1) | 2026-04-22 | **VISION_ROADMAP Phase 3A.1 — privacy model config scaffolding (review gate before 3A.2)** — Phase 3 kickoff per reviewer's split-execution plan. This session is deliberately pure-config; no policy logic, no retrieval-site wiring, no schema changes yet. Three new constants in `core/config.py` right after `CROSS_PERSON_PRIVACY_BLOCK_ENABLED` (Session 91's P3.21 flag — natural topical neighbor): **(1) `PRIVACY_LEVELS: frozenset[str]` — exhaustive locked tier set** `{"public", "personal", "household", "system_only"}`. Frozenset (not set or tuple) is the deliberate choice: closed — adding a tier later requires a code change rather than silently accepting `"secret"` / `"room_private"` / whatever at runtime, which matters because fail-closed classifier logic downstream will be `if level not in PRIVACY_LEVELS: level = PRIVACY_LEVEL_DEFAULT`; immutable — callers can't accidentally mutate it. **(2) `PRIVACY_LEVEL_DEFAULT = "personal"` — fail-closed default** for attributes the classifier hasn't seen and the static-map doesn't cover. Reviewer's choice of "personal" (not "public" or "system_only") is load-bearing: "public" would leak novel attributes cross-person, "system_only" would hide them even from the owner. "Personal" = owner sees it, others don't — safest default, matches every privacy system's "when in doubt, owner-only" rule. **(3) `PRIVACY_LEVEL_STATIC_MAP: dict[str, str]` — pre-classified attribute fast-path** (~22 entries across all 4 tiers). Reviewer's tiering: `public` for identity anchors that the owner would reasonably expect any mutually-known speaker to know (name, from_country, relationship_to_jagan, relationship_to_best_friend); `household` for shared-context facts (preferred_ai_name, lives_in_household, household_role, visited_household, discussed_topic — presence facts and topic-areas, deliberately NOT the contents of those discussions); `personal` for owner-private (lives_in specific town, from_state, works_at, job_title, current_mood/activity, dietary_preference/favorite_food, confided_concern, health_condition); `system_only` for never-disclose (voice_embedding_hash, face_embedding_hash, bootstrap_credits — infrastructure). **Best_friend override semantics documented in the comment block** (reviewer's Session 94 refinement that VISION_ROADMAP.md captures): when the asking speaker IS the best_friend (currently Jagan, the owner), retrieval WILL surface `public + household + any personal facts about themselves` but STILL SUPPRESSES `personal` facts about OTHER people. That's the "owner has full-household-access but never stranger-personal-access" semantic the reviewer locked in after Jagan's "the best friend always has all the access" pushback on P3.21. Note: `system_only` tier is genuinely never disclosed — not even to the owner — because hashes/credits are plumbing, not memory; there's no meaningful reason to surface them in LLM context. **+3 invariant tests (1036→1039):** `test_privacy_levels_exhaustive_and_frozen` (exact 4-tier set + frozenset type — the "closed world" property is the whole point), `test_privacy_default_is_personal_fail_closed` (default name + default-in-levels invariant), `test_privacy_static_map_values_valid` (every value in the static map ∈ PRIVACY_LEVELS + pins the high-stakes anchors: confided_concern/health_condition → personal, visited_household/discussed_topic → household so any future "let's make the list public to simplify retrieval" refactor breaks these asserts immediately). Tests land next to the P1.1 INTENT_LABELS invariant tests in `test_pipeline.py` — same shape, same rigor (enum-exhaustive + structural), same reason for existing (drift-prevention on the data contract that downstream code will trust). **Zero behavior changes this session** — the constants are load-bearing only for 3A.2 (`_classify_privacy_level(entity, attribute, value)` — static-map fast path + LLM fallback + session-scoped cache) which is the next deliverable. **Classifier prompt hash unchanged** (still `df9ae2a1cdde` — S94) because this session touches no LLM prompt. Phase 5 drift detection baseline holds. **Reviewer sign-off refinements (same session, post-initial-ship):** reviewer approved ~90% of the tier assignments but flagged two moves before greenlight. **(1) `relationship_to_jagan` and `relationship_to_best_friend` moved `public` → `household`**. Rationale: "classmate of Jagan" reveals *Jagan's* social graph to third visitors — if a random stranger asks Kara "what's your relationship to Jagan?" the answer "Lexi is Jagan's classmate" leaks Jagan's social info, not just Lexi's. `household` keeps the fact visible to best_friend (owner visibility — correct semantic) while hiding it from random strangers. Single best_friend today (Jagan) makes the cross-household-member nuance moot; matters once flagged roommates come online per roadmap. **(2) `from_country` / `from_state` asymmetry documented with a NB comment**. `from_country='India'` stays `public` (nationality-level, commonly shared); `from_state='Andhra Pradesh'` stays `personal` (narrower location, identifiable). Logically defensible but looks inconsistent at a glance — the comment blocks a future maintainer from "normalizing" them to the same tier and accidentally leaking location specifics. No test changes needed — the existing `test_privacy_static_map_values_valid` anchors (confided_concern/health_condition → personal, visited_household/discussed_topic → household) all hold through the refinement. 1039 still passing. **Handoff (updated)**: reviewer will spec 3A.2 next — `_classify_privacy_level(entity, attribute, value)` with (a) static-map fast path, (b) session-lifetime cache keyed by `(entity_type, attribute)`, (c) LLM classifier fallback with strict-schema JSON response, 5s timeout, `_call_llm_chat` retry, fail-closed to `PRIVACY_LEVEL_DEFAULT='personal'` on error. Review gate on 3A.2 happens AFTER the prompt is written, BEFORE wiring into `BrainDB.store_knowledge` — same flow as Phase 1's classifier prompt review. |
| 94 | 2026-04-22 | **Multi-speaker canary fixes #1 + #2 + #5 (per Jagan's "brain should adapt to humans, not expect rules") + current corpus grew** — 2026-04-22 retry canary surfaced 5 distinct failure modes reviewer catalogued. Jagan's principle: "people act on instinct not on set of rules... the machine should be perfect for all cases." Fix 5 independent bugs, 3 this session (1, 2, 5) + defer 2 to Phase 3 proper (3, 4). **Fix #1 — classifier greeting-vs-assign rule** (~15 min): the classifier misread "Hi Cara, Imlexi, Jagan's classmate" as `assign_system_name` value='Cara' — reading the greeting target as a rename target instead of the self-introduction. New GREETING-vs-ASSIGN block in `_INTENT_CLASSIFIER_SYSTEM` with 3 verbatim counter-examples ("Hi Kara, I'm Sarah" → `assign_own_name` value='Sarah', "Hello Atlas, my name is Mike", "Hey Nova, I'm Priya, Jagan's friend") plus explicit STT-mangling note teaching the classifier to strip "Im" contractions (Whisper compresses "I'm Lexi" → "Imlexi"). +1 golden row `regression_session_94` preserving the exact live-canary utterance with correct expected label. +1 invariant test (rule name + ≥2 of 3 counter-examples + STT-mangling note). **Fix #2 — Im-contraction grounding normalization** (~30 min): new `_strip_im_contraction(s)` helper detecting `^I['\u2019]?m[a-zA-Z].*$` and returning the portion after `Im`/`I'm`/`I\u2019m`. Applied at 3 call sites in `_intent_allows` grounding block — `extracted_value` substring check, `tool_args[arg_key]` cross-check, and the Session-87 elif branch (arg present without classifier extraction). Defense-in-depth: when the classifier OR the LLM copies the Whisper-mangled form verbatim, the grounding check now normalizes both sides before comparison so "Lexi" in "I'm Lexi" matches "Imlexi" in "Imlexi". Capital-I anchoring requirement distinguishes the contraction from mid-sentence words; false positives on "Important"/"Imaginary" as extracted_values are acceptable because those wouldn't be produced as name-extractions in practice. +3 tests (extracted_value strip, both-sides arg cross-check, helper variants covering ASCII/Unicode apostrophe + lowercase-initial rejection). **Fix #5 — bootstrap credit replenishment** (~30 min): 2026-04-22 run showed a stranger stuck at `voice_n=2, bootstrap=0` across multiple session re-opens. Root cause: `N_INITIAL_VOICE_BOOTSTRAP=20` only seeds at engagement-gated open; subsequent re-opens via voice match inherit `bootstrap=0` from `_open_session`'s default and every subsequent `_voice_accum_allowed` refuses via Path C. Without face witness (ElevenLabs / offscreen speakers) Path A is blocked; Path B requires mature profile the stranger never reaches. Fix: top up `bootstrap_credits` by +1 per turn at the start of `_accumulate_voice` when the session is an engaged stranger (`waiting_for_name=False`, `person_type='stranger'`) AND the profile is below `VOICE_ACCUM_MATURE_SAMPLE_COUNT`, capped at new `VOICE_MAX_BOOTSTRAP_CREDITS=10`. Gated by `VOICE_BOOTSTRAP_REPLENISH_ENABLED=True`. Negative guard: replenishment ONLY fires when gate has been passed (preserves the "no credits until system-name said" invariant). +2 tests (engaged-stranger replenishes + gate-still-active negative case). **Phase 3 backlog — Fix #3 + Fix #4 filed, not shipped this session:** Fix #3 (session_id / display_name / extracted-fact entity-key name-space unification — extraction agents must use current display_name, not LLM's guess) is more accurately scoped to P3.24's explicit per-session display name work in RoomOrchestrator. Fix #4 (brain should acknowledge voice recognition on session re-open — "I recognize your voice" vs "I don't know you") fits naturally into P3.26's honesty-with-privacy prompt contract. Both logged in Phase 3 backlog; neither blocks today's work. **+6 tests (1030→1036).** Classifier prompt hash: `6950307c4f8d` → `df9ae2a1cdde` (first change since S84). Intentional calibration revision — documented here so the next bench run's drift analysis sees the boundary and doesn't misread pre-S94 vs post-S94 metrics as a real accuracy regression. **Handoff to Jagan**: factory reset (brain.db still polluted with hallucinated Lexi.likes_cheese et al. from prior canary) + retry the simple canary scenario — enroll as Jagan → ElevenLabs "Hi Kara, I'm Lexi" × 2 turns (longer ≥1.5s each this time so no ambiguity) → let Lexi expire → ask "Who were you talking to when I was away?" for P3.21 validation. All 3 fixes + the tiered threshold + the privacy block exercise in one run. |
| 93 | 2026-04-22 | **P3.23 tiered threshold — ambiguous-zone drop stops memory-pollution cascade** — Session 92's P3.23 landed the hard-mismatch drop (score < 0.20 → drop) which caught clean rejects live (lines 225, 239 of the retry canary: 0.14 and 0.17 correctly dropped). But the retry canary also exposed the **ambiguous zone** (0.20-0.40) the single-threshold didn't address. **Cheese-cascade incident** (2026-04-22 multi-person retry): Lexi's 0.64s "You know, I love cheese" scored 0.38 vs Jagan's mature gallery (well below normal Jagan 0.6-0.8) but above the 0.20 floor → routed to Jagan → brain extracted `Jagan.likes_cheese='true'` → conflicted with prior `Jagan.opinion_of_cheese='negative'` → extracted `Lexi.likes_cheese='true'` (hallucinated — Lexi never said it) → extracted `Lexi.has_influence_on_jagan='true'` (further hallucination built on the first hallucination). Memory pollution compounds in under 5 turns. **Tiered policy**: `< 0.20` → hard mismatch (S92); `0.20 ≤ score < 0.40 AND n_active_sessions ≥ 2` → ambiguous zone drop (S93); `≥ 0.40` OR solo session → trust current (no regression on solo use). Solo-only invariant is the key correctness property reviewer called out — a single person's own brief utterance dipping to 0.35 shouldn't be dropped just because 0.20 ≤ 0.35 < 0.40. Ambiguous drop only activates when the room plausibly contains another speaker. **Shape changes:** new `VOICE_ROUTING_SHORT_UTT_AMBIGUOUS=0.40` config. `_resolve_actual_speaker` gained `n_active_sessions: int = 1` kwarg (default 1 = solo = no-op). Call site at `pipeline.run` passes `len(_active_sessions)`. Tier-2 branch returns the same `"short_utterance_voice_mismatch"` outcome so the existing pipeline dispatch handles it uniformly. **+3 tests (1027→1030):** ambiguous-zone-solo-holds-current (no regression guard, explicitly n_active_sessions=1), ambiguous-zone-multi-session-drops (the Lexi/cheese case, n=2), above-ambiguous-multi-session-holds (0.55 score trusts cur_pid even in multi-room). **Also noted in live log (not blocking)**: 1/6 classifier timeouts = 17%, likely Together.ai tail latency — monitor, don't tune yet. Classifier hash unchanged (`6950307c4f8d`). **Handoff to Jagan**: **FACTORY RESET REQUIRED before retry** — `brain.db` now contains hallucinated `Lexi.likes_cheese`, `Lexi.has_influence_on_jagan`, conflicting `Jagan.likes_cheese`/`opinion_of_cheese` facts from the polluted attribution cascade. Those will poison every future session as "retrieved memory" until wiped. After reset + re-enroll, retry the multi-person canary AND deliberately ask "who were you talking to when I was away?" — this will validate P3.21 + both P3.23 tiers + the honest-phrasing privacy block end-to-end in one session. Do NOT proceed to 3A.1 until this canary passes cleanly. |
| 92 | 2026-04-22 | **P3.23 pulled forward — multi-speaker-aware short-utterance floor** — Session 91's P3.21 live-canary attempt failed not because of the privacy block but because Lexi never got her own session. Every ElevenLabs utterance (0.67s "Hi Kara", 0.86s "I'm not Jagan, my name is Lexi", 0.90s "no it's not me") fell under the Bug F 1.0s floor with voice scores 0.08–0.55 (unambiguously not Jagan's 0.7–0.8 on his own gallery) — but the blanket "hold current" policy routed everything to Jagan's active session. When "I'm Lexi" later fired the classifier's `assign_own_name`/Lexi path, it tried to rename JAGAN rather than opening Lexi's session, triggering dispute state. Jagan + user: **real conversations start short ("Hi", "hey") — a system that needs 1.5s utterances to recognize strangers is broken by design. Fix P3.23 first, then retry P3.21 canary.** The roadmap scoped P3.23 to RoomOrchestrator (3B territory), but the policy is decision-logic not coordination-logic, so lands today in `_resolve_actual_speaker` and will migrate into RoomOrchestrator during 3B. **Design (reviewer Session 92):** keep the 1.0s floor as default, but layer a "voice clearly not cur_pid" escape. ECAPA on 0.5–1.0s is noisy but directional — "is this obviously NOT cur_pid?" is answerable even when "is this definitely X?" isn't. When `audio >= MIN_AUDIO_FOR_SCORE=0.5s` AND `v_score < SHORT_UTT_FLOOR=0.20` (against whole gallery including cur_pid), return new outcome `"short_utterance_voice_mismatch"` → caller drops the turn. User or other speaker naturally repeats with a longer utterance which then routes through the normal new_stranger path. Two-turn cost for correctness; cheaper than opening a phantom stranger session with noisy 0.5s data that'd need cleanup. **3 config additions:** `VOICE_ROUTING_SHORT_UTT_MISMATCH_ENABLED=True` (toggle), `VOICE_ROUTING_SHORT_UTT_FLOOR=0.20` (mismatch threshold), `VOICE_ROUTING_MIN_AUDIO_FOR_SCORE=0.5` (below this, skip even the directional check and fall back to hold-current). **Pipeline dispatch**: `elif _routing_action == "short_utterance_voice_mismatch": continue` — no attribution, no accumulation, no `conversation_turn` fire. **+4 tests**: voice-matches-current holds session (0.7s + 0.75 → current), voice-mismatch drops (0.7s + 0.08 vs mature holder → drop — the exact Lexi scenario), audio-too-short fallback (0.3s + 0.05 → current; ECAPA too noisy even for directional call), config-flag-off regression guard (flag False → old blanket-hold behavior preserved exactly). **1 pre-existing Bug F test updated** (`test_short_utterance_stays_on_current_session`): previously used `v_score=0.05` to simulate "noisy short utterance" under the old blanket policy — that exact condition is now the new drop path. Bumped to `v_pid="jagan_abc", v_score=0.75` so the test simulates the "holder says brief closer" case Bug F originally protected (holder's own voice, matches profile, above FLOOR → holds current). **+4 tests (1023→1027).** Classifier hash unchanged (`6950307c4f8d`). **Handoff to Jagan**: retry P3.21 live canary with the ORIGINAL short utterances — Lexi's "Hi Kara" (~0.67s) should now emit `[Voice] Routing: short_utterance_voice_mismatch` and drop. Lexi's second longer "Hi Kara, I'm Lexi" (~1.2s) should pass the 1.0s floor → `new_stranger` → session opens properly. Then natural flow: Lexi chats briefly, session ends, Jagan asks "who were you talking to when I was away?" → brain should respond with P3.21's honest-not-denial phrasing. Two policies validate together in one canary run. |

Phase 5 drift detection baseline holds. **Handoff to Jagan**: live canary for P3.21 — one ElevenLabs session where John says "Hi Kara, I'm John, just chatting" for 2 turns → session ends → Jagan as himself asks "who were you talking to when I was away?" → expected brain response: "Someone else was in the room and spoke with me — I can't share their specifics without their consent" (NOT "No one"). Fix: migrated `ContradictionAgent._call_together` to the helper — retry on transient errors with exponential backoff, no retry on 4xx, explicit 'choices' shape validation, `(no detail)` fallback for empty messages. Despite the branch only running AFTER the ambient gate confirmed the user said the system name (semantically an engagement-gate pass), `bootstrap_credits` stayed at 0 — every stranger turn refused with `bootstrap=0`. **Fix B** at `pipeline.py:4630` (switch_enrolled branch): the upstream `voice_match_conf` write at the `_v_pid in _active_sessions` guard gets skipped when the session doesn't exist yet (session had expired before the re-entry turn). The fresh session opened by switch_enrolled inherits `voice_match_conf=0.0` from `_open_session` defaults → `_voice_accum_allowed` Path B (mature voice self-match) refuses despite voice_n=20. Added explicit `_update_identity_evidence(_resolved_pid, voice_match_conf=_v_score, voice_last_heard_ts=time.time())` after session open + session-dict updates. +2 source-inspection regression guards: `test_voice_first_stranger_open_grants_bootstrap_credits` (Fix A) + `test_switch_enrolled_writes_voice_match_conf_to_fresh_session` (Fix B). **+3 tests (1018→1021).** No classifier-prompt changes — hash still `6950307c4f8d`. **Four Phase-3 backlog tickets filed** per reviewer's multi-convo analysis: (1) P3.X — privacy-scoped honest phrasing ("I can't share specifics about them" instead of "No one") when brain has knowledge of cross-person sessions it can't disclose; (2) P3.X — RetroScan per-person scope awareness (rename of person A must not invalidate person B's facts); (3) P3.X — multi-speaker-aware short-utterance floor policy (RoomOrchestrator redesign, current floor single-speaker-tuned); (4) P3.X — honesty-vs-privacy prompt contract (Phase 4 overlap but primary home Phase 3). **Handoff**: one more multi-convo live session to confirm Bug 1 + Bug 2 fixes (expected: no more `bootstrap=0` refusals when routing shows real scores + no more silent `ContradictionAgent` ReadTimeouts). | 89 | 2026-04-22 | **Session 88 post-live audit — pyannote load-failure root-cause fix + loader traceback observability** — Session 88's multi-person live test ran for 6 diarize invocations and hit **100% pyannote load failure** (`IndexError: list index out of range`) followed by silent fallback to `_diarize_ecapa_valley`. Net effect: all multi-speaker infrastructure was offline during the live run; the entire session actually executed on the legacy ECAPA-valley backend that returns 0 segments on 2+ speaker audio — the exact Session 39 failure mode Phase 2 was supposed to fix. Fallback chain caught the failure cleanly (no crash) but Phase 2 delivered no user-visible benefit. **Root cause: two layered monkeypatches conflicted.** Session 38 added a module-import runtime monkeypatch in `core/voice.py`: `torchaudio.list_audio_backends = lambda: []` (returns empty list) to let SpeechBrain skip file-backend setup. Session 88 added a file-level patch to pyannote's `io.py`: `getattr(torchaudio, 'list_audio_backends', lambda: ['sox_io'])()` — designed to fall through to `['sox_io']` when the attribute is absent. In pipeline.py's real import order: `core.voice` imports first → installs the runtime patch returning `[]` → the attribute now EXISTS → pyannote's getattr fallback is bypassed → pyannote calls the real function, gets `[]`, hits `backends[0]` at `io.py:214` → IndexError. Smoke test in Session 88 passed only because voice.py wasn't imported before pyannote in that isolated shell. **Fix:** voice.py's monkeypatch now returns `['sox_io']` instead of `[]` — matches the file-level patch sentinel. SpeechBrain treats any non-empty list as "backend available, proceed" (only uses it for file loading, which our ECAPA path never does). Both sites in voice.py updated (module-level + `load_speaker_embedder` guard). **Observability gap closed**: `_load_pyannote_pipeline`'s except block was only logging `{type(e).__name__}: {e}` — the vague "IndexError: list index out of range" told us nothing about which call raised. Added `traceback.format_exc()` to the log so future load failures surface the full stack immediately. Reviewer's Session 89 ask. **Verification:** 3 diagnostics per reviewer's plan — (1) `patch_pyannote_io.py` reports all patches intact, (2) isolated pyannote smoke test passes, (3) pyannote load INSIDE pipeline.py import context now succeeds (was failing before fix). Final confirmation: imported `pipeline` (triggering full real import order), called `_load_pyannote_pipeline()` → pipeline type `SpeakerDiarization`, fallback_count=0. **+0 new tests** (existing `test_diarize_pyannote_error_falls_back_to_ecapa_and_bumps_counter` already covers the fallback path; the conflict was an environment interaction that unit tests can't cleanly reproduce — fixed by the source change itself). **1 pre-existing test updated**: `test_diarize_returns_two_segments_on_speaker_change` was calling the public `diarize()` which now routes to pyannote by default (not the legacy ECAPA backend the test's fake `embed`/`identify` mocks were written for). Changed to call `_diarize_ecapa_valley` directly — semantically correct since the test validates ECAPA-valley's cosine-boundary binary-split behavior, not the public dispatcher's routing. No test-count change (1018 passing). **Phase 2 now genuinely active in pipeline context** — ready for the multi-person re-run. Expected log: `[Voice] pyannote ready — X.Ys` at first diarize call, followed by `[Voice] diarize: pyannote returned N segment(s)` with N>=2 on multi-speaker audio chunks. |

Community-validated workaround: file-level patches to pyannote + speechbrain + huggingface_hub kwarg renames + torch 2.6+ `weights_only` default change. `tests/patch_pyannote_io.py` is an idempotent patcher applying 7 surgical edits (see new "Pyannote dependency maintenance" section below); must be rerun after any `pip install pyannote.audio`. **P2.3 (rename):** `core/voice.py::diarize()` → `_diarize_ecapa_valley()` — legacy 2-speaker binary-split backend kept as fallback. **P2.4 (new backend):** `_diarize_pyannote()` wrapping pyannote's `Pipeline` using the in-memory waveform dict (skips torchcodec / FFmpeg entirely — documented-supported path). Per-segment policy per reviewer's Session 88 contract: `< DIARIZE_MIN_SEGMENT_SECS=0.5s` → drop (ECAPA noise floor), `0.5-1.0s` → keep with `speaker_id=None` (pyannote segmentation preserved but ECAPA attribution skipped below 1.0s minimum embedding window), `≥ DIARIZE_MIN_EMBED_SECS=1.0s` → run `identify()` and attribute via voice_gallery. Empty audio / pyannote failure → fall back to `_diarize_ecapa_valley` when `DIARIZATION_FALLBACK_ON_ERROR=True` (reviewer's default). **New public dispatcher `diarize()`** at module scope routes on `DIARIZATION_BACKEND` (`"pyannote"` default, `"ecapa_valley"` fallback); normalizes return shape to always include `speaker_label` (None on non-pyannote paths) so callers see uniform dicts. **P2.5 (lazy loader):** `_load_pyannote_pipeline()` mirrors `load_speaker_embedder()` — module-level singleton, first-call loads + CUDA transfer, returns None on HF_TOKEN-missing / gated-access denial / import error (no crash at startup on misconfigured envs). Reviewer-requested observability: `[Voice] diarize: {backend} returned {n} segment(s)` log line every call + `_diarize_fallback_count` module-level counter + `get_diarize_stats()` surface for Phase 5 drift detection to spot climbing fallback rate before silent regression. **P2.7-P2.9 + P2.10-P2.12 (tests):** 6 behavioral tests with mocked pyannote Annotation/Segment (no live model, no GPU time in CI): `test_diarize_drops_segments_below_min_segment_secs`, `test_diarize_short_segment_drops_attribution_keeps_label`, `test_diarize_three_speaker_returns_distinct_labels` (the 3+ speaker Phase 2 unlock guard), `test_diarize_empty_gallery_segments_still_have_speaker_label`, `test_diarize_speaker_id_attribution_via_ecapa_gallery`, `test_diarize_pyannote_error_falls_back_to_ecapa_and_bumps_counter`. **Part B (reviewer-flagged critical, same-session):** refactored `pipeline.py:4478` from the legacy `if len(_diar) == 2:` hardcode to `if len(_diar) >= 2:` with span-grouping by `speaker_label` (pyannote often emits multiple adjacent fragments for one speaker's turn after a pause; merging same-label consecutive segments reduces transcribe budget and keeps the brain's `[Name]: text` blocks clean). Multi-speaker emit gate now requires ≥2 non-empty transcripts from the span-transcription pass (preserves the original `_t0.strip() and _t1.strip()` safety invariant in N-segment form). +1 source-inspection regression guard `test_pipeline_consumer_handles_n_segment_diarize_output` asserts both the old hardcode is GONE and the new generalization + span grouping + multi-line gate are all present. Without this Part B, Phase 2's infrastructure would have silently failed on the 3+ speaker audio that motivated the upgrade. **+7 tests (1011→1018).** New config: `DIARIZATION_BACKEND`, `DIARIZATION_FALLBACK_ON_ERROR`, `DIARIZE_MIN_SEGMENT_SECS`, `DIARIZE_MIN_EMBED_SECS`. No classifier-prompt changes — hash still `6950307c4f8d`. **Handoff**: live multi-speaker validation when friends come over (P2 acceptance criteria). Expected log pattern on a 3-speaker utterance: `[Voice] diarize: pyannote returned 3 segment(s)` followed by `[STT] [3 voices] Jagan: "..." / Sarah: "..." / Ali: "..."`. |
| 87 | 2026-04-22 | **VISION_ROADMAP post-P1.7b audit — `_intent_allows` ungrounded-arg bug fix + golden harvest** — reviewer audited the 10 divergence rows produced by Session 86's 3 live runs (5 allows + 5 rejects, 10/10 correct outcomes — Alexa bug officially dead on both rename paths) and caught a real gate bug I had shipped back in Session 80's P1.4 helper. **The bug**: one row showed `update_person_name({'name': 'Kara'})` ALLOWED when the classifier had honestly abstained from extraction (`assign_own_name`, `extracted_value=None`, `conf=0.80`) and the user_text was "Hey, it's not Jagan. I told you to rename my name." — the LLM hallucinated "Kara" from history. User spent 3 subsequent turns correcting back to Gladiator. Root cause: `_intent_allows` had `if extracted_value:` guarding the grounding block, which meant NO grounding check ran when the classifier abstained. Tool args slipped through whatever the LLM proposed. **Fix**: added an `elif arg_key and tool_args.get(arg_key):` branch — when the classifier abstained AND the LLM still proposed a mutation arg, verify the proposed arg itself is present in user_text (NFKC-casefolded substring). Closes the silent-hallucination path. Reviewer's reasoning: rare in theory (requires classifier-abstain AND LLM-hallucinate co-occurring), but the 10-row audit caught one real occurrence — would accumulate silently otherwise, especially as conversation history grows and gives the LLM more material to hallucinate from. **+2 tests**: `test_intent_allows_rejects_ungrounded_arg_when_extracted_value_none` (the Kara case — reject), `test_intent_allows_allows_grounded_arg_when_extracted_value_none` (complementary case where classifier abstained but LLM's arg DOES appear in user_text — allow; guards against over-rejection of legit classifier-abstain renames). **+3 regression_session_86 golden rows**: the Kara ambiguity row (ground truth `casual_conversation` per post-fix semantics), "now tell me my name" (classifier labeled `live_data_query@0.95`, ground truth `casual_conversation` — interesting imperative-read mislabel preserved as calibration signal), "No, I never said Kara, I said call me Gladiator" (positive regression guard — multi-part utterance handled correctly). Golden corpus 146 → 149. **+2 tests (1009→1011).** No classifier-prompt changes — hash still `6950307c4f8d`. **Phase 2 entry cleared**. Reviewer's Path C ratified: start Phase 2 now, let P1.17 flip happen organically. Real_observed subset currently 3/30; Phase 2's multi-person sessions + normal conversations will grow it to threshold over ~1-2 weeks. Dual-gate (classifier primary, regex safety net) is strictly more reliable than regex-alone was for the last week of live use — the Alexa class is provably dead. **Next**: Phase 2.1 — HF_TOKEN + accept pyannote license on HuggingFace + `pip install pyannote.audio==3.1.1` + verify model download/load on GPU. Non-code setup, ~15 min. No code edits until infrastructure is confirmed. |

Auto-confirm scenario test is source-inspection (behavior-level fixture prohibitive given `conversation_turn` entry surface). **3 pre-existing invariant tests updated** (not regressions — source-inspection windows moved): `test_report_identity_mismatch_preserves_prior_person_type_for_best_friend` (widened search from 1500 to the next `elif` boundary), `test_auto_confirm_gated_on_user_self_identification` (now asserts BOTH `_intent_allows` AND `_user_text_gate_passes` present since both branches enforce the G4 invariant), `test_execute_tool_shutdown_returns_signal` (added explicit `user_text="shut down"` since empty user_text no longer auto-passes the stricter P1.7b gate). **Safety rails:** `INTENT_FALLBACK_TO_REGEX=True` stays ON — dual gate. **Prompt hash UNCHANGED** (still `6950307c4f8d` — S84). Two indexes (ts DESC, tool_proposed + ts DESC) for Phase 5 query shapes. `BrainDB.log_intent_divergence()` method is keyword-only (schema evolution safety) and routes through `_safe_commit()` so a racing close doesn't blow up the gate. `BrainDB.get_recent_intent_divergences(limit)` exposes the read surface. **Step 2 — pipeline helper + wiring**. New module-level `_log_intent_divergence()` wraps `_brain_orchestrator._brain_db.log_intent_divergence` with safe access (no-op when orchestrator unset — unit tests and early boot). Exceptions swallowed with a warning log; bookkeeping must not block the user's turn. `_execute_tool` signature gains `intent_sidecar: "dict | None" = None` kwarg (default preserves backward compat for all existing tests). `update_person_name` block now gates through three branches: (a) classifier-available → `_intent_allows()` decides; reject logs reason + returns "rejected", allow logs "allow" + falls through to the rename flow; (b) classifier-unavailable AND `INTENT_FALLBACK_TO_REGEX=True` → legacy `_user_text_gate_passes` decides, logged as `regex_fallback_*`; (c) both unavailable → allow with warning rather than silently drop legit commands. Both `_execute_tool` call sites in `conversation_turn` (safe_calls gather + serial_calls loop) now pass `intent_sidecar=_intent_sidecar`. **Step 3 — 4 scenario tests**: classifier allows → tool fires + divergence=allow; classifier rejects (low conf) → "rejected" + divergence=reject; classifier unavailable + fallback=True → regex decides + divergence=regex_fallback_allow; classifier unavailable + fallback=False (monkeypatched) → allow with warning + divergence=both_unavailable_allow_with_warning. All 4 assert on the exact `gate_decision` string written to the divergence log. **+4 tests (997→1001).** No behavior changes for existing callers — backward-compat kwarg default preserves 100% of prior tests. **Safety rails:** `INTENT_FALLBACK_TO_REGEX=True` stays ON; dual gate = classifier primary, regex secondary. Worst case both reject a legit command → user rephrases (annoying, not broken). **Handoff:** user runs one live rename-correction session ("my name is Jagan" on a stranger session) and verifies (a) divergence row appears in `brain.db.intent_divergences`, (b) gate outcome matches expectation from bench, (c) regex fallback path fires when `intent_sidecar=None` forced. If validation passes → P1.7b replicates the pattern across `report_identity_mismatch` (site 2), `update_system_name` (site 3), auto-confirm promotion (site 5), shutdown (site 4, saved for last — most complex 3-regex fallback chain). |
| 84 | 2026-04-22 | **VISION_ROADMAP P1.6 — narrowed injection defense + shutdown positive anchors** — second bench run (`tests/eval_bench_runs/20260421_195637.json`) showed the S83 fix nailed the dangerous cases (casual precision 0.53→0.79, confirm_identity 0.60→1.00, prompt-injection now `unclear@0.20`) but overcorrected on imperatives: `request_shutdown` recall 1.00 → **0.33**, `unclear` precision 0.75 → **0.23**. 9 legitimate commands ("Shut down", "Turn off", "Stop running", "End session", "Kill the program", "Go offline now", "Bye bye turn off now", "Shut down the system", "Shut down immediately") all classified as `unclear@0.20`. **Root cause:** the S83 INJECTION DEFENSE wording "instructions directed AT you" — the model read "AT you" as "at the system you represent" (since the classifier IS the voice AI from its own POV), so imperative commands got swept into the injection bucket. Kara bug reborn on the shutdown path. **Safety read:** failure mode was false-negative (rejects legit shutdowns) — UX-bad but SAFE, zero false-positive tool fires. **Fix — two-part rewrite of INJECTION DEFENSE clause:** (1) narrow the scope to explicitly target "manipulate YOU (the classifier)" / "YOUR classification" — removes the "at the system" reading; (2) add **both-sided framing** (reviewer's S84 refinement): explicit `INJECTION (classify as unclear, conf < 0.30)` vs `NOT INJECTION (classify normally)` sections, one-sided rules bleed. Also added **positive shutdown anchors** — 5 concrete commands with high-confidence labels ("Shut down" → `request_shutdown` conf ≥ 0.90, etc.) — gives the model counter-gravity so brief imperatives don't default to unclear. **+2 tests (995→997):** `test_injection_defense_scoped_to_classifier` (asserts "classifier" / "YOUR classification" explicit scoping + both-sided "NOT INJECTION" framing), `test_shutdown_positive_examples_present` (≥3 of 5 canonical commands verbatim — counter-examples are the teeth). **Prompt hash chain (S82→S84 is the meaningful window):** baseline `8bde85169f15` → S83 overcorrection `ae5f3e0d6ffb` (deliberately not shipped to stabilize — transient intermediate state, metrics vs this hash alone are not useful for drift analysis) → S84 `6950307c4f8d` (current). The meaningful comparison for Phase 5 drift detection is S82 → S84: BEFORE the fix work vs AFTER. S83 exists only as the "bounced off overcorrection" waypoint. **Next**: user re-runs bench — expected `request_shutdown` recall ~0.90, `unclear` precision ≥0.70, `casual_conversation` precision holds at 0.79+, injection stays `unclear`. Third time's the charm. |

(2) `_intent_allows(tool_name, turn_intent, confidence, extracted_value, user_text, tool_args) → (allowed, reason)` — four-rule validator in short-circuit order: (a) pass-through for tools not in `TOOL_INTENT_MAP` (search_memory etc.), (b) intent-match against the tool's required intent, (c) confidence floor (shutdown uses `INTENT_SHUTDOWN_CONF_MIN=0.80`, all others use `INTENT_CONFIDENCE_MIN=0.75`), (d) when `extracted_value` is non-empty: NFKC-casefolded substring grounding in user_text AND arg-key cross-check (`tool_args[arg_key] == extracted_value`). Returns a short diagnostic `reason` string for the `[Intent]` log line. **Shadow-mode ONLY** — caller must continue using regex gates for dispatch until `INTENT_FALLBACK_TO_REGEX` flips to False at P1.17; validator verdict will be logged side-by-side when P1.7+ wires the call sites. +8 tests in `test_pipeline.py`: happy-path rename (Session 80 Turn 21 "I'd love to call you Atlas"), intent mismatch (Turn 23 goodnight→shutdown), confidence below floor (Turn 19 escape-hatch leak at conf=0.20), shutdown floor strictly higher than general (0.775 admits rename but rejects shutdown), ungrounded extracted_value (classifier hallucinated "Nova"), tool arg mismatch (LLM proposed "Nova" when classifier extracted "Atlas"), **Cyrillic homoglyph rejection** (Kаra with U+0430 vs user's Latin Kara — NFKC does NOT alias the two codepoints, correct behavior), pass-through for unmapped tool. **Three Session 80 behavioral observations documented in CLAUDE.md** under new "Intent Classifier Behavior" section: (a) implicit shutdown phrases classify as `casual_conversation` by design — shutdowns require explicit phrasing; (b) `unclear` escape hatch leaks at ~10% rate (Llama-3.3 picks a specific wrong label at low conf) — not worth tuning, confidence gate catches; (c) NFKC does not alias homoglyphic codepoints — intentional, reject code-point mismatches. **+8 tests (961→969)**. No behavior changes yet — pure helper addition. **Next (P1.5-P1.6)**: golden set (300 pairs) + eval bench with precision/recall/ECE metrics. **Next (P1.7-P1.11)**: wire `_intent_allows()` at 5 call sites behind `INTENT_FALLBACK_TO_REGEX` flag. |
| 79 | 2026-04-22 | **P0 fixes + Phase 1 classifier scope-shrink (mutation-only)** — The 2026-04-22 follow-up session + reviewer audit led to one additional change after the 3 P0 fixes: **`search_web` removed from `TOOL_INTENT_MAP`**. Observation session logged 3 search_web calls and ZERO `[Intent]` log lines. Investigation (pipeline.py:2788 + core/brain.py:1108-1122) showed `search_web` is consumed inline inside `ask_stream` — split out of `raw_tool_calls` and handled in-stream, so its tool_call never reaches the classifier gate in `conversation_turn`. `TOOL_INTENT_MAP["search_web"]` was dead code. Chose Option B (scope shrink) over Option A (wire classifier inside ask_stream): (1) contract mismatch — classifier's grounding rule requires `extracted_value` to literally appear in user_text; `search_web` query is a semantic transformation of user_text, not a substring; (2) threat-model difference — search misfires are routing bugs (wasted Tavily call, small blast radius) whereas rename/shutdown are authorization bugs (DB corruption, large blast radius); regex gate is adequate for the smaller surface; (3) existing gate works — Session 79 line 438 correctly rejected "highest temperature in India today" on the `SEARCH_WEB_LIVE_DATA_PATTERNS` no-live-marker check. Updated `test_tool_intent_map_covers_all_gated_tools` → `test_tool_intent_map_covers_all_gated_mutation_tools` with positive (4 mutation tools required) + negative (search_web must NOT be in map until Phase 1.5) invariants. Deferred re-integration to **Phase 1.5** appendix in VISION_ROADMAP.md, with explicit re-evaluation triggers (mutation classifier stable ≥2wks / `_should_search_web` shows a live miss / golden set ambiguity). **P0 fixes from the 2026-04-22 follow-up live session — JSON-in-TTS leak, short-utterance floor regression, timeout 8→10s** — Session 78's 3 prep items plus the first real classifier observation round surfaced two independent P0s and one orange. Calibration itself looked strong (8/8 classifications correct, 100% per-intent, unclear escape hatch fired exactly once at conf=0.40 as designed). **P0 #1 — main stream speaking raw JSON envelope**: three turns the user heard `'{"content": "...", "turnintent": "livedataquery", "extractedvalue": "...", "confidence": 0.95, "reasoning": "...", "toolcalls": [...]}'` instead of clean speech (Chennai temperature answer, Mumbai Indians opinion, Goodnight casual). Root cause: Session 76's pivot from "JSON mode on main stream" to shadow-classifier made the `<<<STRUCTURED OUTPUT CONTRACT>>>` block in `_build_system_prompt` dead weight — but nobody removed it. Earlier observation sessions hid the leak because every gated-tool turn routed through either Ollama-fallback (rejected rename retries) or canonical-ack (accepted renames), both of which bypass the main stream's raw JSON output. This session had the first "clean main-stream completions" (successful `search_web` + casual convo + goodnight) and the JSON envelope leaked straight into TTS. Also exposed: the model was DROPPING underscores under streaming pressure (`turnintent` not `turn_intent`), further proof the main stream and JSON don't mix. **Fix**: `INTENT_CONTRACT_BLOCK_ENABLED = False` in `core/config.py` — one-line config flip, the flag was designed for exactly this. `<<<STRUCTURED OUTPUT CONTRACT>>>` block still lives in the source for easy re-enable if P1.4's validator ever needs main-stream JSON; the `HEDGED NAMING CONTRACT` block is unaffected (separate flag). The shadow-classifier call is the new intent source; P1.7 validator will consume its sidecar, no more main-stream JSON needed. **P0 #2 — short-utterance floor regression (Item 1 own-goal)**: every `[Voice] Routing:` log in the live session showed `0.00s < 1.0s floor` — including the 88-chunk (~2.8s) "No, my name is not Javan, it's Jagan". Session 78 Item 1 moved the floor from `len(audio_buf) / MIC_SAMPLE_RATE` (buffer length) to `core.audio._last_speech_secs` (speech only), but the publish happened BEFORE the empty-return check in `record_until_silence`. The pipeline's **post-turn addendum probe** (a 3-second listen after every turn looking for continuation speech, usually empty) kept triggering `record_until_silence` with `speech_chunks=0`, which published `_last_speech_secs = 0 * chunk_dur = 0.0` just before returning the empty array — clobbering the main turn's value before the routing gate could read it. Session 78's own `test_last_speech_secs_reset_on_every_recording` asserted the EXACT buggy invariant ("unconditional publish prevents stale state") — the test was wrong and passed only because it tested for the bug. **Two-part fix**. Part A (`core/audio.py`): moved the `_last_speech_secs = speech_chunks * chunk_dur` assignment AFTER the empty-return check — empty probes no longer publish, previous value persists. Part B (`pipeline.py`): added per-listen stashing to prevent a second clobber case (addendum WITH speech publishing its own shorter duration over main's). After the main listen captures `_main_speech_secs = float(getattr(_audio_mod, "_last_speech_secs", 0.0))` immediately; after the addendum probe captures `_addendum_speech_secs` conditional on `addendum_text`; routing now reads `_utterance_secs = _main_speech_secs + _addendum_speech_secs`. Ambient-path branch captures `_main_speech_secs` too (ambient listen published its own value). Tests updated: `test_last_speech_secs_only_published_on_non_empty_recording` asserts the opposite source ordering from the Session 78 version; `test_pipeline_reads_speech_duration_not_buffer_length` updated to assert the accumulator (`_main_speech_secs + _addendum_speech_secs`) is the routing source AND that both captures stash from `core.audio._last_speech_secs` elsewhere in `run()`. **Item 3 — timeout 8s → 10s**: live session showed 1/7 (14%) timeout rate at 8s ("What is your name now?" timed out once). Reviewer's call: raise the timeout OR add retry, not both. Chose raise-first (ship + measure; add retry only if next session still shows >5% rate). `INTENT_CLASSIFIER_TIMEOUT_SECS = 10.0`; updated `test_classifier_timeout_bumped_to_8_seconds` → `test_classifier_timeout_at_least_10_seconds` (lower-bound invariant preserves the progressive-bump history). +0 net tests (1 test rewritten for corrected invariant, 1 test renamed + lower-bound bumped, others unchanged). 961 passing (static; same count as Session 78 end since this session fixed invariants rather than adding coverage). **Handoff to user for next round**: 5-minute verification session BEFORE P1.4 code — 3-4 mixed turns (1 rename, 1 `search_web` live-data, 1 casual, 1 hedge), specifically confirm (a) TTS is clean text on the `search_web` turn, (b) `[Voice] Routing` log shows real durations not 0.00s, (c) classifier precision hasn't drifted after prompt-block flip. Then factory-reset `conversation_log` + `brain.db` (DB has "Javan" polluted from Session 78's regex rejecting "my name is Jagan"). Then P1.4 (`_intent_allows()` validator) — calibration data from this session is sufficient green light. |
| 78 | 2026-04-22 | **VISION_ROADMAP P1.4 prep — 3 reviewer-requested items before next observation round** — Session 77's live run surfaced 3 tuning issues whose fixes were all recommended for landing before the next gated-tool observation session (to ensure clean calibration data for P1.4's `_intent_allows()` validator). **Item 1 (short-utterance floor regression)**: routing call site in `run()` was passing `utterance_duration = len(audio_buf) / MIC_SAMPLE_RATE` — which measures BUFFER length (pre-roll + speech + trailing silence ≈ 1.5–2s), not SPEECH length. That made Session 67 Bug F's `VOICE_ROUTING_MIN_UTTERANCE_SECS=1.0` floor a no-op — a 400ms "yes" was routed as if it were 2.0s. **Root-cause fix**: added module-level `_last_speech_secs: float = 0.0` in `core/audio.py`, published from `record_until_silence` as `_last_speech_secs = speech_chunks * chunk_dur` just before both return paths (mirrors the existing `_last_stt_elapsed_ms` pattern from Session 61). Pipeline now reads it dynamically via `import core.audio as _audio_mod; _utterance_secs = getattr(_audio_mod, "_last_speech_secs", 0.0)` — `from X import Y` would have captured the value at import time (mutable state trap). +3 tests: publication on success, pipeline reads it (routes through `pipeline.run`, not `conversation_turn`), per-recording reset. **Item 2 (classifier unclear escape hatch)**: Session 77 observation logs showed the classifier correctly scoring ambiguous cases at conf=0.00 (calibration working) but STILL emitting a concrete label like `assign_own_name` instead of routing through `turn_intent="unclear"`. Added CRITICAL directive block to `_INTENT_CLASSIFIER_SYSTEM` with an explicit "what's your name?" counter-example demonstrating the correct `unclear` choice when the user is asking a question rather than assigning. +1 test verifying the strong directive is present. **Item 3 (max_tokens reasoning-field truncation)**: 2026-04-22 logs showed reasoning field clipping mid-word (`"so the int..."`) because `INTENT_CLASSIFIER_MAX_TOKENS=300` was too tight for the JSON envelope + 4 required fields + a full-sentence reasoning. Bumped 300 → 500. Free observability win, negligible cost (still a small JSON response, not streamed prose). +1 test asserting `>= 500` with `<= 1000` upper bound guard. **All 3 items landed without behavior changes to the main stream gate** — `INTENT_FALLBACK_TO_REGEX=True` still holds, sidecar is still observation-only. Calibration data quality improves on the next observation round: short social closers ("yes", "thanks") no longer cascade into stranger-session fragmentation, ambiguous queries route to `unclear` as designed, and reasoning field is fully visible in logs for debug. +5 tests (956→961). **Handoff to user for next round**: run 3 more observation sessions deliberately hitting gated-tool paths (2–3 renames, 1 live-data `search_web`, 1 opinion `search_web`, 1 shutdown, 1 question-about-shutdown) to produce ~10 classifier calls. Review `[Intent]` log lines — if `assign_X` vs `unclear` splits cleanly at the 0.75 threshold, P1.4 is ready. |

From now on, you can call me Cara."` That fabrication entered `conversation_log` → the next KAIROS turn saw it as truth and said `"we settled on Drago, right?"`. Classic Session 70 history-contamination, reborn through the offline fallback path. **Fix A**: in `conversation_turn` at the `_all_unreal` Ollama retry branch, detect rejected rename tools (`update_system_name` / `update_person_name`), pull the proposed name (preferring the shadow classifier's `extracted_value` over the LLM's tool arg when available — classifier is the honest ground-truth), and inject a `system_note` into `_ask_offline_safe` that: (a) tells Ollama the rename was ATTEMPTED and REJECTED, (b) explicitly forbids "From now on…" / "you can call me X" / "X it is" phrasings, (c) requires the hedged `"I heard X — is that right?"` template. Subject words correctly distinguish "yourself" (system rename) vs "the user" (person rename). Main-stream HEDGED NAMING CONTRACT unchanged — it already worked; this extends the contract's reach into the fallback path where it was previously blind. **Finding 3**: classifier timeout at 5.0s was too tight for JSON-mode Llama-3.3 calls with conversation context. Bumped `INTENT_CLASSIFIER_TIMEOUT_SECS` to 8.0s. Added module-level counters (`_intent_call_count`, `_intent_timeout_count`, `_intent_parse_fail_count`, `_intent_success_count`) with `get_intent_classifier_stats()` accessor. Timeout log line now shows running count inline (`"total timeouts: N / M calls"`) so base-rate drift is observable without querying the API. +6 tests: 3 for Fix A (wiring, classifier-value preference, subject distinction), 3 for timeout path (bumped value + stats API + log-count inline). **Reviewer's observation-window recommendation held**: P1.4 (`_intent_allows()` validator) deferred until more shadow-mode data accumulates (~20+ gated-tool turns); current sample size = 2 turns from this session. User advised to factory-reset `conversation_log` + `brain.db` before next observation session so the fabricated "From now on, you can call me Drago" turns don't bias calibration data. +6 tests (950→956). No config behavior changes beyond the timeout bump; Fix A is pure wiring. |
| 76 | 2026-04-22 | **VISION_ROADMAP Phase 1.3 — shadow classifier + hedged naming contract** — closes the "structured output is decorative without JSON-mode API flag" finding from the Session 75 live run. Design chosen over the reviewer's "JSON mode on main stream + streaming parser" because Together.ai's JSON mode is incompatible with native tool-calling on Llama-3.3 AND would break streaming TTS (full JSON must arrive before content can be parsed out = 2-3s dead air). **Final shape**: lazy shadow classifier — a separate Together.ai call with `response_format={"type": "json_object"}` + no tools + focused intent-schema prompt, fired only on turns where the main stream proposed a tool in `TOOL_INTENT_MAP` (~5% of turns). Non-gated turns pay zero extra cost. **New module-level `_classify_intent(user_text, conversation_history)`** in `core/brain.py`: focused JSON-mode call, 5s timeout, max 300 tokens, temp 0.1 for deterministic output. Recent conversation (last 4 turns) injected for context. `_INTENT_CLASSIFIER_SYSTEM` prompt documents the 12-label intent enum with examples (Detroit case called out: "do you know the game called Detroit?" → `general_knowledge_query`, NOT `assign_system_name`). **`_parse_intent_sidecar()` validator**: inline JSON parse with brace-salvage fallback for when Llama-3.3 wraps JSON in markdown fences; validates `turn_intent ∈ INTENT_LABELS`, `0.0 ≤ confidence ≤ 1.0`, coerces non-string `extracted_value` / `reasoning` to string. Never raises — returns None on any failure so the gate falls through to regex. **Wired in `conversation_turn`** right before tool dispatch: `any(tc["name"] in TOOL_INTENT_MAP for tc in tool_calls)` → `await _classify_intent(...)` → log as `[Intent] tools=[...] classified=X value=Y conf=0.92 reason=...`. **Gate not enforced yet** (Phase 1.7+); sidecar is observation-only. **`<<<HEDGED NAMING CONTRACT>>>` prompt block (reviewer refinement #2)** added alongside the structured-output contract: tells the 70B that when it proposes `update_system_name` / `update_person_name` / `shutdown`, its spoken content must use hedged phrasing ("I heard Kara — is that right?") not confirmation ("Kara it is!"). Closes the divergence risk where content confirms but the gate rejects. Gated separately via `HEDGED_NAMING_CONTRACT_ENABLED` so it can toggle independently of the main contract. **Config additions**: `INTENT_SHADOW_MODE_ENABLED=True` (observation ON by default), `INTENT_CLASSIFIER_TIMEOUT_SECS=5.0`, `INTENT_CLASSIFIER_MAX_TOKENS=300`, `HEDGED_NAMING_CONTRACT_ENABLED=True`. +10 tests: 6 parser (valid/bad-label/out-of-range-conf/markdown-wrap-salvage/non-json/null-value), 2 contract block (present/covers rename tools), 2 wiring (lazy gate consults TOOL_INTENT_MAP + logs `[Intent]` prefix). Net +4 (some tests may have deduplicated via collection). Total 950 passing. **Next step (P1.4)**: implement `_intent_allows()` server-side validator that consumes the sidecar and enforces the 4-rule gate (intent match + confidence ≥ threshold + extracted_value grounded in user_text + tool_args matches extracted_value). Still behind `INTENT_FALLBACK_TO_REGEX=True` until shadow-mode divergence data confirms calibration. |

The rollout is deliberately staged: observe shape of actual JSON output → build validator → wire gates behind fallback flag → accumulate shadow-mode divergence data (≥1500 classifications, ≤5% divergence) → flip fallback off. +9 tests (937→946). | 74 | 2026-04-22 | **Session 73 post-review fixes (Critical 1-3 + Medium C2)** — reviewer's follow-up pass on Session 73 surfaced four issues that the initial implementation missed. **Critical #1 — grep-invariant hole**: `_kairos_tick` used raw `!= "disputed"` to gate `db.log_turn`. The Step 3 grep-invariant test only scanned for `==`, so the negation form slipped through. This is exactly the "distributed policy = missing policy" antipattern Step 3 was supposed to prevent. **Fix**: routed through `_is_disputed(person_id)`; widened the grep-invariant test to catch BOTH `==` and `!=` polarities. **Critical #2 — auto-clear silently promotes strangers**: `_restore_type = _sess.get("prior_person_type", "known")` — if a future dispute-flip path forgets to capture `prior_person_type`, a stranger session auto-clears as "known" (privilege escalation). **Fix**: fallback now defaults to `"stranger"` (fail-closed); a stranger that loses auth re-establishes via engagement gate on next turn, but a stranger wrongly promoted to "known" would keep those privileges silently. **Critical #3 — multi-word names silently rejected**: `(\w+)` capture stops at spaces, so "call me Sarah Jane" → capture "sarah" → LLM proposes "Sarah Jane" → gate mismatch. **Fix**: added prefix-match accept path in `_user_text_gate_passes` — if `new_value` starts with the captured word AND the remainder of `new_value` appears in `user_text`, accept. Handles "Sarah Jane", "Mary Ann Smith", "Jean-Luc Picard" without regex surgery. Still rejects fabricated multi-word suffixes the user never said. **Medium C2 — voice-only auto-clear threshold tightening**: voice auto-clear trusted the same sensor that triggered the dispute; reviewer correctly noted that 0.70 is the baseline biometric threshold and shouldn't be acceptable alone. **Fix**: new `DISPUTE_AUTO_CLEAR_VOICE_SOLO_MIN = 0.85` for voice-only clear path; `DISPUTE_AUTO_CLEAR_VOICE_MIN = 0.70` stays for face-corroborated path (two independent sensors confirming is stronger than one). `_expire_stale_sessions` picks the appropriate floor based on whether face is co-present. +9 new tests + 2 pre-existing test updates (kairos_tick source-inspection assertions now reference `_is_disputed`). +4 pre-existing auto-clear tests bumped their voice_conf values to ≥ SOLO_MIN since those assumed the old 0.70 voice-only threshold. +9 tests (928→937). No schema migrations. Config additions: 1 (`DISPUTE_AUTO_CLEAR_VOICE_SOLO_MIN`). Pitch-ready. |

Deque fed from the Bug I voice-ID evidence block. +6 tests. **Step 5 — HONESTY POLICY visible-turn exception (Bug D2)**: added bullet telling the LLM to reference visible conversation turns directly when `search_memory` is empty but the answer is in the session history. Fixes the demo-killer where AI said "I don't have details about Chloe" 30s after she introduced herself. +1 test. **Step 6 — `_make_memory_search_fn` dispute skip (Bug D3)**: when caller's session is disputed, tool returns `status="disputed"` with empty facts/excerpts and an explicit hint. Defense-in-depth — don't trust the LLM to follow the `<<<IDENTITY DISPUTED>>>` block alone. Skips BOTH knowledge AND conversation-excerpt paths (reviewer's A4 addition). Uses `_is_disputed` helper. +3 tests. **Total +27 tests (901→928)**. Config additions: 4 (capture-group patterns, `IDENTITY_DENIAL_PATTERNS`, `DISPUTE_AUTO_CLEAR_VOICE_MIN=0.70`, `DISPUTE_AUTO_CLEAR_CONSECUTIVE_TURNS=3`).
