| 72 | 2026-04-22 | **Multi-person + protocol-leak hygiene (Bugs W, X, Y)** — three bugs from the 2026-04-22 live run. Sessions 70-71 fixes confirmed working in run (Bug T gate fired on non-live-data turns at lines 87 and 174; repeat guard didn't trip; no hallucinated tool names). **Bug W (CRITICAL) — thin-stranger silenced by Priority 3 offscreen floor**: Chloe (stranger session, 3/5 voice samples) said "My name is Chloe" — voice scored 0.307 against her own thin profile, holder offscreen → Priority 3 dropped as `ambiguous — low self-match below 0.45 floor`. Subsequent turns 61/62 also dropped; she was effectively silenced. Root cause: `VOICE_ROUTING_SELF_MATCH_OFFSCREEN=0.45` is a poisoning protection calibrated for MATURE speakers, but a bootstrapping stranger's own voice routinely scores 0.30–0.45 against their unstable mean — that's normal profile-warming, not a poisoning signal. **Fix**: profile-maturity-aware gating in Priority 3 — when `_thin_holder = (cur_person_type == "stranger" AND gallery_count < N_INITIAL_VOICE)`, skip the offscreen floor and trust the session. Mirrors what `_effective_switch_threshold` already does for Priority 1. Mature-speaker poisoning protection (the original guardrail intent) stays fully intact. Logs distinct line on relaxation: `Routing: current (thin stranger N/5, offscreen floor skipped, score=X.XXX)`. +4 tests including a regression guard that mature holders still hit the floor. **Bug W-parallel deferred** — session-expired thin stranger can't re-engage voice-only because Priority 2 needs face. Separate design choice; defer until live test confirms within-session fix. **Bug X (HIGH) — LLM speaks "SILENT" protocol token**: line 179 of 2026-04-22 log — TTS spoke literal `"SILENT"` after garbled STT + a Bug-T-blocked search. Same class as Bug H (meta-commentary leak). **Two-layer fix**. Layer 1: extended `_META_COMMENTARY_PATTERNS` in `core/audio.py` with anchored regexes for bare `SILENT` (with optional trailing `.!?`), plus `NO_RESPONSE` / `NO RESPONSE` / `NO-RESPONSE` / `[SILENT]` / `<silent>` variants. Anchored `^...$` so natural sentences ("the room is silent") don't false-positive. Layer 2: removed the global system-prompt instruction telling the LLM to "respond with the literal single word SILENT" for regular turns — that was the leak source. KAIROS path keeps its own SILENT contract via the per-turn `kairos_prompt` (separate code path). New prompt rule: "NEVER emit bare protocol tokens... respond with a natural human phrase like 'I'm not sure what you meant'". +6 tests. **Bug Y (MEDIUM) — cloud-state narration leaked internal terminology**: line 415 of run — `'My cloud connection just came back online, so everything should be smooth sailing now.'` Spoken on `CloudState: SICK → ONLINE` recovery. Found at `pipeline.py:3552-3560`: an Ollama call generated a recovery announcement with a `system_note` containing "Your cloud connection just recovered..." — the LLM faithfully verbalized the infrastructure terminology. **Fix**: suppressed the announcement entirely. Rationale (per reviewer): the user didn't hear when we went SICK (silent Ollama fallback for that turn), so they don't need to hear when we recover. Replaced the Ollama+speak block with a console log: `[Cloud] Recovery TTS suppressed (Bug Y)`. Flag still cleared so we don't re-trigger. +1 test guarding that the leaky template strings stay gone. +11 tests (890→901). No config additions, no schema migrations. |

Applied at BOTH search call sites (streaming `ask_stream` loop AND non-streaming `ask` path). **Tightened from reviewer's spec**: deliberately did NOT block generic `\bdo\s+you\s+(?:have|know|remember)\b` — that would catch legit live-data phrasings like "do you know today's weather?". Added bullet to existing HONESTY POLICY block instructing the LLM to use "you just mentioned X" / "you said earlier" for current-session facts and reserve "I remember that you..." for facts surfaced via `search_memory` or older sessions. Fix: new `SEARCH_QUERY_MIN_CHARS=3` config; `_web_search` now returns a structured `{error, hint}` dict for queries below the threshold (caller surfaces `hint` to the LLM so it routes to training knowledge). Fix: new "Layer 0" filter at the top of `_execute_tool` — if `name not in TOOL_PRIVILEGES`, log `unknown tool, discarded (LLM hallucination)` and return the dedicated `"unknown"` status (distinct from `None` / `"handled"` / `"shutdown"`). `conversation_turn` now tracks `_tool_results` per-tool, computes `_all_unknown`, and on that case (with empty streamed text) retries via Ollama for clean conversational text instead of the error filler. +3 tests. **Bug Q — tool repeat guard never fires across turns**: LLM called `update_system_name('Kara')` on turns 11/15/17/19 with no guard activation. **Root cause A**: `conversation_turn` explicitly `pop`d `_tool_repeat_last` + `_tool_repeat_count` at the top of every user message — defeating the guard's entire purpose (which is precisely to stop cross-turn repeats). **Root cause B**: even when the tool was a no-op, the canonical `"Got it, I'll go by Kara."` ack was written to history; the LLM saw its own prior ack and interpreted it as "I need to confirm again" on the next turn, creating a feedback loop. **Fix Part A**: removed the per-turn `pop` (the `else` branch inside `_execute_tool` already resets state naturally when a different `(name, args)` fires). **Fix Part B**: new `"handled_noop"` return status; `update_system_name` + `update_person_name` return it when the target name already matches current state; `conversation_turn`'s canonical-ack gate is now `_any_tool_effective` + per-tool `_tool_results.get(name) == "handled"` so no-ops no longer emit the ack (and therefore never enter history). **Fix Part C** (LLM alignment): `update_system_name` description adds "CRITICAL: Do NOT call with the name you ALREADY have... The tool is for CHANGES only". +5 tests. 1 pre-existing test updated (`test_execute_tool_unknown_tool_returns_none` → `..._returns_unknown_status`). +12 tests (864→876). Config: 1 addition (`SEARCH_QUERY_MIN_CHARS`). No schema migrations. |
| 69 | 2026-04-21 | **Phase 3 — knowledge-agent hygiene (Bug L + Bugs J/M)** — closes the 2026-04-20 live-run plan. No DB schema destructive changes (one additive column). **Bug L — PromptPref dedup + blacklist (Parts 1+2)**: the 2026-04-20 run produced 4 separate "Prefers brief responses" rows plus `avoid apologizing` inferred from the LLM's own apology in a Bug-N confabulation recovery. **Part 1 (semantic dedup)**: added `embedding BLOB` column to `prompt_prefs` via additive migration; `BrainDB.get_prefs_by_type` / `set_pref_embedding` / `bump_pref_seen` methods expose the dedup surface. The orchestrator's activation loop now embeds the new pref description via the existing `EmbeddingAgent`, compares cosine-sim against each existing pref of the same type (embeds + backfills any pre-migration rows the first time they're touched), and if ≥ `PREF_DEDUP_THRESHOLD` (0.85) bumps the existing row's `sessions_seen` instead of inserting a near-duplicate. E5 vectors are L2-normalized so dot product = cosine. Embedding is backfilled on freshly-inserted rows. **Part 2 (blacklist)**: `PREF_BLACKLIST_PATTERNS` config enumerates mistake-recovery shapes ('avoid apologizing', 'deflect', 'avoid explaining internal mistakes', etc.); activation loop rejects matching descriptions before embedding (saves an LLM call on pure rejects). Regression tests confirm the blacklist covers all 4 documented leak shapes from the run log AND doesn't false-positive on legitimate casual/brief/avoid-So prefs. **Part 3 (revocation detection) deferred** — more design-exploratory than bug-fix work; the reviewer's `_REVOCATION_MARKERS` sketch was speculative. +7 tests (3 blacklist + 4 dedup). **Bugs J + M — unified LLM retry helper**: 2026-04-20 run had `[SocialGraph] Extraction error: 'choices'` (Bug J, KeyError from provider error-shaped JSON) and `[PromptPrefAgent] error: ReadTimeout:` with empty message (Bug M). New module-level `_call_llm_chat(http, messages, *, agent_name, ...)` helper in `core/brain_agent.py` unifies the retry pattern from `ExtractionAgent` / `EmbeddingAgent`: transient errors (`ReadTimeout`, `ConnectTimeout`, `NetworkError`, 5xx) retry with exponential backoff; 4xx (except 429) propagate immediately; response shape validated explicitly (`'choices'` missing → logged + None, never silent); all log paths use `(no detail)` fallback for empty `str(e)`. Migrated 3 agents with documented failure modes: `SocialGraphAgent.extract` (Bug J), `PromptPrefAgent._call_together` (Bug M), `HouseholdExtractionAgent._call_api` (both modes). Remaining agents (InsightAgent, FrictionDetectionAgent, PatternAgent, ContradictionAgent) can migrate incrementally — they don't have documented live-run failures yet, and the helper exists whenever they need it. +7 tests (4 helper behavior + 3 migration audit). +14 tests (850→864). |
| 68 | 2026-04-21 | **Phase 2 — narrative correctness (Bugs N, H, D)** — three independent fixes targeting how the brain responds, not what the pipeline routes. No DB schema changes. **Bug N — confabulation prevention (4-layer defense)**: 2026-04-20 live run had the LLM narrate a fabricated conversation about "the visitor" when the visitor node was actually empty, stitching details from Jagan's own knowledge graph into a plausible-sounding false memory. **Layer 1** — `BriefingAgent.get_briefing` now joins on `conversation_log` and filters strangers by `user_turns >= BRIEFING_VISITOR_MIN_TURNS` (default 2). A phantom / gate-blocked visitor with turn_count=0 no longer triggers "visited and spoke with me" phrasing. **Layer 2+4** — `_make_memory_search_fn` now returns `status` = "empty" / "sparse" / "ok" plus a `hint` field instructing the LLM to hedge when memory is sparse (< `MEMORY_SPARSE_THRESHOLD`, default 2). **Layer 3** — new `<<<HONESTY POLICY>>>` block in `_build_system_prompt` (gated by `HONESTY_POLICY_BLOCK_ENABLED=True`) with explicit rules: "I don't have details about that", never narrate conversations without specific turns, never answer "who was the visitor?" from unrelated facts. +7 tests (2 briefing turn-count filter, 2 honesty-policy block, 3 sparse-signal). **Bug H — meta-commentary leak (3-layer defense)**: 2026-04-20 live run had the LLM speak "No function call is needed for this prompt." aloud. **Layer 1** — new `_META_COMMENTARY_PATTERNS` + `_is_meta_commentary()` in `core/audio.py`; `_clean_for_tts` now returns "" when the whole sentence is pure meta-commentary so Kokoro/Piper never synthesizes it. Patterns narrow enough that "I will tell you about cars" doesn't match. **Layer 2** — added explicit anti-meta instruction to `SYSTEM_PROMPT`: "NEVER narrate your internal reasoning, function calls, tools… respond with the literal single word SILENT and nothing else". **Layer 3** — response-level backstop in `conversation_turn`: if the entire streamed response matches `_is_meta_commentary`, suppress it (response="") so it doesn't pollute history and get imitated next turn. +6 tests. **Bug D — tail-truncation completion retry**: the existing retry fired only for ≤1-word truncations; a long 350-word response with a chopped final fragment ("His bravery and") went unretried and the user heard a mid-sentence cut-off. Now split into **Case A** (≤2 words + no terminator → full-replace via Ollama, `stop_audio()` first to cut the fragment tail) and **Case B** (longer response with non-terminal tail → completion prompt that explicitly forbids repeating what was already said; continuation is appended to `response` so history records the full utterance; NO `stop_audio()` since the original audio already played). Both paths still gate on SSE `finish_reason` (Obs 3) and word/punctuation defense-in-depth. Tail length for termination detection widened from 40→80 chars. Updated pre-existing `test_stream_truncation_detection_in_source` (was encoding `<= 1`). +4 tests. +17 tests (833→850). |
| 67 | 2026-04-21 | **Phase 1 — live-run Bugs F, O, I + BOOTSTRAP voice-vision independence** — four tightly-coupled routing/accumulation fixes landed together (Phase 1 of a 3-phase plan from the 2026-04-20 live run). Run must be live-tested before factory reset. **BOOTSTRAP=20** (was 6 → matches `MAX_VOICE_EMBEDDINGS` ceiling): voice-only speakers can now fully populate their voice profile in a single engagement-gated session without depending on face evidence. Diversity gate (0.85) still filters near-identical samples. New invariant test guards `BOOTSTRAP == MAX_VOICE_EMBEDDINGS`; existing `BOOTSTRAP > MATURE` test still holds. Fixes the root-cause channel coupling behind Chloe's permanent-at-3 problem (Chloe herself needs manual DB refresh). **Bug F (short-utterance floor `VOICE_ROUTING_MIN_UTTERANCE_SECS=1.0`)**: ECAPA-TDNN embeddings below ~1s are noise. New `utterance_duration` kwarg on `_resolve_actual_speaker`; under the floor, routing returns either `(cur_pid, "current")` or `(None, "short_utterance_skip")`. Call site at pipeline.py:~3448 computes `len(audio_buf) / MIC_SAMPLE_RATE` and passes it; new `short_utterance_skip` branch in the dispatch drops the turn silently. Prevents phantom stranger sessions from short social closers ("Thank you", "Yes", "Hey") that cascaded into visitor_log → BriefingAgent fabrication. +4 tests. **Bug O (face-assist floor `VOICE_ROUTING_FACE_ASSIST_MIN=0.42`)**: Priority 2's face+voice-agree shortcut was too permissive — a 0.30 midrange match with the claimed face in frame was treated as "confident switch". In multi-person/phone-audio scenarios the visible face isn't necessarily the speaker. Wasim's phone audio scored 0.314 against Jagan with Jagan's face visible → mis-attributed to Jagan's pid. Now Priority 2 requires voice ≥ 0.42 even with face-agreement. Below that, log as "ambiguous — weak voice despite face in frame" for diagnostics. Updated pre-existing `test_resolve_mid_range_face_confirms_switch` (it encoded the Bug O behavior — a 0.35 face-agree case). +3 tests including invariant (`MIDRANGE_SWITCH_MIN < FACE_ASSIST_MIN < SPEAKER_SWITCH_THRESHOLD`). **Bug I (accumulation ordering + diagnostic)**: every Jagan turn in the second half of the 2026-04-20 run refused accumulation with `voice_n=20, voice_conf=?` (invisible before). Step 1 diagnostic: refusal log now includes `voice_conf={ev.get('voice_match_conf', 0.0):.2f}`. Step 2 fix: the conversation_turn voice-ID result now writes `voice_match_conf` + `voice_last_heard_ts` to `identity_evidence` in the `_v_pid and _v_pid in _active_sessions` block (pipeline.py:~3437), BEFORE `_resolve_actual_speaker` and accumulation decisions consume it. Without this, a freshly-reopened mature session's Path B saw the stale seed value (0.0) and refused despite voice_n=20. Source-inspection test guards the write-precedes-routing ordering. +3 tests. +11 tests (822→833). |
| 66 | 2026-04-20 | **Obs 1–4 hardening (reviewer's post-Session 65 pass)** — four improvements (not bug fixes). **Obs 2 (`_safe_commit` hardening)**: replaced brittle English string-match (`"no transaction" not in str(e).lower()`) in `BrainDB._safe_commit` with deterministic `if self._conn.in_transaction: self._conn.commit()` — prevents the error at source instead of swallowing post-hoc, survives SQLite version/locale changes. Renamed + replaced 2 existing tests + added 1 new (now asserts actual persistence via fresh-connection read-back). **Obs 1 (voice gallery DB fallback)**: out-of-process `delete_person()` from dashboard/CLI could leave `_voice_gallery_sizes` cache stale. Added `FaceDB.count_voice_embeddings(person_id)` (O(log n) via existing index); hardened `_open_session` to prefer live DB count over cache + opportunistically repair divergent entries; added cache reconciliation pass to `_dream_loop` (also reloads embeddings for divergent pids so `voice_mod.identify()` can't keep matching against vanished means). New module-level `_face_db_ref` set in `run()` (and on factory-reset) gives `_open_session` DB access from module scope. +4 tests. **Obs 3 (`finish_reason` plumbing)**: stream-truncation retry heuristic could still misfire on legit single-word non-terminator replies like "Hmm" / "Wow". Added `("finish", reason)` terminal event to `_stream_together_raw`, forwarded through `ask_stream` (captured from initial stream, overwritten by search/memory follow-up streams so the latest stream wins). Fallback path emits `"stop"` since `_ask_together` completes. Pipeline `_token_gen` captures via mutable `_stream_finish_reason[0]` shared with outer scope; retry gate now AND-combines word-count + punctuation + `_truncated = _finish in ("length", "content_filter", None)`. `finish_reason="stop"` now short-circuits the retry even for single-word unterminated replies. +3 tests (behavioral: asserts `ask_stream` yields terminal finish event; source-inspection: retry gate consults `_truncated`), updated existing test. **Obs 4 (voice-session observability)**: after Bug B suppressed the misleading "person left frame" log for voice-only entries, the 5–30s silent window between `SCENE_STALE_SECS` (5s) prune and `VOICE_SESSION_TIMEOUT` (30s) close had no signal. Added `[Voice] {name} no longer heard — session expires in Ns` log when a voice entry ages out with a still-active session (silent when session already closed — `_close_session` already logs that). +2 tests. +10 tests (812→822). |
| 65 | 2026-04-20 | **Phase 2 reliability fixes (Bugs 2, 5, 6) + Phase 3 (Bug 3)** — continuation of the 2026-04-20 live-run findings. **Bug 2 (SQLite transaction error)**: Added `traceback.print_exc()` to `_process_turn` error handler so the next occurrence captures the stack (was silent previously). Added `BrainDB._safe_commit()` helper that swallows only "cannot commit - no transaction is active" (harmless race between concurrent session-end tasks) while re-raising any other `OperationalError` so real DB problems aren't masked. `sqlite3.Connection.commit` itself is read-only so we can't monkey-wrap it; call sites suspected of racing use the helper instead. **Bug 6 (ExtractionAgent silent ReadTimeout)**: `[ExtractionAgent] error: ReadTimeout:` logged empty message because `str(httpx.ReadTimeout)` is often empty. Rewrote `_call_together` with retry pattern mirroring `EmbeddingAgent` (Session 24 A8): `EXTRACT_MAX_RETRIES=2` exponential-backoff retries on `ReadTimeout`/`ConnectTimeout`/`NetworkError`, no retry on 4xx (propagates with body excerpt), non-transient exceptions log with `(no detail)` fallback for missing messages. **Bug 5 (stream truncation double-speak)**: "Hello!" (legit short reply) was triggering the Ollama retry; user heard two TTS clips back-to-back. Tightened heuristic to `len(words) ≤ 1 AND not ends_with_terminal_punctuation`. Added `stop_audio()` before retry `speak()` so the original fragment's tail doesn't overlap. **Bug 3 (null relationship leak)**: `CROSS_PERSON_HYPOTHESIS` nudge template had `(f" (possibly your {rel})" if rel else "")` which let the literal strings `"null"` (Kuzu unset) and `"None"` (JSON-serialized Python None) through. Both sites (graph match + fuzzy match) tightened to reject null-ish strings explicitly. 6 regression tests (3 for ExtractionAgent retry, 2 for `_safe_commit`, 1 source-inspection for stream-retry guard). +6 tests (806→812). |
| 64 | 2026-04-20 | **Phase 1 live-run fixes (Bugs A+B+C)** — three architectural bugs exposed by the 2026-04-20 live run with voice-only stranger "Chloe". **Bug C (false face evidence)**: at stranger engagement-gate pass, pipeline.py:3519+ was writing `face_match_conf=0.50, anti_spoof_live=True` to `identity_evidence` regardless of whether a face embedding was actually captured. The face-embedding-store guard had an `if` block but the evidence write was one level below. Now split into `_face_captured` flag + two branches: with-face (full witness evidence) vs voice-only (bootstrap credits only, zero face evidence). Brain's `<<<IDENTITY EVIDENCE>>>` verdict no longer lies about voice-only speakers. **Bug A (voice-only maturity gap)**: `N_INITIAL_VOICE_BOOTSTRAP=3 < VOICE_ACCUM_MATURE_SAMPLE_COUNT=5` arithmetic gap meant a voice-only stranger exhausted bootstrap credits at sample 3, then Path A (no face) + Path B (<5 samples) both failed permanently. Worse, session re-open wiped `identity_evidence.voice_sample_count=0` even though DB voice rows persisted. **Two-part fix**: Part 1 — `_open_session` now hydrates `voice_sample_count` from the DB-backed `_voice_gallery_sizes` cache so re-opened sessions carry prior progress forward. Part 2 — raised `N_INITIAL_VOICE_BOOTSTRAP=3→6` so first-ever session can reach maturity within budget. Invariant test guards that BOOTSTRAP > MATURE in config. **Bug B (face+voice source conflation)**: `_persons_in_frame` was being populated by BOTH face recognition (`_background_vision_loop`) AND voice ID (`source="voice"` entries added at pipeline.py:3263+), but four consumers treated all entries as face-present: (1) Priority 2 `face+voice agree` shortcut in `_resolve_actual_speaker`, (2) Priority 3 offscreen-floor symmetry, (3) scene heartbeat `[Vision] names` line, (4) "person left frame" log emitting voice_score as "conf". Result: Chloe (voice-only) triggered confident switch_enrolled and was rendered `[Vision] Chloe` despite never being on camera. Fix: new `_face_in_frame(pid, persons_in_frame)` helper checks `source != "voice"`; substituted at all 4 sites; face-scan writer and update path both tag `source="face"`; face scan upgrades source from "voice" to "face" if later confirmed on camera. 8 regression tests (+8, 798→806). |

| 59 | 2026-04-19 | **Anti-spoof preprocessing bug (100% rejection of real faces)** — Session 58 live run showed every real-face frame rejected with `probs=[0.029, 0.016, 0.955] argmax=2 live_prob=0.016` — zero variance, not a threshold issue. Root cause: my Session 52 implementation of `AntiSpoofChecker.is_live()` applied `.div(255.0)` to the input tensor. But upstream minivision-ai's `data_io/functional.py:to_tensor()` explicitly does NOT divide by 255 for numpy inputs — the `.div(255)` line is commented out with note `# modify by zkx`. MiniFASNet was trained on raw [0, 255] float values. Feeding it [0, 1] values landed every real face at class 2 (replay) with 95%+ confidence — exactly the observed bug. Fix: removed `.div(255.0)` from the tensor construction (1 line). Verified with a skin-toned synthetic patch: `probs=[0.384, 0.459, 0.157] argmax=1 verdict=LIVE` — class 1 now wins on face-like inputs. Diagnostic `[Anti-spoof]` print left in place so the user can verify on their real face and tune the threshold back up from the temporary 0.3. +1 line change (no new tests — existing mock-based tests use synthetic logits which are unaffected by input normalization). 770 still passing. |
| 58 | 2026-04-19 | **Kuzu self-heal (pipeline-blocking crash fix)** — prior session's live test hit `IndexError: invalid unordered_map<K, T> key` from Kuzu native init at `GraphDB.__init__`, bricking pipeline startup. Root cause: faces/ had a mismatched state (faces.db reset but brain_graph not wiped — 14:21 .wal timestamp vs 21:46 db timestamp). The crash was structurally unrecoverable because `__init__` raised before `_ensure_graph_sync()` had a chance to rebuild from SQLite. Immediate fix: renamed `faces/brain_graph` → `faces/brain_graph.broken` (and `.wal`) so Kuzu creates a fresh directory. Hardening: wrapped `kuzu.Database()` in try/except — any open failure now wipes the path + retries once; knowledge rows in brain.db are the source of truth, so `_ensure_graph_sync()` + `rebuild_entity_from_knowledge()` restore the graph automatically. If the retry also fails (disk-full, perms), the second exception propagates honestly. Added regression test `test_graphdb_recovers_from_corrupt_kuzu_dir` using monkeypatched `kuzu.Database` to simulate the IndexError on first call. +1 test (769→770). |

Rewrote `AntiSpoofChecker` in `core/vision.py` to load both `.pth` via `torch`, ensemble softmax probabilities across the two models (matches upstream predict flow), ` argmax==1 & conf≥threshold` → live. No new pip deps (torch already present). Old `silent_face_anti_spoofing` import removed. Updated 7 AntiSpoofChecker tests in `test_vision_v1v4.py` to mock `_models` list + fake `forward()` returning crafted logits. Gallery self-update path (Session 51 Finding 1) is now unblocked — `recognition_update` writes get real liveness verification. 751 tests still passing. |
| 51 | 2026-04-19 | Uncle-false-match incident fixes (Findings #1–#8 from issues_to_work.md). #4 Anti-spoof `PASSED` log now gates on `checker.available` (prints `DISABLED` when liveness unavailable — no more lying log). #6 HouseholdAgent silent `except Exception:` now prints `{type}: {exc}` + traceback. #1 Gallery anti-poisoning: `SELF_UPDATE_THRESHOLD` 0.32→0.45 (raises confidence floor), `SELF_UPDATE_CENTROID_MIN=0.55` centroid-distance gate in `add_embedding` for `recognition_update` source (rejects outliers at write time), `recognition_update` path now requires live anti-spoof (fail-closed). #3 `_resolve_actual_speaker` Priority 3 now has unconditional voice floor (`v_score < 0.30` → ambiguous, even when face co-visible) so poisoned vision can't disable voice cross-check. #2A update_person_name tool description softened — speaker's claim OVERRIDES the sensor; handler flips known sessions to `person_type="disputed"` instead of auto-renaming (protects known identities from corruption). #2B new `report_identity_mismatch` LLM tool with `reason` param — flags session disputed when speaker contradicts sensor without giving replacement name. #2C `_build_system_prompt` SENSORS block now surfaces `recognition_conf` as high/medium/low confidence labels, and injects `<<<IDENTITY DISPUTED>>>` block when session is disputed so brain treats speaker as unknown and avoids stored facts. #5 `BrainOrchestrator._disputed_persons` set + `mark_disputed`/`clear_disputed` API; `_process_turn` skips extraction while pid is disputed (prevents polluting either person's knowledge with contradictory facts). #7 `SCHEMA_NORM_THRESHOLD` 0.95→0.97 + `SCHEMA_NORM_DISTINCT_FAMILIES` config (name/presence/time families); `_distinct_schema_families` helper blocks cross-family merges even when embeddings score similar (fixes `presence ← former_name` bug). #8 `FaceDB.prune_stale_stranger_voice(days)` + `STRANGER_VOICE_TTL_DAYS=3` — prunes stranger voice rows that never reached N_INITIAL_VOICE samples, wired into dream loop alongside existing stranger face TTL. +17 tests (734→751). |

## Methodology — structured audits vs reactive patching

Reactive patching surfaces ~30% of an invariant's violations.
Structured audits surface ~100%.

Established empirically by P0.4 (silent excepts: 22 sites surfaced via AST audit vs reactive findings) and confirmed by P1.A1-slice (9 layering violations: 7 new beyond the 2 previously known reactively, a 4.5× discovery ratio).

Implication for P1 architectural work: when an invariant is worth enforcing, schedule the structured audit. Don't budget the work as "fix the reactive findings and call it done." When in doubt, audit; don't react.

Future P1 items where this matters most: P1.A4 (service decomposition), P1.A8 (single SQLite split), and any future invariant that scans for boundary violations.

Every behavior-change P0/P1 cycle has a mandatory Phase 0 with grep-verified findings reported BEFORE any test code is written. Skip this and the rework cost exceeds the Phase 0 cost. Validated empirically across P0.2/P0.3/P0.4/P0.5 — each Phase 0 saved 4-6 hours of Step 1 rework.

**Inverse-check discipline (P0.5 empirical validation):** When a structural invariant uses an enumerated method tuple (`PAIRED_WRITE_METHODS`, `VOICE_GALLERY_METHODS`, etc.), ALWAYS pair it with an inverse check. Forward check: every method in tuple follows the pattern. Inverse check: every method matching the pattern IS in the tuple. Without the inverse, a future method that doesn't get registered silently slips through. P0.5 validated empirically: `test_all_paired_write_sites_are_in_tuple()` (AST scan for FAISS write markers) caught `prune_outlier_embeddings` as a real violation — bare `_conn.commit()` + `_rebuild_faiss()` with no lock, no transaction, no sentinel — that would have shipped as production code. "+30 min for inverse check" caught a 7th bug from one P0 cycle. Carries forward to every future PAIRED_WRITE_METHODS-style invariant including P0.X's Kuzu equivalent.

---

## Layering wrapper conventions

When pipeline.py needs access to a core class's owned state, the owning class exposes:

- **Property** — for read-only state exposure with no side effects.
  Example: `BrainOrchestrator.brain_db` (returns the held BrainDB instance).
- **Method** — for side-effecting operations.
  Example: `FaceDB.close()` (idempotent close with `# CLEANUP:` annotation inside).

Don't refactor properties to `get_X()` methods. Property semantics correctly reflect "expose owned state"; method semantics correctly reflect "perform action."

Both shapes are codified in `tests/test_layering_invariants.py::FORBIDDEN_LAYERING_ACCESSES` as the patterns the structural invariant test rejects.

---

## Coding Standards

- No changes without Jagan's explicit go-ahead. Discuss first, always.
- Don't over-engineer. Only make the change requested — nothing else.
- Never silent `except Exception: pass` — always log at minimum.
- No new utility modules for one-off functions. Inline it or add to the most relevant existing module.
- All blocking I/O → `loop.run_in_executor(None, fn)`.
- All settings → `core/config.py` only. No hardcoded magic numbers elsewhere.
- `add_embedding()` return value must be checked at all call sites.
- `delete_person()` always rebuilds FAISS — never call `_conn.execute` DELETE on embeddings directly.
- Close DB connections in tests: `db._conn.close()` before tmp dir cleanup.
- Run `pytest` after every batch of changes. Confirm full suite passes before session end (1273 as of Session 118).
- **No test may touch real production paths.** Tests that exercise destructive operations (`wipe_all`, file deletes, schema migrations, DB inits with default paths) MUST monkeypatch the relevant module-level path constants (`core.db.DB_PATH`, `BRAIN_DB_PATH`, `FAISS_INDEX_PATH`, `GRAPH_DB_PATH`, `FACES_DIR`) to a `tmp_path`. If a test comment says *"can't redirect without monkey-patching"* — that's a bug, not a documented limitation. Fix it before merging. **Historical bug (Session 122, 2026-04-28):** the original Spec 1 acceptance test for "factory reset doesn't touch classifier DB" called the real `wipe_all()` against the real `faces/` dir and silently deleted enrolled-face data, conversation history, brain.db, and Kuzu graph on every pytest run for several days. After the fix, the test still verifies the same property (classifier DB survives) but with zero side effects on production.

---

## Intent Classifier Behavior (Session 80 observations)

- **Implicit shutdown phrases** (e.g. "goodnight, I'm heading to bed") classify as `casual_conversation`, NOT `request_shutdown`. **By design** — shutdowns require explicit phrasing ("shut down", "turn off", "power down"). Session 80 observation documented as intended behavior; the conservative classifier + strict intent-match gate produces the safer outcome (no implicit shutdowns). Users who want the AI to stop must phrase explicitly.
- **"Unclear" escape hatch leaks at ~10%**: Llama-3.3 occasionally picks a specific (wrong) label with low confidence instead of routing to `unclear` per the CRITICAL directive. Not worth another prompt-tuning pass — the confidence floor (0.75 general, 0.80 shutdown) catches these regardless. Dual-gate design is robust to this failure mode.
- **Cyrillic homoglyph handling**: NFKC normalization in `_intent_allows()` does NOT alias Cyrillic а (U+0430) to Latin a (U+0061) by design. If the classifier extracts a spoofed variant AND the tool_args use the same spoofed variant, grounding fails against the user_text (Latin). Correct behavior — reject code-point mismatches in tool actions.

---

## Pyannote vendoring (P0.R5, 2026-05-23)

Pyannote 3.3.2 + speechbrain 1.0.3 ship as forked git repos under the `HungryFingerss` GitHub organization. The 7 patches that previously required runtime application via `tests/patch_pyannote_io.py` (deleted at P0.R5 closure) now live directly in the fork commits.

**Forks:**
- `github.com/HungryFingerss/pyannote-audio` @ `4978441ee45d1feaea6ce7db7ccf5e67f76a31f8` (3.3.2 base + 9 patches: P0.R5 6 [torchaudio 2.9+ compat + huggingface_hub kwarg + weights_only] + Pre-P1 Bundle 1 3 [pkg_resources→pkgutil.extend_path in pyannote/__init__.py + obsolete setuptools>=38.3 check removal in setup.py + pyscaffold setup_requires removal in setup.cfg; all for setuptools>=81 compat])
- `github.com/HungryFingerss/speechbrain` @ `a9b05847aca696b7eb28dd47c6276afcb2bc14d4` (1.0.3 base + 1 patch: torch_audio_backend list_audio_backends rewrite)

Both forks live on the `dog-ai/<version>-patches` branch off the corresponding upstream version tag. Branch lineage stays clean off the tag so upstream merges are mechanically rebaseable.

**Dependency declarations** (in `requirements.txt`):

    pyannote.audio @ git+https://github.com/HungryFingerss/pyannote-audio.git@4978441ee45d1feaea6ce7db7ccf5e67f76a31f8
    speechbrain @ git+https://github.com/HungryFingerss/speechbrain.git@a9b05847aca696b7eb28dd47c6276afcb2bc14d4

Pip resolves both git URLs at install time and pins the exact SHA. No more post-install patch script required.

**Patches carried by the pyannote fork (P0.R5 6 patches + Pre-P1 Bundle 1 3 patches = 9 distinct patches; P0.R5 6 were originally Patches 1+2+3+3-cont+5+6+7 in the deleted `tests/patch_pyannote_io.py`):**

| File | Patch | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `pyannote/audio/core/io.py` | `-> torchaudio.AudioMetaData:` → `-> object:` | Type annotation only; class removed in torchaudio 2.9 |
| `pyannote/audio/core/io.py` | `torchaudio.list_audio_backends()` → `getattr(…, lambda: ['sox_io'])()` (2 sites) | API removed; our audio path is in-memory tensors |
| `pyannote/audio/core/pipeline.py` | `use_auth_token=use_auth_token,` → `token=use_auth_token,` | huggingface_hub kwarg rename |
| `pyannote/audio/core/inference.py` | same kwarg rename | same reason |
| `pyannote/audio/core/model.py` | same kwarg rename × 2 sites | same reason |
| `pyannote/audio/core/model.py` | `weights_only=False` to `pl_load(...)` + `Klass.load_from_checkpoint(...)` | torch 2.6+ default flip; pyannote checkpoints are HF-gated (trusted source) |
| `pyannote/audio/tasks/segmentation/mixins.py` | `from torchaudio import AudioMetaData` → try/except stub | Import must not crash; stub only hit on training path (we run inference) |
| `pyannote/audio/utils/protocol.py` | same `list_audio_backends()` fallback | same cause, different file |
| `setup.py` (Pre-P1 Bundle 1, 2026-05-28) | DELETED `from pkg_resources import VersionConflict, require` import + DELETED obsolete `require("setuptools>=38.3")` check | setuptools >= 81 (2025-07-22) removed pkg_resources from auto-install; pip's isolated build env hits ModuleNotFoundError at setup.py line 5 → wheel build fails. The setuptools>=38.3 check itself is obsolete (released 2018, long pre-dates Python 3.9 minimum). Modern PEP 517 build envs handle build-system setuptools via pyproject.toml. |
| `pyannote/__init__.py` (Pre-P1 Bundle 1, 2026-05-28) | `__import__("pkg_resources").declare_namespace(__name__)` → `from pkgutil import extend_path; __path__ = extend_path(__path__, __name__)` | Stdlib-only namespace package declaration; semantically equivalent. Required for RUNTIME compatibility with setuptools >= 81 venvs (where pkg_resources is no longer auto-available). |
| `setup.cfg` (Pre-P1 Bundle 1 follow-up, 2026-05-28) | REMOVED `setup_requires = pyscaffold>=3.2a0,<3.3a0` declaration | pyscaffold imports pkg_resources via entry_points; when setuptools' egg_info command runs walk_revctrl() in modern build envs (setuptools >= 81), it enumerates all installed entry_points and tries to load pyscaffold's, which fails. pyscaffold is a project-template / maintenance tool for the pyannote authors — NOT required to build the wheel. setup() in setup.py uses setuptools.find_packages directly. |

**Patch carried by the speechbrain fork (1):**

| File | Patch | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `speechbrain/utils/torch_audio_backend.py` | `torchaudio.list_audio_backends()` → `getattr(…, lambda: ['sox_io'])()` (2 sites) | speechbrain imports torchaudio → crash at module load without this |

**Upstream-merge procedure** (when pyannote or speechbrain releases a new version):

1. `git fetch upstream` in the fork repo
2. `git merge upstream/main` (or target tag)
3. Manually re-apply the dog-ai patches (rebase onto upstream changes)
4. Commit + tag the new patched commit
5. Bump SHA in `requirements.txt`
6. Run `pip install --force-reinstall --no-build-isolation -r requirements.txt`
7. Verify `from pyannote.audio import Pipeline` works without any runtime patch
8. Run `pytest tests/test_p0_r5_vendor_forked_pyannote.py` to confirm all 9 anchors still pass

**Install-time gotcha:** speechbrain 1.0.3's `setup.py` imports `pkg_resources`, which `setuptools>=81` removed by default. Install with `setuptools<81` in the venv OR pass `--no-build-isolation` so pip's build env inherits the venv's older setuptools. The error surfaces as `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'pkg_resources'` during the speechbrain wheel build.

**GitHub-availability assumption + operator mitigation:**

External forks create a deployment dependency on github.com availability + fork-repo persistence. If GitHub goes down or a fork is accidentally deleted or made private, `pip install -r requirements.txt` will fail with a git clone error.

Mitigation: operators should maintain a local clone of each fork on the production host. If a fork repo is lost, re-create from local clone + push to a new URL + bump SHA in `requirements.txt`. For multi-host deployments, consider mirroring forks to a self-hosted git server or vendoring source directly (future P0.R5.X follow-up if the pattern recurs).

**Out-of-scope at P0.R5 — kept in `core/voice.py` runtime wrapper:**

`core/voice.py::load_speaker_embedder()` retains a runtime `hf_hub_download` wrapper that strips `use_auth_token=` from speechbrain's internal calls. Speechbrain 1.0.3 still passes the removed kwarg; the wrapper transparently pops it before delegating. This patch was NOT in the deleted `tests/patch_pyannote_io.py` and is NOT in the speechbrain fork — it's a runtime-only workaround that survives P0.R5 by design. Future cycle may move it into the speechbrain fork.

**Fallback (if forks stale + patches don't apply to a new upstream):**

SpeechBrain's built-in spectral-clustering diarization recipe is the Option D fallback per Session 88. SpeechBrain is already in our venv for ECAPA-TDNN; the recipe trades off ~2-3 percentage points of DER worse than pyannote but eliminates the upstream-compat brittleness.

**Pinned version:** `pyannote.audio==3.3.2` + `speechbrain==1.0.3`. Do NOT bump to pyannote 4.0.x without re-evaluating torchcodec/FFmpeg viability on dev+Jetson.

---

## Classifier graph (Spec 1, 2026-04-27)

The pure-graph classifier's data foundation. Lives at `data/`, separate from `faces/` — system intelligence, NOT personal data. Spec 2 (the actual graph classifier) consumes it; Spec 1 just builds the seed.

**Files:**
- `core/classifier_db.py` — schema, migrations, audit log, read/write API. Single source of truth for all writes.
- `data/classifier_scenarios_seed.jsonl` — committed to git. Built once via the bootstrap pipeline; ships with KaraOS.
- `data/classifier_scenarios.db` — gitignored. Built from seed on first boot (Spec 2's responsibility). Accumulates live outcome supervision data.
- `data/classifier_audit_log.jsonl` — gitignored. Per-deployment append-only log of every counter change / quarantine.
- `data/classifier_snapshots/` — gitignored. Daily backups, 30-day retention.
- `bootstrap/classifier/` — 6-stage offline pipeline + ~100 hand-authored scenarios + run_all orchestrator. Run once, manually, with `TOGETHER_API_KEY` set. ~$1.05 cost, ~15-20 min. Read `bootstrap/classifier/README.md` for the run procedure.

**Factory-reset semantics (load-bearing):**
- `core/db.wipe_all()` ONLY touches `faces/` and `sim_session_state.json`. The `data/` dir is intentionally outside its scope.
- A factory reset wipes who-Jagan-knows. The classifier graph is what-the-system-has-learned-about-language; that survives by design.
- Test `test_factory_reset_does_not_touch_classifier_db` enforces this invariant.

**What's in the seed (post-bootstrap):**
- ~1,500-1,700 abstracted scenarios from Cornell Movie-Dialogs + DailyDialog + EmpatheticDialogues, classified by 70B into the production label space, name+place stripped via spacy NER, embedded via E5.
- ~100 hand-authored scenarios (`bootstrap/classifier/hand_authored_scenarios.py`) covering the 10 high-stakes intent categories from Sessions 71-117 (request_shutdown, question_about_shutdown, assign_*_name, confirm_identity, deny_identity, live_data_query, general_knowledge_query, casual_conversation, direct_address_to_person).
- All embeddings dim-1024 float32 from `intfloat/multilingual-e5-large-instruct`. Embedding model ID is locked in `db_metadata`; switching models requires a re-embed pass (see Spec 1's "embedding model versioning" notes).

**Held out (DO NOT add to bootstrap):** Friends, AMI, MELD, EmotionLines. Including them would leak into `published-papers-tests/` benchmarks.

**Schema versioning:** `schema_migrations` table tracks every applied migration. `label_evolution` table maps deprecated labels at query time. `active` column quarantines bad rows instead of deleting. `audit_log` is append-only.

Continuous-evaluation tooling on top of the bench harness (S82), golden corpus (S81), and `intent_divergences` table (S85). Pure observability — none of this affects production behavior.

### Weekly (every Monday)

```bash
python tests/eval_weekly.py
```

Runs the golden-set bench, persists the run, and compares to the most recent prior run. Queries `intent_divergences` for the last 7 days (configurable via `EVAL_WEEKLY_DIVERGENCE_LOOKBACK_DAYS`). Prints a markdown report covering: per-intent precision/recall drift, top low-confidence gate decisions, recent rejections (for false-reject review), and shadow samples (`mode='shadow'`).

Add `--alert` to exit non-zero when any per-intent precision drops by `EVAL_WEEKLY_ALERT_PRECISION_DROP_PP` percentage points or more (default 5.0). Suitable for cron / CI integration.

Action items: if any per-intent precision drops by ≥5pp, investigate before the next live session.

### Quarterly (every ~3 months)

```bash
# Step 1: export 20 random stratified golden rows for review
python tests/golden_set_drift.py --mode export

# Step 2: review the exported markdown manually (15-30 min)

# Step 3: feed the filled markdown back
python tests/golden_set_drift.py --mode compare \
  --filled tests/golden_set_drift_YYYY-MM-DD.md
```

Detects whether stored ground-truth labels are still correct as user phrasings + the world evolve. Drifted rows get either a re-label, a `regression_session_<n>_relabel` companion row, or marked `legacy_synthetic` if obsolete. **Drift detection is a judgment call** — the script just makes the human review efficient.

### After every live canary

Inspect new `intent_divergences` rows since last review. Specifically:

- Any `mode='gate' AND gate_decision LIKE 'reject%'` rows: was the rejection correct?
- Any new patterns in `mode='shadow'` samples (1% of production turns sampled passively): did the classifier behave honestly on edge cases?
- Add new rows to `tests/golden_intent.jsonl` for any divergences worth preserving as regression guards (tag `regression_session_<n>`).

### Canary shadow sampler

`pipeline.conversation_turn` calls `_classify_intent` on a 1% canary sample of every turn (gated by `SHADOW_SAMPLE_RATE` + `SHADOW_SAMPLE_ENABLED` kill switch) and writes the result to `intent_divergences` with `mode='shadow'`. This catches drift on turns that never trigger a tool gate. Wrapped in try/except so a sampling failure can't break a turn — production behavior is unaffected by shadow-mode failures.

To disable temporarily: flip `SHADOW_SAMPLE_ENABLED = False` in `core/config.py`.

---

## Classifier graph (Spec 2, 2026-04-28) — pure-graph classifier + online learning

Spec 2 ships the actual graph classifier on top of Spec 1's seed. NO LLM call in the classification hot path. NO LLM call in the correction-detection path. NO LLM call in outcome supervision.

**Files:**
- `core/classifier_graph.py` — `classify_intent_graph()` (abstract → embed → top-K cosine k-NN → Wilson lower-bound vote → de-abstract → sidecar). Same return shape as `_classify_intent`. Handles correction loop via `handle_correction()` + `extract_correction_target()`. Module-level `_pending_outcomes` deque + `record_pending_outcome` / `confirm_pending` / `revert_pending` / `age_pending_outcomes` for the 3-turn outcome supervision window.
- `core/abstraction.py` — production-time two-pass abstraction (registry-first, then spacy NER fallback). Times/dates/numbers preserved. Module-level spacy singleton.
- `core/classifier_db.py` v2 schema — `extracted_value` column on scenarios table. Idempotent `ALTER TABLE` migration; existing v1 DBs upgrade in place.
- `core/brain.py::_classify_intent_smart` — three-stage orchestrator routing shadow/primary/retired modes per `GRAPH_CLASSIFIER_MODE` config flag.
- `pipeline.py` — `age_pending_outcomes()` called once per turn (no-op when queue empty); correction-skip-brain branch when sidecar emits `correction_to_previous_response` (calls `handle_correction` and returns without generating brain response — LLM-free path).

**Modes:**
- **shadow (default after ship)** — both classifiers run in parallel; LLM result drives behavior; graph divergences logged via `[Intent] shadow divergence` stdout. Production behavior unchanged.
- **primary** — graph fires first; if `confidence >= GRAPH_PRIMARY_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR` (0.55) return graph; else fall back to LLM safety net. Graph decisions enter the outcome supervision queue.
- **retired** — graph only; LLM never called. If graph abstains (confidence below `GRAPH_ABSTAIN_THRESHOLD=0.40`), classifier returns None and the gate code falls back to default-silent.

**Cutover discipline:** never all-at-once. Ship in shadow, observe divergences for 1-2 weeks of live use, target <5% divergence rate on routine traffic before flipping to primary. Primary mode validates over weeks before flipping to retired. The old `_classify_intent` LLM classifier stays in the repo as the safety net during primary mode.

**Wilson lower-bound aggregation** — a single confirmation does NOT get full credit. `confidence_score` returns the 95% CI lower bound of the confirmation rate, smoothed by initial_confidence when no outcome data exists. Prevents single-correction events from over-skewing the graph.

**Correction loop (LLM-free):**
1. Classifier emits `correction_to_previous_response` (bootstrapped intent, 30 hand-authored examples + 5 negative discriminators in seed).
2. Pipeline detects label, calls `handle_correction(text)` → `latest_pending()` → decrement scenarios that voted for the wrong label on turn N-1.
3. Regex bank (`_DEFAULT_CORRECTION_PATTERNS_TEMPLATE`, ~14 patterns) extracts intended target. Patterns capture pronouns ("you" / "me") get filtered out as decrement-only signals.
4. If real target name extracted: re-abstract turn N-1's user_text with the corrected target, embed, insert as `direct_address_to_person` scenario with `source_tag="live_correction"` + `initial_confidence=0.85`.
5. Brain stays silent — no "sorry, my mistake!" response that would be additional intrusion. Pipeline returns `("continue", None)` early.

**Latency:** the spec's 100ms p95 target is achievable on the local-only path (abstraction + cosine k-NN + aggregation), validated by `test_classifier_graph_latency_under_budget` over a stubbed-embedding fixture. Real production latency depends on the embedding service (Together.ai E5 endpoint, ~300-500ms per call). Future optimization candidates: local E5 model, ANN index in place of brute-force cosine, spacy doc cache.

**Validation gate before primary cutover:** re-run the Qwen-7B + Llama-70B Friends benchmarks with the graph classifier in place. Both numbers should converge — proves the architecture is genuinely model-agnostic. Pre-Spec-2 baseline: Llama-70B+LLM-classifier=58.66%, Qwen-7B+LLM-classifier=52.32% (the divergence that motivated the architecture pivot).

**Tests (1314 = 1294 + 20):** see `tests/test_classifier_graph.py` — output shape, no-LLM-calls in classifier hot path, no-LLM-calls in correction path, abstain on conflicting graph, Wilson formula, abstraction PERSON/LOC/times-preserved, correction decrements + new-scenario writes + null-target handling, 3-turn outcome supervision auto-credit, shadow/primary/retired mode routing, latency p95 under budget.

**Operator hooks:**
- `core.classifier_graph.aclose()` — close singletons on shutdown (httpx client, ClassifierDB connection).
- `core.classifier_graph.reset_pending_outcomes()` — clear the queue (factory reset / test setup).

---

## Golden Intent Set (P1.5, Session 81) — source taxonomy + deprecation rule

Golden corpus lives at `tests/golden_intent.jsonl`. Schema per row:
`user_text`, `expected_intent`, `expected_value`, `source`, `note`
(plus optional `observed_intent` / `observed_value` / `observed_conf` / `source_file` for `real_observed` rows).

**Source taxonomy (fixed upfront — tests enforce):**
- `real_observed` — harvested from archived terminal_output logs via `tests/harvest_golden.py`, hand-labeled by a human.
- `adversarial` — from the locked VISION_ROADMAP 1.9 list (Detroit, Kara, homoglyph, injection, implicit-shutdown, etc.). **Regression-permanent — never deprecated.**
- `synthetic_common` — hand-written templates, each traced to a specific bug class in the Completed Sessions table. Deprecated once `real_observed` per intent reaches the threshold (see rule below).
- `regression_<session>` — bug caught in production, labeled with the session number. **Permanent.**
- `legacy_synthetic` — the post-deprecation state of a `synthetic_common` row. Retained in file as historical record; **excluded from precision/recall computation** so synthetic pairs can't artificially prop up metrics after real data exists.

**Deprecation rule:** when a given intent has ≥25 `real_observed` rows, all `synthetic_common` rows for that intent flip to `legacy_synthetic`. They stay in the JSONL but the eval bench (P1.6) filters them out of metric aggregation. Without this rule, synthetic pairs would silently distort precision numbers long after real data is available; drift detection would become meaningless.

**P1.6 → P1.7 wire-in gate (two conditions, not one):**
1. Hybrid-set (all non-legacy rows): per-intent precision ≥ 0.95, recall ≥ 0.85, ECE ≤ 0.05
2. `source=real_observed` subset alone (≥30 rows total, across intents): same thresholds

A single-gate on the hybrid set would green-light a validator that only looks good because `synthetic_common` was engineered to pass. The real-only gate is the anti-cheat.

**Current corpus (Session 87 end):** 149 rows — 3 `real_observed`, 60 `adversarial`, 77 `synthetic_common`, 6 `regression_session_82_relabel`, 3 `regression_session_86`. All 12 `INTENT_LABELS` represented. Shutdown-family (`request_shutdown` + `question_about_shutdown`) and `deny_identity` each have ≥25 rows (high-blast-radius floor enforced by `test_golden_intent_jsonl_high_blast_radius_min_coverage`).

**`regression_session_82_relabel` tier explained:** these 6 rows came out of the Session 82 bench run where the classifier was CONSISTENT and the GOLDEN labels were wrong (5 out-of-context confirm_identity affirmations + 1 correction phrasing). Distinct provenance from `regression_<session>` (production bug) — both permanent, both excluded from the deprecation rule, but the tier name encodes the reason for permanence. Use `regression_<session>_relabel` suffix for future calibration relabels.

**Archive hook:** `pipeline._archive_terminal_output()` at pipeline startup renames the prior `terminal_output.md` to `terminal_output_YYYY-MM-DD_HHMMSS.md` (using the file's mtime). Collision-safe. This is the P1.5 data-accumulation machinery — every live session now produces a named, preserved archive that the harvest script can parse into new `real_observed` rows.
- Update this file at session end. Verify all values against source code before writing.

## Hardware Targets

| Target | Status |
|---|---|
| Windows 11 laptop | Current dev — DirectShow camera, CUDA |
| Jetson AGX Orin 32GB | Production target — V4L2, faiss-gpu, TensorRT |

Jetson checklist (when ready):
- `pip install faiss-gpu` instead of faiss-cpu
- Confirm buffalo_l auto-downloads (~500MB) or pre-copy
- CUDA 12.x + cuDNN 9.x (match onnxruntime-gpu)
- V4L2 backend: auto via `sys.platform != "win32"` check
- `loop.add_signal_handler(SIGTERM, ...)` works on Linux (not Windows)

---

## Session Protocol

**Starting a session:**
1. This file is auto-loaded — read it first.
2. Check test count at top.
3. Run `pytest` to confirm green state.
4. Discuss what to work on before touching any code.

**Ending a session:**
1. Update test count line at top.
2. Move completed work from Pending to Completed Sessions table.
3. Update bug status (resolved bugs removed, new bugs added).
4. Verify all config values in this file match actual config.py.
